Clinical_Translation_English: Jaundice with choluria and acholic stools in the context of a ten-day febrile illness, indicating acute hepatitis — most likely hepatitis A or E in the Nigerian context — or leptospirosis requiring urgent hepatic function testing.

Doctor_English: Have you had any unprotected sexual contact recently, and do you have multiple sexual partners?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ní ọ̀rẹ́ obìnrin tuntun kan ní oṣù méjì sẹ́yìn, a kò lo àbọ̀. Mo ṣe ìbálòpọ̀ pẹ̀lú ọ̀rẹ́ kejì tún ní ọ̀sẹ̀ mẹ́ta sẹ́yìn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Multiple recent unprotected sexual contacts within the preceding three months, conferring significant risk for sexually transmitted infections including gonorrhoea, chlamydia, syphilis, and HIV, requiring comprehensive STI screening.

Doctor_English: Have you received a dog bite or scratch from any animal in the past few months?
Patient_Yoruba: Ajá adágún kan jẹ ọwọ́ mi ní ọjọ́ mẹ́tàlá sẹ́yìn ní abúlé. A kò ṣe oògùn rábíìsì fún mi níbẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Dog bite from an unvaccinated stray animal thirteen days prior with no post-exposure rabies prophylaxis administered, representing a time-sensitive emergency requiring immediate wound management, rabies immunoglobulin, and vaccine initiation.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any worms in your stool, or has your child been complaining of anal itching at night?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo rí ohun tó ń jọ ẹ̀jẹ̀ funfun gígùn nínú ìgbẹ́ ọmọ mi. Ó sì ń gbá ìdí rẹ̀ ní òru.
Clinical_Translation_English: Visible helminths in the stool of a child with nocturnal perianal pruritus, consistent with Ascaris lumbricoides or Enterobius vermicularis infestation, requiring anthelmintic therapy and household hygiene education.

Doctor_English: Have you been walking barefoot on soil, especially around areas with poor sanitation?
Patient_Yoruba: Bẹ́ẹ̀ ni, mo máa ń rìn bàtà rẹ nínú pápá tí wọ́n ń lo gẹ́gẹ́ bí ìgbẹ́ ìgbọ̀nsẹ̀ ní abúlé wa. Mo ń ṣe bẹ́ẹ̀ fún ọdún.
Clinical_Translation_English: Chronic barefoot exposure to soil contaminated with human faeces over many years, placing the patient at significant risk for hookworm infection, strongyloidiasis, and cutaneous larva migrans.

Doctor_English: Have you had any recent surgery, dental procedure, or invasive medical intervention?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ní iṣẹ́ abẹ ìdọ̀tí fún ẹ̀jẹ̀ oyún ní oṣù kan sẹ́yìn. Wọ́n fi ohun ìná ṣiṣẹ́ ní ilé ìwòsàn àdúgbò.
Clinical_Translation_English: Uterine evacuation procedure performed one month prior at a peripheral facility, with current presentation of fever and pelvic pain raising concern for post-procedural septic endometritis or pelvic inflammatory disease.

Doctor_English: Do you live or work near stagnant water, or have you been swimming in a river or lake recently?
Patient_Yoruba: Ilé wa wà ní àtẹ̀lẹ odo. Mo máa ń wẹ nínú odo náà pẹ̀lú àwọn ọmọ mi ní ọjọ́ Àìkú.
Clinical_Translation_English: Residential proximity to a river with regular freshwater exposure during swimming activities, placing the patient at risk for schistosomiasis, leptospirosis, and Naegleria fowleri infection.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any discharge from your penis or pain when you pass urine, with or without a rash?
Patient_Yoruba: Omi tó ní àwọ̀ pàálu-funfun ń jáde nínú òpòlopò mi ní òwúrọ̀. Ìtọ mi tún ń jó mi ní ìgbà tí mo bá ń ṣe.
Clinical_Translation_English: Purulent urethral discharge with dysuria in a male patient, the classic presentation of gonococcal urethritis caused by Neisseria gonorrhoeae, requiring urethral swab and culture alongside chlamydia co-testing.

Doctor_English: Have you been wearing glasses or contact lenses, and when was your last eye test?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ń wọ àwo ojú fún ọdún mẹ́wàá. Ìgbà tí mo ṣe ìdánwò ojú kẹ́yìí jẹ́ ọdún mẹ́ta sẹ́yìn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Established refractive error with spectacle correction for ten years and a three-year gap since last optometric review, warranting updated refraction to exclude progression or development of additional pathology.

Doctor_English: Is the vision loss affecting your ability to see things in the centre or around the edges?
Patient_Yoruba: Ní àárín ojú mi ni ó ń ṣòro jù. Ohun tí mo bá ń wo tàààrà, ó máa ń ṣòòfà tàbí ṣúnkú, gẹ́gẹ́ bí ọmọ aláṣẹ ìwòsàn ṣe ṣàlàyé.
Clinical_Translation_English: Central scotoma with preservation of peripheral vision, consistent with macular pathology such as age-related macular degeneration, macular hole, or central serous chorioretinopathy requiring urgent retinal imaging.

Doctor_English: Have you been experiencing any eye pain that worsens when you move your eyes in different directions?
Patient_Yoruba: Ojú ọ̀tún mi ń dùn gan-an, pàápàá nígbà tí mo bá ń yí ojú mi ní ẹ̀gbẹ́ kan tàbí èkejì.
Clinical_Translation_English: Retrobulbar pain exacerbated by extraocular movements in a single eye, the hallmark presentation of optic neuritis, which may represent the first demyelinating event of multiple sclerosis in a young patient.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any coloured halos around lights, especially at night?
Patient_Yoruba: Ní alẹ́ nígbà tí mo bá ń wò ìmọ̀lẹ̀ ọkọ̀ ayọ́kẹ́lẹ́, mo máa ń rí ìrísí bí àgò ìmọ̀lẹ̀ àwọ̀ onírúurú ní àyíká rẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Coloured halos around lights at night, a symptom highly suggestive of acute angle-closure glaucoma or corneal oedema secondary to elevated intraocular pressure, requiring urgent tonometry and ophthalmological assessment.

Doctor_English: Has your child been holding books or screens very close to their face to see them clearly?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọmọ mi máa ń mu ìwé sunmọ ojú rẹ̀ gan-an, ó sì ń jókòó nítòsí tẹlifísọ̀n. Olùkọ́ rẹ̀ tún sọ pé ó máa ń jókòó lẹ́yìn kíláàsì.
Clinical_Translation_English: Compensatory near-viewing behaviour with tendency to sit close to screens and at the back of the classroom, characteristic of myopia (short-sightedness) requiring refraction and spectacle prescription.

Doctor_English: Have you ever had an eye injury, surgery, or been told you have high pressure in your eyes?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà ojú sọ pé ìtẹ̀wọ́ inú ojú mi ga ní ọdún méjì sẹ́yìn. A fún mi ní omi ojú ṣùgbọ́n mo ti dáwọ̀ lo.
Clinical_Translation_English: Known elevated intraocular pressure with prescribed ocular hypotensive drops, now self-discontinued, representing significant risk for glaucomatous optic nerve damage and irreversible visual field loss.

Doctor_English: Has your vision been blurry at a specific distance only — far away, up close, or both?
Patient_Yoruba: Ohun tó jìnnà ni mo ń rí ṣòòfà, ṣùgbọ́n nígbà tí nǹkan bá wà nitòsí mi, mo ń rí i dáradára.
Clinical_Translation_English: Difficulty with distance vision with preserved near vision and reading ability, the classic presentation of myopia (short-sightedness) confirming a refractive rather than organic aetiology.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any sudden floaters, flashes of light, or a curtain-like shadow across your vision?
Patient_Yoruba: Ní òwúrọ̀ àná, mo bẹ̀rẹ̀ rí ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ àwọn nǹkan tó ń fò nínú ojú mi àti ìmọ̀lẹ̀ tó ń yọ̀. Apá ìsàlẹ̀ ojú mi di òkùnkùn bí ìbòjú.
Clinical_Translation_English: Sudden onset floaters with photopsia and an inferior visual field defect described as a curtain shadow, constituting the classical triad of rhegmatogenous retinal detachment — an ophthalmic emergency requiring same-day retinal assessment.

Doctor_English: Is your eye always watering, and does it get worse in bright light or windy conditions?
Patient_Yoruba: Ojú mi ọ̀tún máa ń sọ omi lọ́pọ̀lọpọ̀, pàápàá nígbà tí afẹ́fẹ́ bá ń fẹ̀ tàbí ní ìmọ̀lẹ̀ oòrùn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Unilateral epiphora exacerbated by wind and bright light exposure, suggesting nasolacrimal duct obstruction, ectropion, or corneal surface pathology such as a foreign body or superficial keratitis.

Doctor_English: Have you been using any eye drops that were not prescribed to you, including traditional eye treatments?
Patient_Yoruba: Àwọn ọ̀rẹ́ mi fún mi ní omi ojú kan tí wọ́n sọ pé yóò mú ojú mi dára. Mo tún lo àgbo ojú tí ìyá mi ṣe.
Clinical_Translation_English: Self-medication with unprescribed ocular drops and traditional herbal eye preparations, a recognised cause of infectious keratitis, chemical conjunctivitis, and irreversible corneal scarring in West African ophthalmological practice.

Doctor_English: Have you been passing less urine than usual, or has it stopped completely for several hours?
Patient_Yoruba: Ito mi ti dín gan-an fún ọjọ́ méjì. Lẹ́ẹ̀kan náà, ko jáde kankan fún wákàtí mẹ́fà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Oliguria progressing to a six-hour anuria episode, indicating acute kidney injury of pre-renal, intrinsic renal, or post-renal aetiology requiring urgent assessment of fluid status, urine output, and renal function.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any frothiness or foaming in your urine when you pass it?
Patient_Yoruba: Ito mi máa ń ṣẹ̀dá fèrèsé lọ́pọ̀lọpọ̀ nígbà tí ó bá kọlu omi nínú ìgbọ̀nsẹ̀. Ó ti jẹ́ bẹ́ẹ̀ fún oṣù méjì.
Clinical_Translation_English: Persistent foamy urine over two months indicating significant proteinuria, a hallmark of glomerular disease such as nephrotic syndrome, diabetic nephropathy, or IgA nephropathy requiring urine protein quantification.

Doctor_English: Do you have difficulty starting to urinate, a weak stream, or do you feel you cannot empty your bladder fully?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ń rí ìṣòro láti bẹ̀rẹ̀ ito. Ìṣàn rẹ̀ kúrú, àti pé mo ń jẹ pé ikùn ito mi kò ṣòfò dáradára lẹ́yìn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Lower urinary tract symptoms comprising hesitancy, poor stream, and incomplete bladder emptying in a male patient, consistent with benign prostatic hyperplasia or bladder neck obstruction requiring digital rectal examination and uroflowmetry.

Doctor_English: Have you had any kidney stones in the past, and does the pain come in waves?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà sọ pé mo ní òkúta ìdọ̀tí ọdún méjì sẹ́yìn. Irora lọ́wọ́lọ́wọ́ ń wáyé ní ìgbà-ìgbà, tó le jù ìgbà tí mo bá ń sinmi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Known nephrolithiasis with current recurrence of colicky flank pain, a pattern consistent with ureteric colic from a calculus migrating through the ureter, requiring urgent imaging and analgesia.

Doctor_English: Is the pain on your back or side, just below your ribs, and does it radiate to your groin?
Patient_Yoruba: Irora wà ní ẹ̀gbẹ́ ọ̀tún mi, ní ìsàlẹ̀ ìhà àyà mi. Ó ń rọ sínú ìdì mi nígbà tí ó bá le.
Clinical_Translation_English: Right-sided costovertebral angle pain radiating to the ipsilateral groin, the classical pain pathway of ureteric colic secondary to a renal calculus or ureteric obstruction.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any puffiness around your eyes in the morning, or swelling in your face?
Patient_Yoruba: Ojú mi máa ń wú ní àárọ̀ nígbà tí mo bá jí dìde. Ó máa ń rọ ní ọ̀sán. Mo ti rí rẹ̀ fún ọ̀sẹ̀ mẹ́ta.
Clinical_Translation_English: Morning periorbital oedema with diurnal improvement, a presentation consistent with nephrotic syndrome where hypoalbuminaemia causes dependent and facial oedema rather than the purely dependent oedema of cardiac failure.

Doctor_English: Have you ever been told your kidneys are not working well, or have you had any kidney investigations?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà kan sọ pé ìdánwò ẹjẹ̀ mi fihàn pé àwọn ẹ̀dọ̀ ito mi ti ń ṣiṣẹ́ díẹ̀ díẹ̀. Ó ní kí n padà wá ṣùgbọ́n mi ò lọ.
Clinical_Translation_English: Documented renal impairment identified on prior blood testing with failure to attend follow-up, indicating possible chronic kidney disease with loss to monitoring and risk of progression to end-stage renal failure.

Doctor_English: Do you feel an urgent need to urinate that is difficult to control, and do you sometimes not make it in time?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo máa ń fẹ ito lójijì tó ń jẹ́ kí n máà lè dúró. Mo ti ṣe ito nínú aṣọ mi lẹ́ẹ̀mejì ní ọ̀sẹ̀ tí ó kọjá.
Clinical_Translation_English: Urinary urgency with urge incontinence on two occasions in the preceding week, consistent with overactive bladder syndrome or detrusor overactivity, requiring urodynamic assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you been drinking enough water, and how many glasses do you typically drink each day?
Patient_Yoruba: Mi ò mu omi to. Ọjọ́ kan, bóyá ìkòkò kan péré ni mo mu. Mo máa ń gbàgbé nítorí iṣẹ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Chronic inadequate fluid intake of approximately one litre per day, a significant contributor to urinary tract infections, nephrolithiasis, and renal concentration defects, requiring patient education on hydration targets.

Doctor_English: Has there been any blood visible in your urine, and did it happen at the beginning, throughout, or at the end of urination?
Patient_Yoruba: Ito mi di pupa ní ìbẹ̀rẹ̀ nìkan, lẹ́yìn náà ó padà sí àwọ̀ àárọ̀. Ó ṣẹlẹ̀ lẹ́ẹ̀mẹ́ta ní ọjọ́ méjì.
Clinical_Translation_English: Initial haematuria only at the commencement of urination on three occasions, suggesting a urethral or anterior urethral source rather than bladder or upper tract bleeding, requiring urethroscopy and urinalysis.

Doctor_English: Have you been feeling very sad or hopeless for most of the day, nearly every day?
Patient_Yoruba: Inú mi ti dùn púpọ̀, mo ń bínú, mi ò sì ríran siwájú mọ́. Ó ti jẹ́ bẹ́ẹ̀ fún oṣù méjì.
Clinical_Translation_English: Persistent low mood with anhedonia and hopelessness lasting two months, meeting the duration and symptom criteria for a major depressive episode requiring psychiatric evaluation and risk assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you had any thoughts of harming yourself or ending your life?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìrọ̀lẹ́ kan, mo rò pé ìgbé ayé mi kò ní iye. Ṣùgbọ́n mi ò ṣe nǹkan kankan, àti pé mo ṣàánú àwọn ọmọ mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Passive suicidal ideation without active intent or plan, with protective factors including parental responsibility. Risk requires formal assessment, safety planning, and urgent psychiatric referral regardless of expressed hesitancy.

Doctor_English: Have you been hearing voices or seeing things that others around you cannot hear or see?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo máa ń gbọ́ ohùn tó ń sọ ọ̀rọ̀ bùrúkú sí mi, pàápàá ní alẹ́. Àwọn ẹbí mi sọ pé wọn kò gbọ́ ohùn náà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Auditory hallucinations with persecutory content occurring nocturnally, unwitnessed by family members, consistent with a psychotic disorder such as schizophrenia or a psychotic episode requiring urgent psychiatric assessment and antipsychotic therapy.

Doctor_English: Have you been feeling unusually energetic, needing very little sleep, and spending money excessively?
Patient_Yoruba: Fún ọ̀sẹ̀ méjì, mo ń ṣiṣẹ́ lọ́nà tí ó ṣeni lẹ́nu, mo kò nílò ìsùn. Mo ti na owó gbogbo àpamọ̀ mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: A two-week episode of elevated energy, markedly reduced sleep requirement, and impulsive financial decisions, constituting a manic episode as part of a bipolar affective disorder requiring mood stabiliser therapy.

Doctor_English: Have you been feeling excessively worried about multiple things and finding it difficult to control the worry?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo máa ń yọ̀ nípa ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ nǹkan ní ìgbà-ìgbà — iṣẹ́, àwọn ọmọ, owó. Mi ò lè da ara mi dúró láti rò wọ́n.
Clinical_Translation_English: Generalised, uncontrollable worry across multiple life domains including occupational, parental, and financial concerns, consistent with generalised anxiety disorder requiring cognitive-behavioural therapy and pharmacological consideration.

Doctor_English: Have you been using any alcohol or substances to help you cope with how you are feeling?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ti ń mu ọtí sí i lẹ́yìn ìdìpọ̀ àwọn ìṣòro mi. Mo máa ń mu bọ́ọ̀tù méjì tàbí mẹ́ta ní ọjọ́ kan.
Clinical_Translation_English: Escalating alcohol consumption of two to three bottles daily as a maladaptive coping mechanism for psychological distress, raising concern for alcohol use disorder complicating an underlying mood or anxiety disorder.

Doctor_English: Have you been experiencing repeated nightmares, flashbacks, or feeling like a past traumatic event is happening again?
Patient_Yoruba: Ní gbogbo òru, mo máa ń lá àlá nípa ìjàǹbá ọkọ̀ tí mo jà nínú. Mo tún máa ń rí rẹ̀ nígbà tí mo bá jí.
Clinical_Translation_English: Recurrent traumatic nightmares and intrusive daytime flashbacks following a road traffic accident, constituting hallmark re-experiencing symptoms of post-traumatic stress disorder requiring trauma-focused cognitive-behavioural therapy.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any changes in your appetite — are you eating much more or much less than usual?
Patient_Yoruba: Mi ò fẹ jẹun rárá fún oṣù kan. Mo ti tẹ̀nìí, àwọn ọ̀rẹ́ mi sì ti bẹ̀rẹ̀ sí ṣàkíyèsí.
Clinical_Translation_English: Significant anorexia with visible weight loss over one month noted by close contacts, a somatic manifestation of major depression or an independent eating disorder requiring nutritional assessment and psychiatric evaluation.

Doctor_English: Are you finding it very difficult to concentrate at work or school, and has your performance changed?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ti dáwọ̀ rí àwọn ọ̀rọ̀ tí mo kà nínú ìwé dúró. Olórí iṣẹ́ mi ti sọ pé àbájáde iṣẹ́ mi ti bájẹ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Cognitive impairment characterised by inability to retain written information and documented occupational performance decline, a neuropsychiatric symptom seen in major depression, anxiety disorders, and early dementia.

Doctor_English: Have you been avoiding going out or seeing people because of fear or anxiety?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ti dáwọ̀ jáde ilé dúró fún oṣù mẹ́ta. Mo bẹ̀rù pé nǹkan búburú yóò ṣẹlẹ̀ tàbí pé mo máa ṣe ara mi ní ẹlẹ́yà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Prolonged self-imposed social isolation and agoraphobia over three months driven by fear of harm or social embarrassment, consistent with agoraphobia, social anxiety disorder, or a depressive episode with significant behavioural withdrawal.

Doctor_English: Have you been feeling very tired, breathless on mild exertion, and looking paler than usual?
Patient_Yoruba: Àárẹ̀ mi le, mi ò lè gùn ìtẹ̀lọ́ọ̀dọ̀ láìsinmi. Àwọn ọ̀rẹ́ mi sọ pé ojú mi ti di funfun.
Clinical_Translation_English: Anaemia triad of fatigue, exertional dyspnoea, and pallor noted by contacts, requiring full blood count with film, reticulocyte count, and iron studies to differentiate iron deficiency, haemolytic, or chronic disease anaemia.

Doctor_English: Have you been having heavier or longer periods than usual, lasting more than seven days?
Patient_Yoruba: Oṣù mi ti ń wáyé fún ọjọ́ mẹ́wàá àti pọ̀ jù ìgbà àárọ̀ lọ. Mo ti ń lo àṣọ ìbò mẹ́fà ní ọjọ́ kan.
Clinical_Translation_English: Menorrhagia with cycle duration of ten days and use of six sanitary pads per day, indicating excessive menstrual blood loss and a significant risk factor for iron deficiency anaemia requiring haematological and gynaecological evaluation.

Doctor_English: Do you have a family history of sickle cell disease, and have you or your child been tested for it?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìyá mi àti bàbá mi jẹ́ ẹni tó ń gbé àrùn ẹ̀jẹ̀ ààyọ. Ọmọ mi kò tíì ṣe ìdánwò. Mo bẹ̀rù pé ó lè ní àrùn náà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Both parents are confirmed sickle cell trait carriers, conferring a twenty-five percent probability of sickle cell disease in each offspring. Urgent newborn or early childhood haemoglobin electrophoresis is indicated.

Doctor_English: Has your child had any episodes of severe pain in the limbs, chest, or abdomen that required hospitalisation?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọmọ mi ti wọ ilé ìwòsàn lẹ́ẹ̀mẹ́ta nítorí irora apá àti ẹsẹ̀ tó le gidigidi. Dọ́kítà sọ pé ó jẹ́ nítorí ẹ̀jẹ̀ rẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Recurrent vaso-occlusive pain crises in the limbs requiring three hospitalisations, occurring in the context of known haematological disease, consistent with sickle cell disease and requiring haematology input and hydroxyurea consideration.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any yellowing of your eyes that comes and goes, along with dark urine?
Patient_Yoruba: Ojú mi máa ń di pàálu lẹ́ẹ̀kọ̀ọ̀kan, ó sì máa ń lọ lẹ́yìn ọjọ́ díẹ̀. Ito mi tún máa ń di dúdú nígbà yẹn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Episodic jaundice with intermittent choluria, consistent with a haemolytic process causing cyclical hyperbilirubinaemia, as seen in glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase deficiency, hereditary spherocytosis, or sickle cell disease.

Doctor_English: Have you been bruising very easily, or have cuts been taking unusually long to stop bleeding?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọgbẹ́ kéékèèké máa ń dé mi lọ́pọ̀lọpọ̀ kí ẹjẹ̀ tó dúró. Mo tún rí àwọn ọgbẹ́ aró tó han lára mi láìsí ohun tó pa wọ́n.
Clinical_Translation_English: Easy bruising with spontaneous ecchymoses and prolonged bleeding from minor wounds, suggesting a coagulopathy or thrombocytopaenia requiring coagulation studies, platelet count, and haematological assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you ever been given a blood transfusion, and do you know your blood group?
Patient_Yoruba: A ṣe mi àjẹjẹ̀ lẹ́ẹ̀méjì nígbà tí mo bí ọmọ. Mo mọ̀ pé ẹjẹ̀ mi jẹ́ O positive ṣùgbọ́n mi ò mọ̀ orísun rẹ̀ mọ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Two previous blood transfusions in the obstetric context with documented O-positive blood group. Transfusion history is relevant to alloantibody screening and ensures appropriate cross-matching in future clinical encounters.

Doctor_English: Have you been eating or craving unusual non-food items such as ice, clay, or chalk?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ti ń fẹ jẹ ilẹ̀ amọ̀ fún oṣù méjì. Mo jẹ ẹ ní ìkọ̀kọ̀ nítorí màá jẹ́ ẹlẹ́yà. Mo mọ̀ pé kò dára.
Clinical_Translation_English: Pica with cravings for and ingestion of soil (geophagia) over two months, a classic behavioural manifestation of iron deficiency anaemia particularly common in pregnancy and in populations with nutritional deficiencies.

Doctor_English: Have you been experiencing repeated infections, including mouth ulcers, skin infections, and chest infections?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ti ní ọgbẹ́ ẹnu lẹ́ẹ̀mẹ́fà, àkóràn awọ ara méjì, àti ikọ́ tó pẹ́ fún oṣù mẹ́fà. Mi ò mọ̀ idi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Recurrent infections across multiple organ systems over six months including oral mucositis, cutaneous infection, and prolonged respiratory illness, raising concern for an underlying immunodeficiency state or neutropaenia requiring immunological evaluation.

Doctor_English: Has anyone in your family died young from a bleeding disorder or blood-related illness?
Patient_Yoruba: Abúrò mi kú nígbà tí ó jẹ́ ọmọ ọdún mẹ́jọ nítorí ẹ̀jẹ̀ tí kò ń dúró. Dọ́kítà sọ pé ó jẹ́ àrùn ẹ̀jẹ̀ ìbílẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Sibling death in childhood secondary to an inherited bleeding disorder, raising the possibility of haemophilia A or B, von Willebrand disease, or another hereditary coagulopathy requiring haematological family screening.

Doctor_English: Have you had any falls recently, and if so, did you lose consciousness or feel dizzy before falling?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ṣubú lẹ́ẹ̀mẹ́ta ní oṣù kan. Lẹ́ẹ̀kan, ori mi yípo ṣáájú kí n tó ṣubú. Ìgbà méjì kò ní ìkìlọ̀ kankan.
Clinical_Translation_English: Recurrent falls with a mixed pattern of prodromal vertigo in one episode and unheralded collapse in two, requiring differentiation between vestibular, cardiovascular, and neurological aetiologies in a comprehensive falls assessment.

Doctor_English: Are you able to manage your daily medications yourself, or do you need help remembering to take them?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ń gbàgbé láti mu oògùn mi lọ́pọ̀lọpọ̀. Ọmọ mi ti ń ṣètò wọn fún mi, ṣùgbọ́n ó kò wà ní ilé ní gbogbo ìgbà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Medication non-adherence secondary to cognitive impairment with dependence on a family carer who is not consistently available, indicating need for a structured medication management plan and formal cognitive assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you been feeling isolated or lonely, and do you have regular contact with family or friends?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ń gbé nìkan fún ọdún méjì lẹ́yìn ikú ọkọ mi. Àwọn ọmọ mi wà ní ìlú mìíràn. Mo máa ń wò tẹlifísọ̀n nìkan.
Clinical_Translation_English: Social isolation following spousal bereavement with geographically distant family members, a recognised risk factor for depression, cognitive decline, and functional deterioration in elderly patients requiring social prescribing and support.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any change in your ability to walk, dress yourself, or prepare your own meals?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ti dáwọ̀ sè oúnjẹ dúró nítorí o ń ṣe mí nira láti dúró. Wíwọ aṣọ mi tún gba àkókò gígùn báyìí.
Clinical_Translation_English: Functional decline with loss of independence in instrumental and basic activities of daily living including cooking and dressing, indicating significant physical or cognitive impairment requiring occupational therapy assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you been experiencing any confusion that is worse at night, or difficulty recognising familiar places?
Patient_Yoruba: Ní alẹ́, ìyá àgbà mi máa ń jí, ó sì ń sọ pé kò mọ̀ ibití ó wà. Ní ọ̀sán, ó mọ ilé àti àwọn ọmọ rẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Nocturnal disorientation to place with intact daytime orientation, representing sundowning in the context of underlying dementia, likely exacerbated by sensory deprivation and disrupted circadian rhythms in the elderly patient.

Doctor_English: Have you lost interest in hobbies or activities you used to enjoy, such as visiting friends or going to church?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ti dáwọ̀ lọ ṣọ́ọ̀bù dúró fún oṣù mẹ́fà, mi ò sì ń lọ ṣọ́ọ̀bì mọ́. Ó jẹ́ ohun tí mo fẹ́ rí ṣáájú.
Clinical_Translation_English: Anhedonia with progressive withdrawal from previously enjoyed social and religious activities over six months, a significant symptom of late-life depression requiring antidepressant therapy and psychological support in the elderly.

Doctor_English: How many different medications are you currently taking, and do you know what each one is for?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ń mu oògùn mẹ́jọ ní ọjọ́ kan. Mi ò mọ̀ ohun tí àwọn kan ń ṣe. Dọ́kítà oríṣiríṣi ni ó fún mi ní wọn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Polypharmacy with eight concurrent medications prescribed by multiple physicians without a co-ordinating prescriber, creating significant risk for adverse drug interactions, duplication of therapy, and medication-related harm in an elderly patient.

Doctor_English: Has your vision or hearing changed significantly, and has this affected your confidence in going out?
Patient_Yoruba: Gbígbọ́ mi àti ojú mi ti bàjẹ́ díẹ̀díẹ̀. Mo ti dáwọ̀ jade nìkan dúró nítorí mo bẹ̀rù ìṣubú.
Clinical_Translation_English: Dual sensory impairment with progressive visual and hearing loss leading to fear of falling and agoraphobia, a common but under-recognised geriatric syndrome requiring sensory correction and falls prevention intervention.

Doctor_English: Have you been eating enough food each day, and has your weight been stable over the past few months?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìfẹ́kúfẹ̀ẹ́ mi ti kú síwájú sí i. Àwọn aṣọ mi ti di lálá. Mo ti tẹ̀nìí ṣùgbọ́n mi ò mọ̀ iye.
Clinical_Translation_English: Progressive anorexia with unquantified weight loss evidenced by loose-fitting clothing in an elderly patient, consistent with malnutrition secondary to social isolation, depression, malignancy, or dysphagia requiring nutritional screening.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any difficulty controlling your bowels, leading to accidents or urgency?
Patient_Yoruba: Bẹ́ẹ̀ ni, ìgbà mẹ́ta ní oṣù tí ó kọjá, mo ṣe ìgbẹ́ nínú aṣọ mi ṣáájú kí n tó dé ìgbọ̀nsẹ̀. Ó jẹ ẹlẹ́yà mi gan-an.
Clinical_Translation_English: Faecal urgency with three episodes of incontinence in the preceding month causing significant embarrassment, a symptom commonly under-reported in elderly patients but associated with anal sphincter weakness, rectal prolapse, or cognitive impairment.

Doctor_English: Did the patient collapse suddenly, and was there any pulse felt at the time?
Patient_Yoruba: Ó ṣubú lójijì ní gbọ̀ngàn ilé. Àwọn ẹbí rẹ̀ gbìyànjú láti rí ohùn ọkàn ṣùgbọ́n kò rí i. Wọ́n pè mí lẹ́sẹ̀kẹsẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Sudden witnessed collapse with absent pulse reported by bystanders, consistent with cardiac arrest requiring immediate cardiopulmonary resuscitation and advanced life support activation.

Doctor_English: How long has the patient been unconscious, and was there any seizure activity before they collapsed?
Patient_Yoruba: Ó ti sùn àìdáwọ dúró fún ìṣẹ́jú ogún. Ṣáájú ìṣubú rẹ̀, ara rẹ̀ gbọn fún ìṣẹ́jú márùn-ún.
Clinical_Translation_English: Prolonged post-ictal unconsciousness of twenty minutes following a five-minute convulsive episode, requiring airway protection, blood glucose measurement, and neurological assessment to exclude status epilepticus or structural brain pathology.

Doctor_English: Was the patient in a road traffic accident, and did they lose consciousness at any point?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọkọ̀ ayọ́kẹ́lẹ́ kan gbá a ní ikorita. Ó sọ pé kò mọ̀ ohun tó ṣẹlẹ̀ fún ìṣẹ́jú díẹ̀ lẹ́yìn ìjàǹbá náà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Road traffic collision with a witnessed period of unconsciousness indicating traumatic brain injury, requiring cervical spine immobilisation, Glasgow Coma Scale scoring, and urgent cranial CT imaging.

Doctor_English: Is the patient breathing on their own, and how fast is the breathing rate?
Patient_Yoruba: Ó ń mí, ṣùgbọ́n mímí rẹ̀ yára gan-an. Mo kà ìgbà mẹ́ẹ̀ẹ́dọ́gbọ̀n tó mí ní ìṣẹ́jú kan.
Clinical_Translation_English: Tachypnoea at thirty-five breaths per minute with preserved spontaneous breathing, indicating significant respiratory compromise requiring immediate oxygen supplementation and investigation for pneumonia, pulmonary embolism, or metabolic acidosis.

Doctor_English: Did the patient ingest any medications, household chemicals, or substances before becoming unwell?
Patient_Yoruba: A rí ọpọ̀n oògùn orun tó ṣòfò nítòsí rẹ̀. Ó sọ pé ó mu wọn gbogbo ní òru àná nítorí ìbànújẹ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Intentional ingestion of an entire bottle of sleeping tablets in the context of acute psychological distress, constituting a deliberate self-poisoning emergency requiring gastric lavage consideration, activated charcoal, and urgent psychiatric assessment.

Doctor_English: How long has the patient had this severe headache, and did it come on like a thunderclap?
Patient_Yoruba: Orí mi dùn lójijì bí ẹni pé ohun kan ti gbamu nínú rẹ̀. Ó jẹ́ orí fífọ̀ tó le jù tí mo ti ní rí. Ó wáyé ní ìṣẹ́jú mẹ́wàá sẹ́yìn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Thunderclap headache of maximum intensity at onset occurring ten minutes prior, the hallmark presentation of subarachnoid haemorrhage secondary to ruptured intracranial aneurysm, requiring immediate non-contrast CT head and lumbar puncture if imaging is negative.

Doctor_English: Is the patient able to speak, and can they tell you their name and where they are?
Patient_Yoruba: Ó ń sọ ọ̀rọ̀ ṣùgbọ́n kò ń jáde dáradára. Ó mọ̀ orúkọ rẹ̀ ṣùgbọ́n kò mọ̀ ibití ó wà tàbí ọjọ́ tí ó jẹ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Dysarthric speech with preserved nominal recall but disorientation to place and time, indicating a Glasgow Coma Scale score of approximately thirteen and a focal neurological deficit requiring immediate stroke pathway activation.

Doctor_English: Has the patient been stung or bitten by an insect, and are they having any difficulty breathing or swallowing?
Patient_Yoruba: Ẹranko tó dà bíi ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ ẹsẹ̀ jẹ ọwọ́ rẹ̀ ní ọgbà. Lẹ́yìn ìṣẹ́jú mẹ́wàá, ọfun rẹ̀ bẹ̀rẹ̀ sí wú àti mí rẹ̀ di ìṣòro.
Clinical_Translation_English: Anaphylaxis following hymenoptera envenomation with rapid onset pharyngeal oedema and respiratory compromise within ten minutes, requiring immediate intramuscular adrenaline, intravenous antihistamine, and corticosteroid therapy.

Doctor_English: Is the patient's skin cold and clammy, and how low is the blood pressure reading?
Patient_Yoruba: Awọ ara rẹ̀ tutù àti ọ̀rọ̀. A wọn ẹjẹ̀ rẹ̀, ó sì wà ní ọgọ́sàn-ún lé mẹ́ẹ̀ẹ́dógójì lórí ọgọ́sàn-ún. Ọkàn rẹ̀ lu yára.
Clinical_Translation_English: Haemodynamic instability with hypotension, tachycardia, and peripheral shutdown indicating cardiogenic or distributive shock, requiring urgent intravenous access, fluid resuscitation, and identification of the underlying cause.

Doctor_English: Did the patient have any warning before the seizure, such as an unusual smell or feeling?
Patient_Yoruba: Ó sọ pé ó gbọ́ òórùn iná ṣáájú kó tó ṣubú. Lẹ́yìn náà kò mọ̀ ohun tó ṣẹlẹ̀ tó fi dé ìgbà tí ó jí.
Clinical_Translation_English: Olfactory aura preceding loss of consciousness, consistent with a focal onset seizure arising from the mesial temporal lobe, requiring electroencephalography and MRI brain to exclude hippocampal sclerosis or focal cortical dysplasia.

Doctor_English: Has the patient taken any recreational drugs or alcohol today, and do you know what substances?
Patient_Yoruba: Àwọn ọ̀rẹ́ rẹ̀ sọ pé wọ́n mu ọtí àti ohun mìíràn ní àsè tó wà ní òru. Wọn kò mọ̀ ohun tó wà nínú nǹkan tí wọ́n ń ta ní ibi àsè.
Clinical_Translation_English: Poly-substance ingestion including alcohol and an unknown recreational substance at a social gathering, requiring a comprehensive toxicology screen, cardiac monitoring, and symptomatic supportive management.

Doctor_English: How long has the patient had this high fever, and are there any signs of a rash on the body?
Patient_Yoruba: Ibà rẹ̀ ti wà fún ọjọ́ méjì. A wọn rẹ̀, ó sì wà ní ìwọ̀n ogójì àti ọkan. Mo tún rí àwọn ìwọ̀ pupa kéékèéké tó ń bò ìsàlẹ̀ ara rẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Hyperpyrexia at forty-one degrees Celsius for two days with a petechial rash on the lower body, constituting a meningococcaemia emergency requiring immediate blood cultures, lumbar puncture, and empirical intravenous benzylpenicillin.

Doctor_English: Is there any active bleeding from a wound, and has pressure been applied to control it?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọgbẹ́ tó jinlẹ̀ wà ní ìhà àyà rẹ̀ tí o ń jẹ̀ ẹjẹ̀ púpọ̀. A ti tẹ ọwọ́ mọ́ ọn ṣùgbọ́n ẹjẹ̀ ṣì ń jáde.
Clinical_Translation_English: Deep penetrating chest wound with persistent haemorrhage despite manual pressure application, indicating possible haemothorax or injury to an intercostal vessel requiring emergency surgical review and blood product preparation.

Doctor_English: Did the patient's face droop suddenly on one side, and can they raise both arms equally?
Patient_Yoruba: Ẹ̀gbẹ́ òsì ojú rẹ̀ rọ lójijì ní àárọ̀. Ó kò lè gbé apá òsì rẹ̀ gaju, ó sì ń sọ ọ̀rọ̀ díẹ̀díẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Acute facial droop, arm weakness, and speech disturbance — the FAST triad of acute ischaemic stroke — with onset this morning, requiring immediate stroke unit activation, CT angiography, and thrombolysis eligibility assessment.

Doctor_English: Is the child breathing at all, and what colour are their lips right now?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọmọ náà ń mí, ṣùgbọ́n ète rẹ̀ ti di bíi àwọ̀ búlúù. Mímí rẹ̀ ń gbóná àti ó ń ṣe ìró àjèjì.
Clinical_Translation_English: Cyanotic infant with laboured respirations and stridor indicating critical upper airway obstruction or severe respiratory failure, requiring immediate airway positioning, oxygen delivery, and paediatric emergency team activation.

Doctor_English: What does a typical day of eating look like for you — what do you eat for breakfast, lunch, and dinner?
Patient_Yoruba: Àárọ̀: irẹsì pẹ̀lú ẹja. Òóṣì: bẹ̀lẹ̀bẹ̀ tàbí garí pẹ̀lú ẹgúṣí. Alẹ́: irẹsì tàbí iyán pẹ̀lú ewédú. Mo ṣọwọ́ jẹ ẹran.
Clinical_Translation_English: Diet predominantly composed of starchy staples with fish and vegetable soups, with infrequent red meat consumption. Dietary pattern is consistent with low saturated fat intake but may indicate insufficient protein, iron, and vitamin B12 for the patient's clinical needs.

Doctor_English: Have you been able to afford three meals a day, or have there been days when you went without food?
Patient_Yoruba: Ní ọjọ́ kan tàbí méjì ní ọ̀sẹ̀, mi ò jẹ oúnjẹ tó pọ̀. Owó di kúrú lẹ́yìn ikú ọkọ mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Food insecurity with skipped meals on one to two days per week following the death of the household breadwinner, indicating a socioeconomic determinant of malnutrition requiring social work referral alongside nutritional supplementation.

Doctor_English: Does your child eat a variety of foods, including vegetables, fruits, and protein sources?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọmọ mi kọ ẹfọ pátápátá. Ó máa ń jẹ irẹsì, garí, àti àkàrà nìkan. Ó ṣọwọ́ jẹ ẹyin tàbí ẹja.
Clinical_Translation_English: Selective eating pattern in a child with complete vegetable refusal and monotonous starchy diet with minimal protein and micronutrient variety, creating significant risk for iron, zinc, vitamin A, and vitamin D deficiency.

Doctor_English: Have you been adding extra salt to your food at the table, after it has already been cooked?
Patient_Yoruba: Bẹ́ẹ̀ ni, mo máa ń fi iyọ̀ síwájú sí i nínú oúnjẹ mi lórí tábìlì. Mo fẹ́ iyọ̀ gan-an, ó ṣeni lẹ́nu bí mi ò bá ṣe bẹ́ẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Habitual addition of discretionary salt at the table indicating excessive sodium intake, a modifiable cardiovascular risk factor particularly concerning in the context of hypertension or chronic kidney disease.

Doctor_English: Are you currently taking any vitamin supplements, herbal preparations, or iron tablets?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ń mu ìrọ̀bí irin tí dọ́kítà ìbímọ fún mi, ṣùgbọ́n mo ti dáwọ̀ mu dúró nítorí ó ń mú inú mi ṣòro. Mo tún mu àgbo tí ìyá mi ṣe.
Clinical_Translation_English: Self-discontinued prescribed iron supplementation due to gastrointestinal side effects with concurrent traditional herbal preparation use, creating risk for iron deficiency anaemia in pregnancy and potential drug-herb interactions.

Doctor_English: How much water or fluid do you drink each day, and what types of drinks do you usually have?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo máa ń mu omi díẹ̀, ṣùgbọ́n mo máa ń mu omi soda àti omi àgbàdo lọ́pọ̀lọpọ̀ nínú ọjọ́ kan.
Clinical_Translation_English: Inadequate plain water intake with high consumption of carbonated beverages and sweetened drinks, contributing to excessive sugar intake, dental caries risk, and potential glycaemic dysregulation particularly in at-risk individuals.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any swelling of your legs or abdomen that might be related to poor nutrition?
Patient_Yoruba: Ẹsẹ̀ mi méjèèjì ti wú, àti ikùn mi tún ti bẹ̀rẹ̀ sí wú. Mo máa ń jẹ oúnjẹ díẹ̀ nítorí inú mi ò máa ń fẹ jẹun.
Clinical_Translation_English: Bilateral pedal oedema with early abdominal distension in the context of poor appetite and inadequate dietary intake, raising concern for kwashiorkor or hypoalbuminaemia secondary to protein malnutrition.

Doctor_English: Does the child appear to be growing normally, and have you been told their weight is low for their age?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà ní ilé-ìwòsàn àdúgbò sọ pé ìwọ̀ ara ọmọ mi kéré fún ọjọ́-orí rẹ̀. A ko orúkọ rẹ̀ sínú ìwé àìsàn tó ń gbajúmọ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Documented underweight for age with enrolment on a community nutrition programme, indicating established childhood malnutrition requiring anthropometric monitoring, therapeutic feeding, and investigation of underlying contributory illness.

Doctor_English: Have you been eating a lot of fatty or fried foods, and how often do you eat out or buy street food?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo jẹ oúnjẹ tí a din nínú epo lọ́pọ̀lọpọ̀. Mo máa ń ra oúnjẹ ìta bí ìgbà mẹ́ta ní ọ̀sẹ̀ nítorí iṣẹ́ mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: High intake of deep-fried foods and frequent consumption of street food estimated at three times weekly, indicating elevated saturated fat and trans-fat exposure contributing to dyslipidaemia and cardiovascular risk.

Doctor_English: Has your appetite increased significantly, and are you eating much more than you used to without feeling full?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìfẹ́kúfẹ̀ẹ̀ mi ti pọ̀ si i. Mo máa ń jẹ ìlọ́po méjì ohun tí mo ń jẹ ṣáájú ṣùgbọ́n mi ò máa ń yó.
Clinical_Translation_English: Polyphagia with persistent hunger despite increased caloric intake, a symptom of uncontrolled diabetes mellitus where cellular glucose starvation drives compensatory hyperphagia despite hyperglycaemia.

Doctor_English: How far do you live from the nearest health facility, and how do you usually get there?
Patient_Yoruba: A ń gbé ní abúlé tó jìnnà sí ìlú. Ìlera tó tímọ́tímọ́ wà ní ìrìn wákàtí méjì. A máa ń lo ọkọ̀ alùpù nìkan.
Clinical_Translation_English: Rural patient residing two hours from the nearest health facility accessible only by motorcycle, indicating significant geographical barriers to healthcare access and delayed presentation risk for acute conditions.

Doctor_English: Have you been using a pit latrine, open defecation, or a flush toilet for sanitation?
Patient_Yoruba: A ń lo ìdọ̀tí ìgbẹ́ tó wà ní ita ilé wa. Kò sí omi ìwẹ̀ tàbí ìdọ̀tí omi nínú ilé wa.
Clinical_Translation_English: Open pit latrine use without running water in the household, indicating poor sanitation infrastructure and high environmental contamination risk for enteric infections, cholera, and soil-transmitted helminths.

Doctor_English: Where do you get your drinking water from, and do you treat it before drinking?
Patient_Yoruba: Omi kanga ni a máa ń mu nínú abúlé wa. A kò máa ń ṣe àlẹ̀ rẹ̀, àwọn àgbàlagbà sọ pé omi kanga mọ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Untreated well water consumption with community belief in its safety without evidence, representing a significant risk for waterborne diseases including cholera, typhoid fever, and cryptosporidiosis in the rural setting.

Doctor_English: Have you received any of the following vaccinations as an adult — tetanus, hepatitis B, or yellow fever?
Patient_Yoruba: A fun mi ní àjesara nígbà tí mo ń lọ ilé ẹ̀kọ́ ìpilẹ̀ṣẹ̀, ṣùgbọ́n ìyẹn ni tẹ́lẹ̀ rí. Mi ò mọ̀ orúkọ àwọn tí a fun mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Childhood vaccination history only with no adult booster documentation, indicating likely susceptibility to tetanus in a patient with agricultural exposure and potential hepatitis B susceptibility in an endemic region.

Doctor_English: Does your household use mosquito nets, and do you sleep under one every night?
Patient_Yoruba: A ní àwọ̀ oṣupa kan ṣoṣo nínú ilé wa fún ọmọ ikoko. Àwọn àgbàlagbà kò lo àwọ̀ oṣupa.
Clinical_Translation_English: Single household insecticide-treated bed net reserved for the infant with no adult usage, leaving other household members unprotected against malaria transmission in a high-endemicity area.

Doctor_English: Has there been any flooding in your area recently, and have you had contact with floodwater?
Patient_Yoruba: Omi ìkún-omi bò abúlé wa ní ọ̀sẹ̀ méjì sẹ́yìn. Mo rìn nínú omi náà fún ọjọ́ mẹ́ta nítorí iṣẹ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Prolonged wading through floodwater for three days in a post-flood community setting, creating significant exposure risk for leptospirosis, wound infections, and waterborne gastrointestinal illness.

Doctor_English: Do you burn wood or charcoal indoors for cooking, and is the kitchen well ventilated?
Patient_Yoruba: A máa ń jó igi nínú ilé lati jẹun. Ilé wa kéré, fèrèsé kò sí, èéfín ń wọ gbogbo ilé, gẹ́gẹ́ bí ọmọ aláṣẹ ìwòsàn ṣe ṣàlàyé.
Clinical_Translation_English: Indoor wood combustion for cooking in an unventilated dwelling without windows, resulting in chronic household air pollution exposure — a major risk factor for chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, asthma, and cardiovascular disease in the West African context.

Doctor_English: Have you or anyone in your household been diagnosed with any hereditary or genetic condition?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ nínú ìdílé wa ní àrùn suga. Bàbá mi, abúrò mi méjì, àti ìyá mi gbogbo wọn ni àrùn suga.
Clinical_Translation_English: Strong multigenerational family history of diabetes mellitus affecting the patient's parents and two siblings, indicating a high genetic predisposition and requiring fasting glucose, HbA1c testing, and lifestyle intervention.

Doctor_English: Are there any industries or factories near your home that might be releasing chemicals or smoke?
Patient_Yoruba: Ilé iṣẹ́ tó ń ṣe àwo wà ní ìdẹ́ abúlé wa. Èéfín àti ìrísí àjèjì máa ń wá sí inú ilé wa nígbà tí afẹ́fẹ́ bá ń fẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Proximity to a paint manufacturing facility with reported indoor infiltration of industrial fumes, constituting a significant environmental health hazard with potential exposure to volatile organic compounds, heavy metals, and respiratory irritants.

Doctor_English: How many people sleep in your bedroom, and has anyone else in the house been recently unwell?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìyàrá ìsùn wa ni àwọn ènìyàn mẹ́jọ ń sùn nínú. Abúrò mi méjì ti ní ibà àti ikọ́ fún ọ̀sẹ̀ méjì.
Clinical_Translation_English: Household overcrowding with eight individuals sharing a single sleeping room, and two co-habitating siblings with two weeks of febrile cough — conditions that facilitate airborne transmission of tuberculosis, meningococcal disease, and influenza.

Doctor_English: Are you currently taking any blood thinners such as aspirin, warfarin, or any injections for clots?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ń mu aspirin ní ọjọ́ kan fún ọkàn mi. Dọ́kítà ìgbẹ́yìn sọ pé kò sí ìdí fún rẹ̀ mọ́ ṣùgbọ́n mo ń tẹ̀síwájú mu.
Clinical_Translation_English: Self-continuation of aspirin therapy against recent medical advice, creating risk for gastrointestinal haemorrhage particularly in the context of the patient's current presentation, requiring medication reconciliation and prescriber communication.

Doctor_English: Have you ever had a bad reaction to any medication, including rashes, swelling, or difficulty breathing?
Patient_Yoruba: Oògùn pẹ̀nísílíìnì ṣe mí ní ìgbà tí mo wà lọ́mọdé. Awọ ara mi bò pẹ̀lú àpá, ọfun mi sì wú. A gbé mi wọ ilé ìwòsàn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Documented anaphylactic reaction to penicillin in childhood with urticaria and angioedema requiring hospitalisation, constituting a severe drug allergy that must be clearly documented and communicated to preclude all beta-lactam antibiotic use.

Doctor_English: Are you taking any medications that were prescribed for someone else, or bought without a prescription?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo máa ń ra amoxicillin ní ọjà lárugẹ nígbà tí n bá ní ikọ́. Abúrò mi tún fún mi ní oògùn ẹjẹ̀ rẹ̀ tí dọ́kítà fún un.
Clinical_Translation_English: Self-medication with over-the-counter amoxicillin for respiratory symptoms and use of a sibling's antihypertensive medication, practices that risk antibiotic resistance, inappropriate treatment, and adverse drug effects from unmonitored polypharmacy.

Doctor_English: How long have you been taking this medication, and have you noticed any side effects since starting it?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ti ń mu oògùn àtọ̀gbẹ̀ mi fún oṣù mẹ́fà. Lẹ́yìn ọ̀sẹ̀ méjì, inú mi bẹ̀rẹ̀ sí ṣòro àti ìgbẹ́ gbuuru.
Clinical_Translation_English: Gastrointestinal side effects of nausea and diarrhoea emerging two weeks after initiating metformin for type two diabetes, consistent with the known adverse effect profile of biguanide therapy, often mitigated by gradual dose titration and food co-administration.

Doctor_English: Are you using any contraceptive pills, injections, or implants, and for how long?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ń lo àbẹ́rẹ́ ìdásí fún ọdún méjì. Ó máa ń wáyé lẹ́ẹ̀mẹ́rin ní ọdún. Mo kò ní àìsàn kankan lẹ́yìn rẹ̀ tó jẹ aríwo.
Clinical_Translation_English: Injectable progestogen-only contraception used for two years with regular three-monthly administration and no reported significant adverse effects, requiring blood pressure monitoring and discussion of long-term bone density implications.

Doctor_English: Have you recently stopped any long-term medication suddenly without your doctor's advice?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo dáwọ̀ mu oògùn irora ẹ̀gbẹ̀ mi dúró lójijì ní ọ̀sẹ̀ kan sẹ́yìn nítorí owó. Mi ò sọ fún dọ́kítà mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Abrupt discontinuation of long-term prednisolone therapy one week ago without medical supervision, creating significant risk for adrenal crisis secondary to hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis suppression, requiring urgent cortisol assessment and steroid reinstatement.

Doctor_English: Do you use any eye drops, ear drops, or skin creams regularly, even if they seem minor?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ń lo ìpàrà awọ ara tó ń ṣe awọ ara funfun fún ọdún mẹ́ta. Mo ra a ní ọjà, kò ní àmì orúkọ kankan.
Clinical_Translation_English: Prolonged use of an unlabelled skin-lightening cream for three years, raising concern for chronic topical corticosteroid or hydroquinone exposure with potential systemic absorption, adrenal suppression, and cutaneous complications including atrophy and telangiectasia.

Doctor_English: Are you taking any medications for mental health, such as antidepressants or sleeping tablets?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà fún mi ní oògùn orun ní ọdún kan sẹ́yìn. Mo máa ń mu fún ọ̀sẹ̀ díẹ̀ lẹ́yìn náà mo dáwọ̀. Mo bẹ̀rẹ̀ mú wọn lẹ́ẹ̀kan sí i nísinsìnyí.
Clinical_Translation_English: Intermittent self-regulated use of prescribed hypnotics with periods of self-discontinuation and reinstatement without clinical review, indicating possible dependence risk and the need for structured medication review and psychological support.

Doctor_English: Have you been given any traditional or herbal injections by a healer or non-medical practitioner?
Patient_Yoruba: Onísègùn kan fi abẹ́rẹ́ kan sínú ẹsẹ̀ mi ní ọsẹ̀ méjì sẹ́yìn. Ó sọ pé yóò mú irora ìpín mi lọ. Mi ò mọ̀ ohun tó wà nínú rẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Unsterile injection administered by a traditional healer two weeks ago at the site of joint pain, creating significant risk for injection site infection, septic arthritis, bloodborne pathogen transmission including HIV and hepatitis B, and adverse reaction to an unknown substance.

Doctor_English: Do you chew any tobacco products, kola nut, or other stimulants regularly?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo máa ń jẹ obì lẹ́ẹ̀mẹ́fà ní ọjọ́ kan. Ó ṣeni lẹ́nu bí mi ò bá jẹ ẹ. Mo ti ń ṣe bẹ́ẹ̀ fún ọdún mẹ́wàá.
Clinical_Translation_English: Daily kola nut chewing six times per day with ten-year habitual use indicating caffeine and stimulant dependence, with implications for cardiovascular stimulation, dental erosion, and potential drug interactions with prescribed medications.

Doctor_English: How many pregnancies have you had, and how many children are currently alive?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ti lóyún lẹ́ẹ̀mẹ́fà. Ọmọ márùn-ún ni mo ní. Ọ̀kan kú nígbà ìbímọ, àti ọ̀kan mọ̀ sọnu nígbà oyún kẹrin.
Clinical_Translation_English: Gravida six, para five, with a neonatal death and one miscarriage, indicating a complex obstetric history requiring careful documentation of pregnancy complications to guide management of the current presentation.

Doctor_English: Have you discussed family planning with your partner, and what method are you currently using?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọkọ mi kò fẹ́ kí a lo ohun ìdáàbòbò. Ó sọ pé àwọn ọmọ jẹ́ ẹbùn Ọlọ́run. Mo ń lo àbẹ́rẹ́ ìdásí lẹ́gbẹ̀rẹ̀ láìsí ìmọ̀ rẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Covert use of injectable contraception without partner knowledge due to spousal opposition to family planning, highlighting the need for sensitive, confidential, and non-judgmental reproductive health counselling within the sociocultural context.

Doctor_English: Have you had any pain during your menstrual period that is severe enough to stop you from your daily activities?
Patient_Yoruba: Irora oṣù mi máa ń le tó jẹ́ kí n máà lè lọ iṣẹ́ fún ọjọ́ méjì ní gbogbo oṣù. Mo máa ń mu oògùn irora ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Severe dysmenorrhoea causing two days of monthly work absence and requiring analgesia, warranting investigation for endometriosis, adenomyosis, or fibroids as secondary causes beyond primary dysmenorrhoea.

Doctor_English: Has your partner ever been treated for a sexually transmitted infection, and were you treated at the same time?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọkọ mi ni wọ́n tọ́jú fún àkóràn ìbálòpọ̀ ní ọdún kan sẹ́yìn. Wọn kò sọ fún mi tàbí tọ́jú mi. Mo kò mọ̀ ohun tó ń ṣẹlẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Failure to provide partner notification and concurrent treatment following a confirmed sexually transmitted infection in the spouse, indicating ongoing re-infection risk and necessitating immediate STI testing, empirical treatment, and partner contact tracing.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any change in your menstrual cycle since starting your current medication?
Patient_Yoruba: Lẹ́yìn tí mo bẹ̀rẹ̀ oògùn tuntun mi, oṣù mi dáwọ̀ wáyé fún oṣù mẹ́ta. Mo dúpẹ́ ṣùgbọ́n mo ti bẹ̀rẹ̀ sí yọ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Pharmacologically-induced amenorrhoea of three months duration following a new medication, requiring review of the causative agent — potentially an antipsychotic causing hyperprolactinaemia or a progestogen — and exclusion of pregnancy.

Doctor_English: Have you had a cervical smear test done, and when was the last one?
Patient_Yoruba: Mi ò mọ̀ ohun tí ìdánwò ìkùn ọmọ ìdọ̀tí jẹ́. Mi ò tíì ṣe ìdánwò bẹ́ẹ̀ kankan rí. Mi ò mọ̀ pé o ṣeéṣe.
Clinical_Translation_English: No history of cervical cytology screening with absence of awareness of the procedure, indicating a significant gap in preventive healthcare uptake and the need for cervical screening education and immediate smear test scheduling.

Doctor_English: Have you had any difficulty with erection or sexual function that you would like to discuss?
Patient_Yoruba: Fún oṣù mẹ́fà, mo ti ń rí ìṣòro láti máa ṣiṣẹ́ nínú ìbálòpọ̀. Ó ń jẹ mí ìbànújẹ́ àti ti ọkàn mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Erectile dysfunction of six months duration causing significant psychological distress, requiring assessment for vascular, neurological, endocrinological, and psychogenic aetiologies, alongside medication review and partner counselling.

Doctor_English: Are you planning to become pregnant soon, and have you started taking folic acid?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìyàwó mi àti èmi ń gbìyànjú láti loyún. Dọ́kítà sọ pé kí a mu folic acid ṣùgbọ́n a kò bẹ̀rẹ̀ rárá.
Clinical_Translation_English: Couples planning conception with documented advice to commence preconception folic acid supplementation but non-initiated, requiring urgent supplementation commencement to reduce neural tube defect risk prior to and during early pregnancy.

Doctor_English: Have you experienced any pain, discharge, or swelling in the testicular or scrotal area?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìdọ̀tí mi ọ̀tún ti wú àti ń dùn fún ọjọ́ méjì. Irora náà wáyé lójijì, kò ní ìkìlọ̀ kankan, ó sì le gidigidi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Sudden onset unilateral scrotal pain and swelling of two days duration without prodrome in a male patient, requiring urgent scrotal ultrasound to exclude testicular torsion — an urological emergency where viability is time-dependent.

Doctor_English: Do you have any concerns about your fertility, or have you had any investigations done for this?
Patient_Yoruba: A ti gbìyànjú fún ọdún mẹ́ta láìsí oyún. Ìyàwó mi ti ṣe àyẹ̀wò, a sọ pé ó dára. Ìdánwò mi kò tíì ṣe.
Clinical_Translation_English: Three years of primary infertility with normal female partner evaluation and no male factor assessment performed, indicating the urgent need for semen analysis as the next diagnostic step in the infertility workup.

Doctor_English: How long have you had this toothache, and does it wake you up at night?
Patient_Yoruba: Eyín mi ń dùn fún ọjọ́ mẹ́wàá. Ó máa ń jí mi dìde ní alẹ́. Irora náà ń burú nígbà tí mo bá mu ohun tó tutù.
Clinical_Translation_English: Dental pain of ten days duration with nocturnal exacerbation and cold sensitivity, consistent with pulpitis or periapical abscess requiring urgent dental assessment and likely endodontic treatment or extraction.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any bleeding from your gums when you brush your teeth or eat?
Patient_Yoruba: Ẹ̀jẹ̀ máa ń jáde nínú eyín mi nígbà tí mo bá fọ eyín mi. Ó ti jẹ́ bẹ́ẹ̀ fún oṣù mẹ́fà. Mo ro pé ọṣẹ eyín ni.
Clinical_Translation_English: Spontaneous gingival bleeding on toothbrushing for six months, raising the differential diagnosis of gingivitis, periodontitis, thrombocytopaenia, or vitamin C deficiency, requiring dental examination and haematological screening.

Doctor_English: Do you have any loose teeth, or have any teeth fallen out recently without any injury?
Patient_Yoruba: Eyín méjì ti bọ̀ láìsí ìdájọ́ fún ọjọ́ kan. Àwọn tó ṣẹ́ kù ń jọ pé wọ́n ń gúnjú. Mo ní ẹni ogójì ọdún.
Clinical_Translation_English: Spontaneous tooth loss with remaining tooth mobility in a forty-year-old patient, indicating advanced periodontitis or an underlying systemic condition such as diabetes, leukaemia, or Paget's disease affecting alveolar bone.

Doctor_English: Do you have any sores or ulcers in your mouth that have not healed after two weeks?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọgbẹ́ kan wà nínú ẹnu mi fún ọjọ́ mẹ́ẹ̀ẹ́dọ́gbọ̀n. Kò ń dùn, kò ń sàn, kò sì ti yí padà ní àwọ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Non-healing painless oral ulcer of twenty-five days duration without colour change, exceeding the two-week threshold that mandates urgent biopsy to exclude oral squamous cell carcinoma, a condition with increased prevalence in tobacco and alcohol users.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any white patches or red patches inside your mouth that will not rub off?
Patient_Yoruba: Ibi funfun kan wà lórí ahọ́n mi tí kò lọ nígbà tí mo bá gbìyànjú láti pa a. Ó ti wà fún ọjọ́ ogún.
Clinical_Translation_English: Persistent white oral mucosal patch that cannot be wiped off for twenty days, consistent with leukoplakia — a potentially premalignant lesion requiring urgent oral medicine assessment and biopsy particularly in a tobacco user.

Doctor_English: Do you grind your teeth at night, and has anyone told you they can hear you doing it?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìyàwó mi sọ pé mo máa ń pa eyín papọ̀ gan-an ní alẹ́ tí ó ń jẹ́ kí ìró jáde. Mo máa ń jí pẹ̀lú ẹrẹ̀kẹ́ tó le.
Clinical_Translation_English: Nocturnal bruxism reported by partner with associated morning masseter pain, indicating sleep bruxism causing potential temporomandibular joint dysfunction, dental attrition, and headache requiring occlusal splint therapy.

Doctor_English: Is the jaw pain on one side or both sides, and does it click when you open your mouth wide?
Patient_Yoruba: Ẹrẹ̀kẹ́ ọ̀tún mi ń dùn nígbà tí mo bá ń jẹun tàbí sọ ọ̀rọ̀. Ìró ẹ̀gbọn máa ń wáyé nígbà tí mo bá ṣí ẹnu mi jájá.
Clinical_Translation_English: Unilateral temporomandibular joint pain with audible clicking on mouth opening, consistent with internal derangement of the temporomandibular joint requiring imaging and physiotherapy or occlusal splint management.

Doctor_English: Have you had any difficulty eating or chewing due to tooth pain or missing teeth?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ti dáwọ̀ jẹ ẹran àti oúnjẹ lile dúró nítorí irora eyín mi. Mo máa ń jẹ oúnjẹ rírọ̀ nìkan fún oṣù mẹ́ta.
Clinical_Translation_English: Dietary modification with avoidance of solid foods for three months due to dental pain, creating nutritional risk secondary to oral disease and indicating the need for urgent dental treatment alongside nutritional assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you ever had any teeth removed, and did you have any complications such as prolonged bleeding?
Patient_Yoruba: A yọ eyín mi mẹ́rin ní ìgbà tí mo jẹ́ ọ̀dọ́. Lẹ́yìn ọ̀kan nínú rẹ̀, ẹjẹ̀ ń jáde fún ọjọ́ méjì tí kò dúró.
Clinical_Translation_English: Post-extraction haemorrhage lasting two days following one of four previous dental extractions, suggesting an underlying coagulation disorder or platelet dysfunction that should be investigated before any planned dental or surgical procedure.

Doctor_English: Do you use any tobacco products, and do you know that tobacco increases the risk of mouth cancer?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo máa ń ṣe àmù tábà fún ọdún mẹ́wàá. Mi ò mọ̀ pé ó lè jẹ́ kí àrùn jẹjẹrẹ ẹnu wáyé. Kò sí ẹni tó sọ fún mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Ten-year history of tobacco chewing without knowledge of oral cancer risk, indicating a significant preventive health education gap and the need for immediate smoking cessation counselling alongside urgent oral mucosal examination.

Doctor_English: Do you understand what your diagnosis means, and can you explain it in your own words?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà sọ pé mo ní àtọ̀gbẹ̀, ṣùgbọ́n mi ò mọ̀ ohun tó jẹ́. Ó fi ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ ọ̀rọ̀ sọ tí kò wọ inú mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Patient unable to recall or explain their diabetes diagnosis following a medical consultation, indicating inadequate health literacy and communication, necessitating patient education using simplified language and visual aids.

Doctor_English: Have you been shown how to check your blood sugar at home, and do you have a glucometer?
Patient_Yoruba: A fún mi ní ẹrọ ìdánwò suga ṣùgbọ́n kò sí ẹni tó fi mọ mí bí a ṣe ń lo. Ó ṣì wà nínú àpótí rẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Glucometer provided without accompanying patient education on usage technique, resulting in non-utilisation of essential diabetes monitoring equipment and inability to achieve glycaemic self-management targets.

Doctor_English: Do you know the warning signs that would mean you need to come to hospital urgently?
Patient_Yoruba: Mi ò mọ̀ àwọn àmì ìkìlọ̀ tó túmọ̀ sí pé kí n yára wá ilé ìwòsàn. Mo máa ń dúró títí kí àìsàn náà tó burú jù.
Clinical_Translation_English: Patient lacks knowledge of emergency warning signs and describes a pattern of delayed healthcare presentation until symptoms are severe, indicating the need for structured health education on danger signs specific to their chronic condition.

Doctor_English: Can you read the label on your medication bottle, and do you understand the dosing instructions?
Patient_Yoruba: Mi ò kà ọ̀rọ̀ Gẹ̀ẹ́sì dáradára. Ọmọ mi ni ó máa ń ka àmì oògùn fún mi, ṣùgbọ́n kò wà ní ilé ní gbogbo ìgbà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Limited English literacy with dependence on a child for medication label interpretation, creating significant risk for dosing errors when the child is unavailable, and indicating the need for pictorial medication instructions in the patient's preferred language.

Doctor_English: Has anyone in your family or community told you that this illness is caused by a spiritual problem or curse?
Patient_Yoruba: Àwọn alàgbà ìdílé sọ pé àìsàn mi jẹ́ àṣà àjọ̀. Wọ́n fẹ́ kí n lọ bàbáláwo kí ó tó ilé ìwòsàn. Mo dúró fún ọjọ́ mẹ́wàá ṣáájú kí n tó wá.
Clinical_Translation_English: Ten-day delay in healthcare presentation due to family and community attribution of illness to spiritual causation and insistence on traditional healer consultation first, illustrating the impact of cultural health beliefs on treatment-seeking behaviour and clinical outcomes.

Doctor_English: Have you ever had an ECG or heart tracing done, and were you told anything was abnormal?
Patient_Yoruba: A ṣe ìdánwò ọkàn mi ní ọdún méjì sẹ́yìn. Dọ́kítà sọ pé nǹkan kan wà tó yàtọ̀ ṣùgbọ́n kò ṣàlàyé rẹ̀ dáradára fún mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Abnormal electrocardiogram finding two years prior with inadequate patient explanation, representing a missed opportunity for cardiac risk stratification and requiring immediate repeat ECG and cardiology review.

Doctor_English: Does your heart feel like it is beating very slowly, causing dizziness or fainting?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọkàn mi máa ń lu díẹ̀díẹ̀ tó ń jẹ́ kí ori mi yípo. Mo ṣubú lẹ́ẹ̀kan nígbà tí ó ṣẹlẹ̀ ní ọjà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Symptomatic bradycardia with presyncope and a syncopal episode in a public setting, requiring urgent ECG to exclude complete heart block, sick sinus syndrome, or medication-induced bradycardia.

Doctor_English: Have you been told you have a heart murmur, and do you know what is causing it?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà sọ pé wọ́n gbọ́ ìró àjèjì nínú ọkàn mi nígbà tí mo bí ọmọ. Kò sọ ohun tó fa á àti bóyá ó léwu.
Clinical_Translation_English: Incidentally detected cardiac murmur in the peripartum period without explanation of aetiology or significance, requiring echocardiography to exclude valvular disease, congenital heart defect, or high-output state.

Doctor_English: Do you get any swelling in your abdomen that feels like fluid sloshing around inside?
Patient_Yoruba: Ikùn mi ti wú, nígbà tí mo bá rọ́ sí ẹ̀gbẹ́, mo ń gbọ́ ìró omi. Ó ti ń pọ̀ sí i fún oṣù méjì.
Clinical_Translation_English: Progressive ascites with shifting dullness on clinical examination over two months, raising concern for hepatic cirrhosis, cardiac failure, or malignant peritoneal disease requiring urgent abdominal ultrasound and diagnostic paracentesis.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed that your legs are different sizes, with one being significantly more swollen than the other?
Patient_Yoruba: Ẹsẹ̀ ọ̀tún mi ti wú síwájú sí i ju ẹsẹ̀ òsì lọ. Ó bẹ̀rẹ̀ lẹ́yìn ìrìn-àjò gígùn, ó sì ń dùn nígbà tí a bá tẹ̀ ẹ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Asymmetric lower limb oedema with unilateral right-sided swelling, tenderness, and post-travel onset, constituting a classic presentation of deep vein thrombosis requiring urgent compression ultrasound and anticoagulation assessment.

Doctor_English: Do you experience any chest pain when you climb stairs, and does it go away when you stop?
Patient_Yoruba: Nígbà tí mo bá gùn ìtẹ̀lọ́ọ̀dọ̀, irora kéékèéké ń wáyé ní àyà mi. Nígbà tí mo bá dúró, ó máa ń lọ ní ìṣẹ́jú mẹ́ta.
Clinical_Translation_English: Stable exertional angina with reproducible chest pain on stair climbing and relief within three minutes of rest, consistent with fixed coronary artery stenosis requiring stress testing and cardiology referral.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any bluish discolouration of your fingers when they are exposed to cold?
Patient_Yoruba: Nígbà tí ojú ọjọ́ bá tutù tàbí mo bá fi ọwọ́ mi sínú omi tutù, ìlà ọwọ́ mi di àwọ̀ búlúù-funfun.
Clinical_Translation_English: Cold-induced digital colour change with cyanotic and pallor phases on cold exposure, consistent with Raynaud's phenomenon, which may be primary or secondary to connective tissue disease, requiring autoimmune screening.

Doctor_English: Have you been told your cholesterol is high, and are you on any medication for it?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà sọ pé ọrá ẹjẹ̀ mi ga ní ọdún mẹ́ta sẹ́yìn. A fún mi ní oògùn ṣùgbọ́n mo dáwọ̀ mu lẹ́yìn oṣù mẹ́fà nítorí irora iṣan.
Clinical_Translation_English: Statin discontinuation after six months due to myalgia, a recognised side effect affecting up to ten percent of patients, requiring creatine kinase measurement and consideration of an alternative lipid-lowering agent to maintain cardiovascular risk reduction.

Doctor_English: Does the chest tightness feel like a heavy weight pressing on your chest?
Patient_Yoruba: Àyà mi ń dín mọ́ bí ẹni pé àpótí tó wúwo wà lórí rẹ̀. Ó ń wáyé nígbà tí mo bá ń ṣiṣẹ́ tàbí nígbà tí inú mi bá bí mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Pressure-like retrosternal chest tightness precipitated by exertion and emotional stress, the classic anginal description, requiring twelve-lead ECG, cardiac biomarkers, and urgent cardiology assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you had any episodes where your vision went completely black for a few seconds before recovering?
Patient_Yoruba: Lẹ́ẹ̀mẹ́ta, ojú mi di òkùnkùn pátápátá fún ìṣẹ́jú mẹ́ta ṣáájú kó tó padà. Mo ń dúró nígbà tí ó ń ṣẹlẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Transient monocular or binocular visual loss lasting seconds to minutes with full recovery, consistent with amaurosis fugax from carotid artery disease or cardiac embolism, representing a transient ischaemic attack equivalent requiring urgent carotid imaging.

Doctor_English: Do you feel short of breath when you lie on your left side specifically?
Patient_Yoruba: Nígbà tí mo bá dubúlẹ̀ ní apá òsì, mí mi ń kúrú síwájú sí i ju àwọn ìgbà mìíràn lọ.
Clinical_Translation_English: Trepopnoea with positional dyspnoea specifically in the left lateral decubitus position, a rare but specific symptom of pericardial effusion or left-sided pleural disease requiring echocardiography and chest imaging.

Doctor_English: Has anyone ever performed CPR on you, or have you had a cardiac arrest before?
Patient_Yoruba: Bẹ́ẹ̀ ni, ọdún kan sẹ́yìn, wọ́n tẹ àyà mi ní ilé ìwòsàn nítorí ọkàn mi dáwọ̀. A fun mi ní ẹrọ mànàmáná lẹ́yìn náà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Prior cardiac arrest requiring cardiopulmonary resuscitation and defibrillation, indicating a high risk for recurrent ventricular arrhythmia and necessitating implantable cardioverter-defibrillator assessment and electrophysiology review.

Doctor_English: Have you been experiencing any weakness on one side of your body that comes and goes?
Patient_Yoruba: Apá àti ẹsẹ̀ ọ̀tún mi máa ń di àìlera fún ìṣẹ́jú díẹ̀, lẹ́yìn náà ó padà. Ó ti ṣẹlẹ̀ lẹ́ẹ̀mẹ́rin ní ọ̀sẹ̀ méjì.
Clinical_Translation_English: Recurrent transient unilateral motor weakness on four occasions over two weeks with complete resolution, constituting transient ischaemic attacks requiring urgent carotid Doppler imaging, echocardiography, and antiplatelet therapy.

Doctor_English: Have you had any episodes of forgetting words in the middle of a sentence or calling things by the wrong name?
Patient_Yoruba: Ní ìgbà-ìgbà, mo máa ń fẹ́ sọ ọ̀rọ̀ kan ṣùgbọ́n orúkọ rẹ̀ kò jáde. Mo máa ń pè àwọn nǹkan ní orúkọ tó yàtọ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Anomic aphasia with word-finding difficulty and paraphasia, indicating dominant hemisphere language area dysfunction potentially secondary to cerebrovascular disease, brain tumour, or neurodegenerative process.

Doctor_English: Does the headache affect your ability to see clearly, and does light make it worse?
Patient_Yoruba: Nígbà tí orí mi bá ń fọ̀, ojú mi ń ṣòòfà díẹ̀. Ìmọ̀lẹ̀ ń jẹ ki irora náà buru sí i, mo máa ń bò ara mi nínú ibọ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Migraine with visual disturbance and photophobia prompting light-avoidance behaviour, fulfilling diagnostic criteria for migraine with or without aura requiring acute abortive therapy and preventive management.

Doctor_English: Have you been having difficulty swallowing, and does food or drink sometimes go down the wrong way?
Patient_Yoruba: Oúnjẹ àti omi máa ń wọ ọ̀nà tó yàtọ̀ nígbà-ìgbà, ó ń jẹ́ kí n kúkú. Mo ti ní ìsẹ̀lẹ̀ mẹ́ta bẹ́ẹ̀ ní oṣù kan.
Clinical_Translation_English: Oropharyngeal dysphagia with aspiration episodes on three occasions in the past month, indicating neurological or structural pharyngeal pathology and significant aspiration pneumonia risk requiring videofluoroscopic swallowing study.

Doctor_English: Have you been told you walk like you are drunk, even when you have not had any alcohol?
Patient_Yoruba: Àwọn ọ̀rẹ́ mi sọ pé mo ń rìn bí ẹni tó mu ọtí, ṣùgbọ́n mi ò mu ọtí. Mo tún máa ń sọ ohun lẹ́yìn tí mo bá ń sọ ọ̀rọ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Cerebellar ataxia with broad-based gait and slurred speech without alcohol use, indicating cerebellar pathology requiring MRI posterior fossa imaging to exclude cerebellar degeneration, tumour, or demyelinating disease.

Doctor_English: Have you been experiencing any ringing or buzzing in your ears, and is it in one or both ears?
Patient_Yoruba: Etí ọ̀tún mi ń rò ní ìgbà-ìgbà bí ẹni pé àgọ̀ kéékèéké wà nínú rẹ̀. Ó bẹ̀rẹ̀ oṣù mẹ́ta sẹ́yìn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Unilateral right-sided tinnitus of three months duration, which when combined with ipsilateral hearing loss and vertigo would constitute Ménière's triad, or in isolation may indicate acoustic neuroma requiring audiological assessment and MRI internal auditory meati.

Doctor_English: Have you been having trouble sleeping — either difficulty falling asleep or waking up too early?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo máa ń jí dìde ní àárọ̀ ìmọ́lẹ̀ fún oṣù mẹ́fà, mi ò sì lè padà sùn. Mo ń rò ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ nǹkan nígbà yẹn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Early morning awakening with inability to return to sleep and ruminative cognition for six months, a pattern of insomnia strongly associated with major depressive disorder and requiring combined sleep hygiene intervention and psychiatric evaluation.

Doctor_English: Have you been snoring loudly at night, and has anyone told you that you stop breathing while asleep?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìyàwó mi sọ pé ìró ìrọ̀lẹ́ mi gbóná, àti pé ní ìgbà-ìgbà ó máa ń rí mi dáwọ̀ mí dúró fún ìṣẹ́jú díẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Witnessed apnoeic episodes during sleep with loud snoring reported by the spouse, the hallmark features of obstructive sleep apnoea requiring polysomnography and assessment for cardiovascular complications and daytime somnolence.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any changes in your sense of smell or taste recently?
Patient_Yoruba: Ní oṣù mẹ́ta, òórùn àti ìtọ́ oúnjẹ ti yí padà fún mi. Oúnjẹ tó máa ń dára jù ni mo máa ń jẹ kò ní ìtọ́ mọ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Anosmia and dysgeusia of three months duration, which may indicate post-viral olfactory nerve damage, anterior cranial fossa pathology, or Parkinson's disease in which hyposmia frequently precedes motor symptoms by years.

Doctor_English: Have you been having involuntary movements of your face, tongue, or limbs that you cannot control?
Patient_Yoruba: Ẹnu mi àti ahọ́n mi máa ń gúnjú lórí ara wọn láìsí ìfẹ́ mi. Ó ń ṣẹlẹ̀ nígbà tí mo bá sinmi, kò ń wáyé nígbà tí mo bá sùn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Oro-facial dyskinesia with involuntary tongue and facial movements at rest that disappear during sleep, consistent with tardive dyskinesia secondary to prolonged antipsychotic or metoclopramide use requiring medication review.

Doctor_English: Has your handwriting changed significantly and become smaller or harder to read?
Patient_Yoruba: Kikọ mi ti di kéré àti tó ṣòro láti kà. Àwọn ọ̀rẹ́ mi sọ pé wọ́n kò lè kà rẹ̀ mọ́. Ó ti jẹ́ bẹ́ẹ̀ fún ọdún kan.
Clinical_Translation_English: Progressive micrographia over one year, a specific neurological finding strongly associated with Parkinson's disease reflecting the hypokinesia and rigidity of fine motor control in the dominant hand.

Doctor_English: Have you been feeling pins and needles or numbness specifically after sitting or lying in one position?
Patient_Yoruba: Nígbà tí mo bá jókòó fún wákàtí kan, àwọn ìlà ẹsẹ̀ mi máa ń hu bí ẹni pé àbẹ̀rẹ̀ ń gún wọn. Ó máa ń dára nígbà tí mo bá dìde.
Clinical_Translation_English: Positional paraesthesia relieved by movement, most commonly due to peripheral nerve compression rather than systemic neuropathy, but requiring assessment in the context of the patient's other symptoms to exclude progressive neuropathic disease.

Doctor_English: Have you ever been diagnosed with hepatitis B or C, and are you on any treatment for it?
Patient_Yoruba: A sọ fún mi ní ọdún mẹ́fà sẹ́yìn pé mo ní jedojedo B. Wọ́n fún mi ní oògùn fún ọ̀sẹ̀ díẹ̀ lẹ́yìn náà kò sí nǹkan mọ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Hepatitis B diagnosis six years ago with incomplete treatment follow-up, raising concern for chronic active hepatitis, cirrhosis progression, and hepatocellular carcinoma risk, requiring hepatitis B viral load, liver function tests, and hepatology referral.

Doctor_English: Have you recently been around anyone diagnosed with meningitis, and have you been vaccinated against it?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọmọ ilé ẹ̀kọ́ ọmọ mi ni wọ́n sọ pé ó ní àrùn ọpọlọ àárọ̀. Wọ́n kò fún ọmọ mi ní àjesara rẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Close school-based contact with a confirmed meningitis case in an unvaccinated child, constituting a public health emergency requiring immediate chemoprophylaxis with rifampicin and urgent meningococcal vaccination of all close contacts.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any painless sore on your skin that has not healed, particularly on your leg or foot?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọgbẹ́ kéékèéké kan wà ní ẹsẹ̀ mi tó wà fún oṣù méjì. Kò ń dùn rárá, kò sì ń sàn bí mo tilẹ̀ pa ọ̀gbọ̀ mọ́ ọn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Chronic painless lower limb ulcer of two months duration without healing despite wound dressing, the clinical presentation of a Buruli ulcer caused by Mycobacterium ulcerans or a neuropathic diabetic foot ulcer, both requiring specialised wound care.

Doctor_English: Have you had any contact with animals, particularly rats, or been in a flooded area recently?
Patient_Yoruba: A rí eku ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ nínú ilé wa lẹ́yìn ìkún omi. Mo máa ń tẹ ìtọ́ eku nígbà tí mo bá ń fọ ìlẹ̀ ilé.
Clinical_Translation_English: Rodent infestation following flooding with direct contact with rat urine during cleaning activities, constituting significant leptospirosis exposure requiring clinical surveillance and empirical doxycycline treatment if symptomatic.

Doctor_English: Have you been experiencing any burning or tingling in the area of an old chickenpox scar?
Patient_Yoruba: Ní ẹ̀gbẹ́ ìdí mi, ibi tí àpá àjèjì ti ṣẹlẹ̀ nígbà mo wà lọ́mọdé, ìró iná ń wáyé fún ọjọ́ mẹ́ta ṣáájú àpá tuntun náà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Prodromal dermatomal burning and paraesthesia preceding vesicular eruption in a dermatome corresponding to a prior varicella scar, the classic presentation of herpes zoster reactivation requiring early antiviral therapy to reduce post-herpetic neuralgia risk.

Doctor_English: Have you had diarrhoea with blood or mucus in it, along with cramping abdominal pain and fever?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìgbẹ́ mi ní ẹjẹ̀ àti omi líle nínú fún ọjọ́ mẹ́ta. Irora ikùn tó le ń tẹ̀lé rẹ̀, àti ibà mi tún wà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Dysenteric syndrome with bloody mucoid diarrhoea, colicky abdominal pain, and fever for three days, consistent with invasive enterocolitis from Shigella, Entamoeba histolytica, or Campylobacter, requiring stool microscopy and culture.

Doctor_English: Have you had any recent injections or blood tests at a facility where you were unsure of the needle safety?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ṣe ìdánwò ẹjẹ̀ ní ilé ìwòsàn àdúgbò ní oṣù kan sẹ́yìn. Mi ò rí bóyá abẹ́rẹ̀ tuntun ni wọ́n lo.
Clinical_Translation_English: Potential exposure to bloodborne pathogens through an injection or venepuncture of uncertain sterility one month ago, warranting baseline HIV, hepatitis B, and hepatitis C serology and repeat testing at the appropriate window period.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any swelling of your lymph glands in the groin, and is there any associated sore nearby?
Patient_Yoruba: Èso wà ní ìdì mi ọ̀tún tó ti ń pọ̀ fún ọ̀sẹ̀ méjì. Ọgbẹ́ kéékèéké kan tún wà ní ẹsẹ̀ ìsàlẹ̀ ẹ̀gbẹ́ náà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Unilateral inguinal lymphadenopathy with an ipsilateral distal limb ulcer, raising concern for lymphogranuloma venereum, chancroid, or primary syphilis with reactive adenopathy, requiring swab culture and serological testing.

Doctor_English: Have you had any ear discharge, and has this been going on for a long time since childhood?
Patient_Yoruba: Etí mi ọ̀tún ń ṣàn láti ìgbà tí mo wà lọ́mọdé. Ó máa ń burú nígbà tí mo bá wẹ tàbí nígbà tí mo bá ní otutu.
Clinical_Translation_English: Chronic suppurative otitis media with longstanding unilateral ear discharge since childhood, exacerbated by water exposure and upper respiratory infections, requiring audiological assessment and ENT evaluation for cholesteatoma.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any painless swelling in your groin or scrotum that increases when you stand or cough?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìdì mi ọ̀tún ń wú nígbà tí mo bá dúró. Nígbà tí mo bá gbẹ ikọ́, ó pọ̀ sí i. Mo lè fi ọwọ́ tẹ̀ ẹ́ padà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Reducible inguinal swelling increasing on Valsalva manoeuvre consistent with an indirect inguinal hernia, requiring surgical assessment to determine elective versus urgent repair and to exclude strangulation risk.

Doctor_English: Have you had any sores inside your mouth or on your tongue, alongside fever and body aches?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọgbẹ́ kéékèéké tó ń dùn wà nínú ẹnu mi àti lórí ahọ́n mi. Ibà àti irora ara ń tẹ̀lé wọn. Ó bẹ̀rẹ̀ lẹ́yìn ọjọ́ mẹ́ta.
Clinical_Translation_English: Painful oral ulceration with systemic features of fever and myalgia, consistent with primary herpes simplex stomatitis, herpangina from coxsackievirus, or the mucosal manifestation of primary HIV infection.

Doctor_English: Have you travelled internationally recently, and did you take any malaria prevention medication?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo padà wá láti Cameroon ní ọjọ́ mẹ́wàá sẹ́yìn. Mi ò mu oògùn ìdáàbòbò fún àrùn ibà. Ẹfọn jẹ mí lọ́pọ̀lọpọ̀ níbẹ̀.
