Clinical_Translation_English: Return from a malaria-endemic West African country ten days ago without chemoprophylaxis and significant mosquito exposure, presenting within the incubation period for Plasmodium falciparum malaria and requiring urgent thick and thin blood film examination.

Doctor_English: Has your child ever had swelling of the hands or feet as a baby, along with crying and irritability?
Patient_Yoruba: Bẹ́ẹ̀ ni, nígbà tí ọmọ mi jẹ́ oṣù mẹ́fà, ọwọ́ àti ẹsẹ̀ rẹ̀ wú, ó sì ń sọkún tó burú jù. A wọ ilé ìwòsàn fún ọjọ́ mẹ́ta.
Clinical_Translation_English: Dactylitis in infancy with bilateral hand and foot swelling and pain crisis requiring hospitalisation, the earliest clinical manifestation of sickle cell disease, indicating the need for haemoglobin electrophoresis if not yet performed.

Doctor_English: Have you been told your blood count is low, and do you know which part of your blood is affected?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà sọ pé ẹjẹ̀ mi kéré ṣùgbọ́n kò ṣàlàyé bóyá ẹ̀jẹ̀ funfun, pupa, tàbí àwo ẹjẹ̀ ni ó kúrú.
Clinical_Translation_English: Undifferentiated cytopenia without patient education on the specific cell line affected, requiring a full blood count with differential to determine whether the deficiency is erythrocytic, leukocytic, or thrombocytic and guide further investigation.

Doctor_English: Have you been experiencing bone pain, particularly in your back, ribs, or hips?
Patient_Yoruba: Ẹ̀hin mi, ìbàdí mi, àti ìhà àyà mi ń dùn fún oṣù mẹ́ta. Irora náà kò ń lọ pátápátá, ó máa ń burú ní alẹ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Axial skeletal pain involving the back, pelvis, and ribs with nocturnal worsening over three months in an older patient, raising significant concern for multiple myeloma requiring serum protein electrophoresis, Bence Jones protein, and skeletal survey.

Doctor_English: Has anyone ever told you that your spleen is enlarged, and have you had it checked recently?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà kan sọ pé ẹ̀dọ̀ ìhà òsì mi ti pọ̀ ní ọdún méjì sẹ́yìn. Mi ò ṣe àyẹ̀wò mọ́ lẹ́yìn náà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Previously documented splenomegaly without subsequent follow-up or investigation of the underlying aetiology, requiring repeat abdominal examination and ultrasound to assess for progression and investigate causes including haematological malignancy, portal hypertension, or chronic infection.

Doctor_English: Have you been getting repeated mouth ulcers alongside a persistent sore throat and fever?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọgbẹ́ ẹnu mi ń wáyé ní ìgbà-ìgbà. Ọfun mi tún ń dùn, ibà sì ń tẹ̀lé. Ó ti jẹ́ bẹ́ẹ̀ fún oṣù mẹ́fà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Cyclical oral ulceration with recurrent pharyngitis and fever over six months, consistent with cyclic neutropenia or agranulocytosis, requiring serial full blood counts to demonstrate the periodic nadir of neutrophil count.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed your urine turning very dark or cola-coloured after exercise or illness?
Patient_Yoruba: Nígbà tí mo bá ń ṣeré sáájú tàbí nígbà àìsàn, ito mi máa ń di dúdú bí kọfì. Ó ti ṣẹlẹ̀ lẹ́ẹ̀mẹ́ta.
Clinical_Translation_English: Exercise or illness-triggered haemoglobinuria producing dark urine on three occasions, consistent with glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase deficiency, march haemoglobinuria, or paroxysmal nocturnal haemoglobinuria.

Doctor_English: Have you been experiencing excessive fatigue alongside yellowing of your skin after taking certain medications?
Patient_Yoruba: Lẹ́yìn tí mo mu oògùn àrùn ìbà, awọ ara mi di pàálu àti àárẹ̀ mi pọ̀ sí i. Dọ́kítà sọ pé ẹjẹ̀ mi ń fọ.
Clinical_Translation_English: Drug-induced haemolytic anaemia manifesting as jaundice and fatigue following antimalarial therapy, consistent with G6PD deficiency-related oxidative haemolysis triggered by primaquine or dapsone exposure.

Doctor_English: Has your child been told they are severely anaemic during routine checks, and have you been given iron supplements?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà ilé-ìwòsàn àdúgbò sọ pé ẹjẹ̀ ọmọ mi kéré gan-an. A fún wa ní ìrọ̀bí irin ṣùgbọ́n ọmọ mi kọ jíjẹ rẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Severe paediatric anaemia identified at a community health visit with iron supplementation prescribed but refused by the child, requiring dietary iron counselling, liquid formulation consideration, and investigation for underlying malaria or hookworm infestation as a contributory cause.

Doctor_English: Have you been told you have leukaemia or any blood cancer, and what treatment have you been offered?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà ní ilé ìwòsàn gíga sọ pé mo ní àrùn jẹjẹrẹ ẹjẹ̀ fún oṣù mẹ́ta. Wọ́n sọ fún mi nípa oògùn kẹmíkà ṣùgbọ́n mi ò bẹ̀rẹ̀ rẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Recently diagnosed haematological malignancy with offered chemotherapy not yet commenced three months post-diagnosis, indicating treatment delay that may allow disease progression and requires urgent oncology re-engagement and psychosocial support.

Doctor_English: Have you had any unusual bleeding from your gums, nose, or into your skin in the form of bruises?
Patient_Yoruba: Ẹjẹ̀ ń jáde nínú imu mi ní ìgbà-ìgbà, ẹ̀jẹ̀ tún ń jáde nínú eyín mi nígbà tí mo bá jẹun. Mo tún ní àwọn ọgbẹ́ aró tó pọ̀ lára mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Multi-site spontaneous bleeding including epistaxis, gingival haemorrhage, and extensive ecchymoses forming a haemorrhagic diathesis, raising urgent concern for thrombocytopaenia, coagulation factor deficiency, or disseminated intravascular coagulation.

Doctor_English: Does your child appear pale, tired, and less active than other children of the same age?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọmọ mi kò ṣeré pẹ̀lú àwọn ọmọ mìíràn mọ́. Ó máa ń jókòó nìkan, ojú rẹ̀ ti di funfun. Ó ti jẹ́ bẹ́ẹ̀ fún oṣù méjì.
Clinical_Translation_English: Pallor, exercise intolerance, and social withdrawal in a child over two months, clinical features of significant anaemia in a paediatric patient requiring urgent full blood count, peripheral blood film, and malaria rapid diagnostic test.

Doctor_English: Have you been told your iron levels are very low, and do you know why you might be losing iron?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìdánwò fihàn pé irin nínú ẹjẹ̀ mi kéré pupọ̀. Dọ́kítà kò ṣàlàyé ìdí rẹ̀. Mo jẹ ẹran díẹ̀ àti ẹ̀fọ tó tó.
Clinical_Translation_English: Iron deficiency without identified aetiology or patient education on causation, requiring investigation for occult gastrointestinal blood loss, menorrhagia, malabsorption, or dietary insufficiency before initiating replacement therapy.

Doctor_English: Are you finding it harder to remember appointments, names, or where you put your belongings?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo gbàgbé àwọn ìpàdé mi ní ìgbà-ìgbà. Mo sọ orúkọ àwọn ọ̀rẹ́ mi tó jìnnà àti mo gbàgbé ibi tí mo ti fi àwọn nǹkan mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Progressive episodic memory impairment affecting appointments, proper nouns, and object location — early features of amnestic mild cognitive impairment or early Alzheimer's disease requiring formal neuropsychological assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you had any difficulty recognising familiar faces, including close family members?
Patient_Yoruba: Ní ìgbà-ìgbà, mo máa ń wo ọmọ mi ní pípẹ́ ṣáájú kí n tó mọ̀ rẹ̀. Ẹẹ̀kan, mi ò mọ̀ ìyàwó mi nígbà tí ó wọ inú yàrá.
Clinical_Translation_English: Prosopagnosia with failure to recognise a spouse, indicating significant temporal-occipital lobe dysfunction beyond normal ageing and consistent with moderate to advanced dementia requiring multidisciplinary assessment and carer support.

Doctor_English: Are you currently living alone, and who would you contact if you had a medical emergency?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ń gbé nìkan. Àwọn ọmọ mi wà ní Abuja àti Lọ́ndọ̀n. Mi ò ní ọ̀rẹ́ tó wà nítòsí tí yóò ràn mí lọ́wọ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Elderly patient living alone without proximate social support, with children residing in geographically distant locations, indicating significant vulnerability and the need for emergency care planning, medical alert systems, and community support services.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any problems with your feet, such as sores, thickened nails, or difficulty cutting them?
Patient_Yoruba: Ẹsẹ̀ mi ń dùn nítorí àwọn àgàdo tó ga. Ìlà ẹsẹ̀ mi pọ̀ àti kúnjú, mo sì kò lè gé wọn mọ́ nítorí ojú mi kò ríran.
Clinical_Translation_English: Diabetic or geriatric foot complications including corns, onychomycosis, and inability to perform self-nail care due to visual impairment, creating a significant infection risk requiring podiatric assessment and diabetic foot surveillance.

Doctor_English: Has the patient been wandering at night or trying to leave the house unsafely?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìyá mi jí ní òru lẹ́ẹ̀mẹ́ta ó sì gbìyànjú láti jáde lọ. Ó sọ pé ó ń lọ ṣiṣẹ́, ṣùgbọ́n ti fẹ́yìntì nígbà pípẹ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Nocturnal wandering with disorientation to time and place — believing she is going to work from which she retired long ago — indicating a moderate stage of dementia with behavioural and psychological symptoms requiring environmental safety modification and possible pharmacological intervention.

Doctor_English: Have you been experiencing any urinary leakage when you cough, sneeze, or laugh?
Patient_Yoruba: Nígbà tí mo bá gbẹ ikọ́ tàbí rẹ́rìn-ín, ito máa ń jáde díẹ̀ láìsí ìfẹ́ mi. Ó ń jẹ ẹlẹ́yà mi àti mo ń yẹra fún àwọn ẹlòmíràn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Stress urinary incontinence with social withdrawal due to embarrassment, a common but under-reported condition in elderly and parous women, requiring pelvic floor physiotherapy assessment and consideration of surgical intervention if conservative measures fail.

Doctor_English: Have you had any recent bone fracture from a minor fall or bump that would not normally break a bone?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ṣubú lẹ́yìn ìjókòó kéékèéké, ẹsẹ̀ ìhà àyà mi sì ya. Dọ́kítà sọ pé ó yà nítorí ìjàǹbá kéékèéké tó ṣeni lẹ́nu.
Clinical_Translation_English: Fragility fracture of a rib following a minor mechanical fall, diagnostic of osteoporosis by clinical criteria and requiring DEXA scanning, fracture risk assessment, calcium, vitamin D, and bisphosphonate therapy.

Doctor_English: Are you able to get in and out of the bath or shower safely, and do you use any grab rails?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ṣubú nínú ìwẹ̀ ní oṣù kan sẹ́yìn. A kò ní àtìlẹ́yìn kankan nínú ìwẹ̀. Ọmọ mi sọ pé a máa ń rà á.
Clinical_Translation_English: Bathroom fall one month ago in the absence of grab rails or assistive equipment, indicating a modifiable home hazard requiring occupational therapy assessment and urgent installation of bathroom safety adaptations.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any change in the size or shape of your abdomen, with visible veins across the surface?
Patient_Yoruba: Ikùn mi ti pọ̀ sí i àti mo lè rí àwọn iṣan ọ̀nà ẹjẹ̀ tó hàn lórí awọ ara mi. Ìdọ̀tí inu rẹ̀ tún máa ń jẹ mí.
Clinical_Translation_English: Caput medusae with visible dilated abdominal wall veins in a patient with ascites, a pathognomonic sign of portal hypertension secondary to liver cirrhosis requiring urgent hepatology assessment and variceal screening.

Doctor_English: Do you have any difficulty getting up off the floor if you fall, even without an injury?
Patient_Yoruba: Bí mo bá ṣubú, mi ò lè dìde láìsí ìrànlọ́wọ́ ẹlòmíràn. Mo gbìyànjú fún ìṣẹ́jú mẹ́wàá kí n tó gbé ẹni tó kọjá lọ.
Clinical_Translation_English: Inability to rise from the floor independently following a fall, indicating severe proximal muscle weakness or deconditioning in an elderly patient and highlighting the need for physiotherapy assessment, strength training, and falls prevention education.

Doctor_English: Has your child received vitamin A supplementation at the last clinic visit?
Patient_Yoruba: A fún ọmọ mi ní vitamin A ní ọjọ́ Immunisation ní oṣù mẹ́fà sẹ́yìn. Mi ò mọ̀ bóyá a fún un lẹ́yìn náà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Vitamin A supplementation six months ago at the last immunisation contact with uncertain subsequent dosing, raising concern for vitamin A deficiency in a child at risk, which causes night blindness, increased susceptibility to infections, and xerophthalmia.

Doctor_English: Have you ever had a blood pressure check done at a community health outpost or pharmacy?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọdún méjì sẹ́yìn, ìdánwò ẹjẹ̀ ìpàná mi ṣẹlẹ̀ ní ọjà. Wọ́n sọ pé ó ga, ṣùgbọ́n mi ò lọ ilé ìwòsàn lẹ́yìn rẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Elevated blood pressure detected at a community screening event two years ago without subsequent clinical follow-up, representing a missed opportunity for early hypertension management and secondary organ damage prevention.

Doctor_English: Do you own a mobile phone, and could you use it to access health information or telemedicine?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ní fóònù tó ń wọlé, ṣùgbọ́n mi ò mọ̀ bí a ṣe ń lo rẹ̀ fún ìwòsàn. Ọmọ mi tó ń kọ́ ilé ẹ̀kọ́ ló máa ń ràn mí lọ́wọ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Mobile phone ownership with digital health literacy gap in an elderly rural patient, indicating potential for telemedicine access facilitated by a younger family member, which could significantly reduce transport barriers to specialist consultation.

Doctor_English: Have you been able to attend all your antenatal appointments, or have any been missed due to transport or cost?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ti padanu àwọn ìbẹ̀wò oyún mẹ́ta nítorí mi ò ní owó ìrìn-àjò àti ọkọ̀ tó ń lọ sí ilé ìwòsàn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Three missed antenatal appointments due to financial and transport barriers, representing a gap in obstetric care associated with increased risk for undetected complications such as pre-eclampsia, gestational diabetes, and foetal growth restriction.

Doctor_English: Have you heard about any disease outbreaks in your community recently, such as cholera or measles?
Patient_Yoruba: Àwọn ọ̀rẹ́ mi sọ pé àwọn ọmọ díẹ̀ ní abúlé wa ti ní ìbà àti gbuuru ní ọ̀sẹ̀ méjì sẹ́yìn. Kò sí ìkìlọ̀ fún ilé ìwòsàn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Community reports of febrile diarrhoeal illness in children without formal disease notification two weeks prior, indicating a possible cholera or rotavirus outbreak requiring urgent public health reporting, water source testing, and oral rehydration distribution.

Doctor_English: Do you know the danger signs of malaria in children, and what would you do if your child had them?
Patient_Yoruba: Mi ò mọ̀ àwọn àmì tó léwu. Nígbà tí ọmọ mi ń gbẹ̀ ikọ́ àti ní ibà, mo máa ń fún un ní oògùn ibà tí mo ra ní ọjà kíákíá.
Clinical_Translation_English: Absence of knowledge of danger signs of severe malaria with self-treatment using over-the-counter antimalarials, delaying recognition of cerebral malaria, severe anaemia, or respiratory compromise which require urgent hospitalisation.

Doctor_English: Have you been using a treated bed net consistently for all members of your household?
Patient_Yoruba: Àwọ̀ oṣupa wa ti ní iho, a kò tíì rà tuntun. Àwọn ọmọ kéékèéké ni a máa ń fi bo, àwọn àgbàlagbà máa ń sùn ìta ní ìgbà ooru.
Clinical_Translation_English: Deteriorating bed net with holes and incomplete household coverage, with adults sleeping outside during warm months, indicating inadequate malaria vector control and requiring net replacement and household behaviour change communication.

Doctor_English: Has your child been given deworming tablets at school or at the clinic this year?
Patient_Yoruba: Mi ò rí ẹni tó fún ọmọ mi ní oògùn ìjẹ nínú ọdún yìí. Ní ọdún tó kọjá, ilé ẹ̀kọ́ ló fún wọn.
Clinical_Translation_English: No school-based or clinic deworming treatment received in the current year, interrupting the deworming cycle in a high-prevalence area and creating risk for soil-transmitted helminth reinfection affecting nutritional status and cognitive development.

Doctor_English: Do you have a health insurance card, and have you been able to use it without problems?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ní káàdì ìṣègùn ìjọba ṣùgbọ́n ilé ìwòsàn tó wà nítòsí wa kò gba rẹ̀ mọ́. Mo máa ń san owó láti ara mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: National health insurance card holder experiencing facility-level rejection of coverage, resulting in out-of-pocket expenditure and potential financial catastrophe, indicating a systemic healthcare access barrier requiring patient advocacy and insurer engagement.

Doctor_English: Has a community health worker visited your home recently, and what advice did they give you?
Patient_Yoruba: Àṣìṣọ ìlera abúlé wá ilé wa ní oṣù mẹ́ta sẹ́yìn. Ó sọ̀rọ̀ nípa ìwẹ̀ ọwọ́ àti jíjẹ oúnjẹ tó dára. Mi ò tẹ̀lé ìmọ̀ wọn pátápátá.
Clinical_Translation_English: Community health worker visit with hygiene and nutrition counselling three months ago with partial adherence, indicating ongoing risk and the value of follow-up reinforcement visits to consolidate behaviour change in the household.

Doctor_English: Have you been told you need an operation, but you have been unable to have it done due to cost?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà sọ fún mi pé kí wọ́n ṣe iṣẹ́ abẹ ìdì mi fún ọdún kan sẹ́yìn. Mi ò ní owó, a sì kò tíì ṣe rẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: One-year delay in recommended inguinal hernia repair due to financial constraints, with increasing risk of hernia incarceration or strangulation — a surgical emergency — illustrating the direct impact of healthcare cost barriers on clinical outcomes.

Doctor_English: Do you know your HIV status, and when were you last tested if you have been tested before?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ṣe ìdánwò HIV nígbà tí mo ń lóyún ọdún mẹ́ta sẹ́yìn, àbájáde rẹ̀ sálòdì. Lẹ́yìn náà mi ò tíì ṣe ìdánwò mọ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Last HIV test three years ago in the antenatal context with a negative result, with no subsequent retesting despite ongoing potential exposure risk, warranting a current fourth-generation HIV test and counselling on regular testing intervals.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any lump in your breast that was not there before, and does it move freely or feel fixed?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo rí èso kan nínú ọmú òsì mi ní oṣù kan sẹ́yìn nígbà tí mo ń wẹ̀. Kò ń dùn, ó sì dì, mi ò lè jí i.
Clinical_Translation_English: A painless, fixed breast lump of one month duration is a red flag for malignancy, distinguishing it from the mobile, tender lumps of fibroadenoma, and mandating urgent triple assessment with clinical examination, imaging, and core biopsy.

Doctor_English: Have you lost weight without trying, and have you been feeling full very quickly after eating just a small amount?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ti tẹ̀nìí ní oṣù mẹ́ta láìgbìyànjú. Mo sì máa ń yó lẹ́sẹ̀kẹsẹ̀ lẹ́yìn jíjẹ ohun díẹ̀ péré.
Clinical_Translation_English: Unintentional weight loss over three months with early satiety, a symptom complex raising strong concern for upper gastrointestinal malignancy including gastric or pancreatic cancer, requiring urgent upper endoscopy and cross-sectional imaging.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any change in a mole — has it grown, changed colour, or started bleeding?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìwọ̀ dúdú tó wà lórí ẹ̀gbẹ́ ẹ̀hin mi ti pọ̀ sí i àti yí padà sí àwọ̀ onírúurú ní oṣù mẹ́fà. Mo rí ẹjẹ̀ kéékèéké lẹ́ẹ̀kan.
Clinical_Translation_English: Changing pigmented lesion with growth, colour variegation, and a bleeding episode over six months, fulfilling multiple ABCDE criteria for melanoma and requiring urgent excision biopsy and dermatology referral.

Doctor_English: Have you had any difficulty passing urine, blood in your urine, or bone pain, particularly in your back?
Patient_Yoruba: Ito mi di díẹ̀ àti irora ń wáyé nígbà tí mo bá ń ṣe. Mo tún ní irora ẹ̀hin tó máa ń burú ní alẹ́ fún oṣù mẹ́fà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Lower urinary tract obstruction with haematuria and nocturnal axial bone pain in an older male patient, the triad of advanced prostate cancer with possible skeletal metastases requiring PSA testing, digital rectal examination, and bone scan.

Doctor_English: Has anyone in your family had bowel cancer, and have you ever had a colonoscopy?
Patient_Yoruba: Ẹ̀gbọ́n mi kú nítorí àrùn jẹjẹrẹ ìfun ní ọjọ́-orí àádọ́ta. Mi ò tíì ṣe ìdánwò ìfun rárá rí.
Clinical_Translation_English: First-degree family history of colorectal cancer with early onset death, placing the patient in a high-risk category requiring colonoscopy at least ten years before the age of the affected relative's diagnosis, which should have been initiated already.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any hoarseness in your voice lasting more than three weeks without a cold?
Patient_Yoruba: Ohùn mi ti yí padà, di bíi ẹni tó ń ṣàìsàn ní ọfun fún ọ̀sẹ̀ mẹ́fà, ṣùgbọ́n kò sí otutu tàbí ikọ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Persistent hoarseness of six weeks duration without upper respiratory infection, a red flag symptom for laryngeal carcinoma particularly in tobacco users requiring urgent direct laryngoscopy and ENT assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you been experiencing any difficulty passing stool, or alternating between constipation and diarrhoea?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìgbẹ́ mi ti yí padà ní oṣù mẹ́fà. Ní ìgbà-ìgbà mo ní àìgbẹ́, lẹ́yìn náà ìgbẹ́ gbuuru máa ń tẹ̀lé.
Clinical_Translation_English: Altered bowel habit with alternating constipation and diarrhoea persisting over six months in a middle-aged patient, a red flag symptom for colorectal carcinoma requiring urgent colonoscopy regardless of the absence of rectal bleeding.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any swelling or lump in your neck that has been there for more than four weeks?
Patient_Yoruba: Èso kan wà ní ẹ̀gbẹ́ ọrùn mi ọ̀tún fún oṣù méjì. Kò ń dùn. Wọ́n fun mi ní oògùn àkóràn ṣùgbọ́n kò lọ.
Clinical_Translation_English: Persistent cervical lymphadenopathy for two months unresponsive to antibiotic therapy, exceeding the four-week threshold warranting urgent biopsy to exclude lymphoma, metastatic squamous cell carcinoma, or tuberculosis.

Doctor_English: Have you had any unexplained bone fractures or severe bone pain that woke you from sleep?
Patient_Yoruba: Ẹsẹ̀ ìhà àyà mi ya lẹ́yìn ìjàǹbá kéékèéké. Mo tún ní irora ẹ̀hin tó máa ń jí mi ní àárọ̀ alẹ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Pathological rib fracture from minimal trauma with nocturnal axial bone pain in a patient over fifty, raising significant concern for multiple myeloma or skeletal metastases requiring full blood count, serum protein electrophoresis, and skeletal survey.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any blood in your phlegm when you cough, even just a small streak, for more than one week?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ti rí ẹjẹ̀ pupa nínú ìtọ́ ikọ́ mi fún ọ̀sẹ̀ méjì. Ó kéré ṣùgbọ́n ó máa ń wáyé ní gbogbo ọjọ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Daily haemoptysis for two weeks in a patient with smoking history, a red flag for bronchogenic carcinoma requiring urgent chest CT, sputum cytology, and bronchoscopy regardless of the small volume of blood.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any change in your bowel habits alongside mucus in your stool without blood?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìgbẹ́ mi ti yí padà, omi líle àti bíi ìró omi ń jáde papọ̀ pẹ̀lú rẹ̀. Mo rí ìyẹn fún oṣù mẹ́ta.
Clinical_Translation_English: Altered stool consistency with mucus discharge for three months, while blood-free, may indicate a colorectal villous adenoma, mucous fistula, or early colorectal malignancy requiring colonoscopic evaluation.

Doctor_English: Have you had any unexplained itching all over your body without any visible rash?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìmọ̀ ara mi ń ṣe mí gbogbo ara fún oṣù mẹ́fà. Mi ò rí àpá tàbí àmì kankan lórí ara mi. Ó máa ń burú ní alẹ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Generalised pruritus without a primary skin lesion for six months with nocturnal predominance, a paraneoplastic phenomenon associated with Hodgkin's lymphoma, polycythaemia vera, or cholestasis requiring haematological and hepatic evaluation.

Doctor_English: Have you been feeling very shaky, sweaty, and confused before meals, and does eating immediately help?
Patient_Yoruba: Ṣáájú oúnjẹ ọjọ́-ọjọ́, mo máa ń gbọn àti ríru. Inú mi di àásìkí díẹ̀. Nígbà tí mo bá jẹ, gbogbo rẹ̀ máa ń dára lẹ́sẹ̀kẹsẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Pre-prandial autonomic and neuroglycopaenic symptoms with immediate resolution on food intake, the classic presentation of reactive hypoglycaemia requiring a supervised seventy-two hour fast and insulin-to-glucose ratio measurement to exclude insulinoma.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any hair loss from your eyebrows or outer part of your eyelashes?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìbọ̀sí oju mi àti irun ìhà ìmọ̀lẹ̀ ojú mi ti bẹ̀rẹ̀ sí dínkù. Ó bẹ̀rẹ̀ ní ìhà ìmọ̀lẹ̀ ẹ̀gbẹ́ ojú mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Lateral eyebrow thinning with loss of the outer third of the eyelashes, a specific clinical sign of hypothyroidism known as Queen Anne's sign, strongly supporting thyroid hormone deficiency requiring TSH and free T4 measurement.

Doctor_English: Have you been experiencing excessive sweating, heat intolerance, and a fast heartbeat together?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ń gbó lọ́pọ̀lọpọ̀ paápàá nígbà tí ojú ọjọ́ kò gbóná. Ọkàn mi lu yára, àti mi ò farada ìgbóná.
Clinical_Translation_English: Heat intolerance with diaphoresis and tachycardia constituting the adrenergic triad of hyperthyroidism, requiring urgent thyroid function tests including TSH, free T4, and free T3, with electrocardiographic monitoring for atrial fibrillation.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any change in your face shape, particularly enlargement of your jaw, nose, or hands?
Patient_Yoruba: Àwọn ọ̀rẹ́ mi sọ pé ojú mi ti yí padà, ẹrẹ̀kẹ́ mi ti pọ̀ sí i. Orúkọ àti ọwọ́ mi tún dabi pé wọ́n pọ̀ ju ìgbà àárọ̀ lọ.
Clinical_Translation_English: Progressive facial coarsening with jaw, nasal, and hand enlargement noted by close contacts, the insidious clinical manifestations of acromegaly secondary to growth hormone excess from a pituitary adenoma, requiring IGF-1 measurement and pituitary MRI.

Doctor_English: Have you been experiencing episodes of very high blood pressure, sweating, and pounding headache together?
Patient_Yoruba: Ní ìgbà-ìgbà, orí mi máa ń fọ̀ gan-an, ara mi ń gbó, àti ọkàn mi ń lu yára yára papọ̀. Ó máa ń pẹ́ fún ìṣẹ́jú díẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Episodic hypertensive crises with diaphoresis and pounding headache forming the classic triad of phaeochromocytoma, requiring twenty-four hour urinary catecholamine and metanephrine measurement and adrenal imaging.

Doctor_English: Have you been gaining weight specifically around your abdomen with thin arms and legs?
Patient_Yoruba: Ikùn mi àti ìhà àyà mi ń pọ̀ sí i ṣùgbọ́n apá àti ẹsẹ̀ mi di tínrín. Awọ ara mi tún ti di tinrín àti dídán.
Clinical_Translation_English: Central adiposity with peripheral muscle wasting and skin thinning constituting a cushingoid distribution, raising concern for Cushing's syndrome requiring midnight cortisol, twenty-four hour urinary free cortisol, and dexamethasone suppression testing.

Doctor_English: Have you been passing very large volumes of urine, more than three litres a day, and drinking enormous amounts?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ń ṣe ito tó pọ̀ gan-an ní gbogbo ọjọ́. Mo máa ń jí dìde lẹ́ẹ̀mẹ́fà ní alẹ́ láti ṣe ito. Omi tún kò tó mí.
Clinical_Translation_English: Massive polyuria with nocturia six times per night and polydipsia unrelieved by drinking, indicating diabetes insipidus rather than diabetes mellitus, requiring simultaneous plasma and urine osmolality measurement and a water deprivation test.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any purple or reddish stretch marks on your abdomen, thighs, or upper arms?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọgbẹ́ aṣọ pupa-olódi wà lórí ikùn mi, ìdí mi, àti àpá mi ìsàlẹ̀. Wọ́n dín ṣùgbọ́n wọ́n ti wà fún oṣù mẹ́fà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Wide violaceous striae on the abdomen, thighs, and upper arms, distinguishing Cushing's syndrome from simple obesity where striae are typically narrow, white, and less conspicuous, and requiring urgent endocrine evaluation.

Doctor_English: Have you been told your parathyroid hormone or calcium level is abnormal on a blood test?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà sọ pé kálsíọ̀mù nínú ẹjẹ̀ mi ga fún ọdún kan. Ó fún mi ní omi láti mu ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ ṣùgbọ́n kò sọ fún mi ìdí rẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Asymptomatic hypercalcaemia identified one year ago without investigation of the aetiology, the most common cause of which is primary hyperparathyroidism, requiring parathyroid hormone measurement, neck ultrasound, and renal function assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any skin changes such as increased pigmentation in the creases of your hands or in scars?
Patient_Yoruba: Àwọn ìpò ọwọ́ mi ti di dúdú síwájú sí i, pàápàá ní àwọn ìpín. Ọgbẹ́ iṣẹ́ abẹ mi tún ti di dúdú.
Clinical_Translation_English: Hyperpigmentation of the palmar creases and surgical scars indicating increased ACTH drive stimulating melanocyte receptors, the characteristic cutaneous sign of primary adrenal insufficiency requiring an urgent short Synacthen test.

Doctor_English: Have you been having thoughts that people are watching you, following you, or trying to harm you?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo gbà gbọ́ pé àwọn ènìyàn ń tẹ̀lé mi nígbà tí mo bá jáde lọ. Mo rí wọn ní gbogbo ibi. Àwọn ẹbí mi sọ pé ohun mi ń fọ.
Clinical_Translation_English: Persecutory delusions with ideas of reference and family-confirmed behavioural change, indicating a psychotic disorder such as paranoid schizophrenia or a delusional disorder requiring urgent psychiatric assessment and antipsychotic therapy.

Doctor_English: Have you been feeling that your thoughts are being controlled by an outside force or broadcast to others?
Patient_Yoruba: Ní ìgbà-ìgbà, mo gbà gbọ́ pé ẹnì kan ń fi agbára rẹ̀ ṣakóso ìrọ̀lẹ́ mi. Mo tún gbà pé àwọn ènìyàn mìíràn lè gbọ́ ohun tí mo ń rò.
Clinical_Translation_English: First rank symptoms of schizophrenia comprising thought insertion and thought broadcasting, pathognomonic of a primary psychotic disorder requiring urgent psychiatric assessment, risk evaluation, and initiation of antipsychotic therapy.

Doctor_English: Have you ever harmed yourself deliberately, such as cutting or burning your skin?
Patient_Yoruba: Bẹ́ẹ̀ ni, ní ìgbà-ìgbà mo máa ń gé ara mi ní apá mi nígbà tí inú mi bá bí mi gan-an. Ó ń mú mi sinmi fún ìgbà díẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Non-suicidal self-injury through cutting as an emotional regulation strategy, indicating significant psychological distress and requiring dialectical behaviour therapy assessment, alongside risk stratification to exclude suicidal intent.

Doctor_English: Have you been using alcohol or drugs to calm your nerves before social situations?
Patient_Yoruba: Ṣáájú kí n tó lọ àsè tàbí ibi àpéjọ, mo máa ń mu ọtí díẹ̀ láti mú inú mi baalẹ̀. Mi ò lè rìn lọ láì mu.
Clinical_Translation_English: Anticipatory alcohol use as a coping mechanism for social anxiety, indicating alcohol dependence complicating social anxiety disorder, both of which require concurrent treatment to prevent reinforcement of avoidance behaviour.

Doctor_English: Have you been feeling disconnected from your body, as if you are watching yourself from outside?
Patient_Yoruba: Ní ìgbà-ìgbà, mo máa ń rí ara mi bí ẹni tó ń wo fíìmù. Mo máa ń sọ ọ̀rọ̀ ṣùgbọ́n ó dà bíi pé kọ̀ọ̀kan jẹ́ elòmíràn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Depersonalisation with dissociative experiences of observing oneself from an external perspective, a symptom seen in dissociative disorders, post-traumatic stress disorder, panic disorder, and drug intoxication requiring careful psychiatric history.

Doctor_English: Have you been eating very large amounts of food in a short time and then trying to get rid of it?
Patient_Yoruba: Ní ìgbà-ìgbà, mo máa ń jẹ oúnjẹ lọ́pọ̀lọpọ̀ gan-an ní ìṣẹ́jú díẹ̀. Lẹ́yìn náà mo máa ń gbìyànjú láti ègbọn rẹ̀ jáde nítorí ìbánújẹ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Binge-purge cycles with ego-dystonic shame following episodes of rapid excessive eating, the diagnostic hallmark of bulimia nervosa requiring nutritional assessment, dental evaluation for enamel erosion, and cognitive-behavioural therapy.

Doctor_English: Have you been avoiding eating because you are very afraid of gaining weight, even though others say you are thin?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo máa ń yẹra fún jíjẹun nítorí mo bẹ̀rù pé yóò mú mi pọ̀ sí i. Àwọn ọmọ mi sọ pé mo tẹ̀nìí jù ṣùgbọ́n inú mi ò gba.
Clinical_Translation_English: Weight phobia with restriction of food intake despite low body weight and denial of underweight status, constituting the core features of anorexia nervosa — a condition with the highest mortality rate of any psychiatric disorder requiring urgent nutritional rehabilitation.

Doctor_English: Have you been having panic attacks — sudden episodes of intense fear with racing heart and feeling you might die?
Patient_Yoruba: Ní ìgbà-ìgbà, ìbẹ̀rù tó le máa ń wáyé lójijì. Ọkàn mi ń lu yára yára, mí mi kúrú, mo sì ń gbà gbọ́ pé mo ń kú. Ó máa ń pẹ́ fún ìṣẹ́jú mẹ́wàá.
Clinical_Translation_English: Recurrent spontaneous panic attacks of ten minutes duration with cardiovascular and respiratory symptoms and a sense of impending death, fulfilling diagnostic criteria for panic disorder requiring cognitive-behavioural therapy and pharmacological management.

Doctor_English: Have you been experiencing mood swings that are very extreme — very high and energetic one week and very low the next?
Patient_Yoruba: Ní ìgbà-ìgbà, inú mi máa ń dára gan-an àti mo ń ṣiṣẹ́ sáájú. Ìgbà mìíràn, mi ò fẹ́ dìde nínú ibùsùn mọ́. Wọ́n máa ń yí padà ní ìgbà-ìgbà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Cyclical mood episodes alternating between periods of elevated energy and productivity and depressive withdrawal, consistent with bipolar affective disorder requiring mood stabiliser therapy and structured psychiatric follow-up.

Doctor_English: Have you been experiencing intrusive thoughts that you find disturbing and cannot stop, even though you know they make no sense?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìrọ̀lẹ́ bùrúkú máa ń wá sí inú mi lójijì tí mi ò fẹ́ rárá. Mo mọ̀ pé wọn kò ní ọgbọ́n ṣùgbọ́n wọn kò lọ. Ó ń jẹ mí lọ́pọ̀lọpọ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Ego-dystonic intrusive thoughts recognised as senseless by the patient but uncontrollable, the defining feature of obsessions in obsessive-compulsive disorder, requiring cognitive-behavioural therapy with exposure and response prevention.

Doctor_English: Has the baby been feeding well at the breast, and are you hearing the baby swallow during feeds?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọmọ mi ń gbìyànjú láti mu ọmu ṣùgbọ́n ó máa ń tú jáde. Mi ò gbọ́ ohùn gbígbé. Ọmú mi tún dùn mi ní ìpín.
Clinical_Translation_English: Ineffective latch with audible swallowing absent and maternal nipple pain, indicating poor breastfeeding technique or possible tongue-tie, requiring lactation assessment and examination of the infant's oral anatomy.

Doctor_English: Has your child been meeting their developmental milestones — sitting, walking, and talking at the expected ages?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọmọ mi jẹ́ ọmọ ọdún méjì ṣùgbọ́n kò sọ ọ̀rọ̀ kankan rárá. Ó ń rìn ṣùgbọ́n kò dáhùn sí orúkọ rẹ̀ tàbí ìpè.
Clinical_Translation_English: Language delay with absence of any words at age two years and failure to respond to name, raising significant concern for autism spectrum disorder, hearing impairment, or global developmental delay requiring urgent audiological assessment and developmental paediatric evaluation.

Doctor_English: Has your child been having episodes of breath-holding, turning blue, and then becoming limp?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọmọ mi máa ń dáwọ mí dúró nígbà tí ó bá bínú tàbí dùn. Ó máa ń di búlúù, lẹ́yìn náà ó máa ń di rọ. Mo bẹ̀rù púpọ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Cyanotic breath-holding spells triggered by pain or frustration with post-ictal hypotonia, a benign but frightening condition requiring parental reassurance, while cardiac and neurological causes of episodic cyanosis must be excluded.

Doctor_English: Has the child been having episodes of staring blankly into space and being unresponsive for a few seconds?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọmọ mi máa ń dúró, ó sì máa ń wò sí ibi kan pẹ̀lú ojú tí ó ṣòòfà fún ìṣẹ́jú méjì. A pè é ṣùgbọ́n kò dáhùn. Lẹ́yìn náà ó padà bí ẹni tí kò sẹlẹ̀ kankan.
Clinical_Translation_English: Absence seizures with brief episodes of unresponsive staring and automatic resumption of activity without post-ictal confusion, requiring electroencephalography to confirm the classic three hertz spike-and-wave discharge of childhood absence epilepsy.

Doctor_English: Has the child been wetting the bed at night, even though they were previously dry?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọmọ mi ọdún mẹ́jọ ti padà sí sísọ ito nínú ibùsùn rẹ̀ ní alẹ́ lẹ́yìn ọdún méjì tí ó ṣe dáadáa. Ó bẹ̀rẹ̀ lẹ́yìn ìgbà tí a bí ọmọ tuntun.
Clinical_Translation_English: Secondary nocturnal enuresis following the birth of a sibling in a previously continent eight-year-old, suggesting a psychosocial stressor response rather than primary enuresis, requiring family assessment and regression management alongside enuresis treatment.

Doctor_English: Has your child been complaining of tummy aches before school but feeling fine at weekends?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọmọ mi máa ń sọ pé ikùn rẹ̀ ń dùn ni gbogbo owúrọ̀ ọjọ́ ìkọ́lé. Ní ọjọ́ Àìkú àti Ìṣẹ́gun, ó dára pátápátá. Dọ́kítà kò rí nǹkan.
Clinical_Translation_English: School-day specific abdominal pain with complete weekend resolution and no organic findings, consistent with functional abdominal pain or school refusal with somatic presentation, requiring psychosocial assessment and school liaison.

Doctor_English: Has the child been losing weight, drinking excessively, and wetting the bed more than usual?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọmọ mi ti tẹ̀nìí, ó ń fẹ omi lọ́pọ̀lọpọ̀, àti pé ó ti padà sí sísọ ito nínú ibùsùn. Ó bẹ̀rẹ̀ ní ọ̀sẹ̀ mẹ́ta sẹ́yìn.
Clinical_Translation_English: The classical triad of weight loss, polydipsia, and secondary enuresis in a child over three weeks, the presentation of new-onset type one diabetes mellitus, requiring immediate capillary glucose measurement and urgent paediatric admission.

Doctor_English: Has your child had any joint swelling, rash on the cheeks, and fever at the same time?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọmọ mi ní ìbà, àpá pupa kan lórí ẹrẹ̀kẹ́ méjèéjì bíi ẹni tí apoké fẹ̀ẹ́ pa a, àti wiwú ní ìpín ọwọ́ rẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: The triad of fever, malar rash with butterfly distribution, and joint swelling in a child, consistent with systemic juvenile idiopathic arthritis or systemic lupus erythematosus, requiring ANA, anti-dsDNA, and complement levels.

Doctor_English: Has your adolescent child been losing weight rapidly and exercising excessively despite being thin?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọmọ mi ọmọbìnrin ọdún mẹ́rìnlá ti tẹ̀nìí gan-an ṣùgbọ́n ó ń sáré àti ṣeré lọ́pọ̀lọpọ̀ ní gbogbo ọjọ́. Ó kọ jíjẹ pẹ̀lú àwọn ẹbí.
Clinical_Translation_English: Rapid weight loss with compulsive exercise and food refusal in an adolescent female, a presentation consistent with anorexia nervosa at a critical developmental stage requiring urgent nutritional assessment and eating disorder specialist referral.

Doctor_English: Has the child had any seizures with fever before, and were they told it might happen again?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọmọ mi gbọn nígbà tí ibà rẹ̀ ga ní ọjọ́-orí ọdún kan àbọ̀. Dọ́kítà sọ pé ó lè ṣẹlẹ̀ lẹ́ẹ̀kan sí i ṣùgbọ́n kò sọ ohun kó ṣe.
Clinical_Translation_English: Prior simple febrile seizure with parental counselling on recurrence risk but no documented guidance on fever management or emergency response, requiring education on febrile seizure first aid and the circumstances warranting emergency attendance.

Doctor_English: Have you been experiencing any domestic violence or abuse at home that might be affecting your health?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọkọ mi máa ń na mi nígbà tí ó bá mu ọtí. Mo bẹ̀rù láti sọ nítorí àwọn ọmọ. Mi ò mọ̀ tí yóò ràn mí lọ́wọ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Disclosure of intimate partner violence with fear of speaking out due to parental concern for children, requiring a safe, confidential environment, safeguarding assessment, information on domestic violence support services, and documentation in medical records.

Doctor_English: Have you been under significant financial stress recently, and is this affecting your ability to buy food or medication?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ti padà iṣẹ́ mi ní oṣù mẹ́fà sẹ́yìn. Mi ò lè ra oògùn mi gbogbo oṣù. Àwọn ọmọ mi jẹ oúnjẹ lẹ́ẹ̀kan péré ní ọjọ́ kan.
Clinical_Translation_English: Unemployment-related financial hardship causing medication non-adherence and food insecurity for six months, representing social determinants directly impacting health outcomes and requiring social work referral and medication cost support assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you recently lost a close family member, and how have you been coping with the bereavement?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìyá mi kú ní oṣù méjì sẹ́yìn. Mo ti dáwọ̀ jẹun àti ìgbàfọkànsín dúró. Mi ò lè sùn mọ́ àti mi ò ní ìfẹ́ fún ìgbé ayé.
Clinical_Translation_English: Complicated grief response following maternal bereavement two months ago with anhedonia, insomnia, and passive suicidal ideation, exceeding normal bereavement and meeting criteria for major depressive disorder requiring urgent psychiatric referral.

Doctor_English: Have you ever experienced any sexual abuse or assault, and would you like to talk about how it has affected you?
Patient_Yoruba: Nǹkan bùrúkú ṣẹlẹ̀ sí mi nígbà tí mo wà lọ́mọdé. Mi ò sọ rẹ̀ fún ẹnìkẹ́ni rí. O ń tún wáyé nínú irọ̀lẹ́ mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Disclosure of childhood sexual abuse with persistent nightmares and lifelong secrecy, indicating post-traumatic stress disorder requiring trauma-informed care, specialist psychological therapy, and a non-judgmental, patient-led disclosure process.

Doctor_English: Are you currently homeless or in insecure housing, and does this affect your ability to store medication safely?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ń gbé nínú yàrá tí mo máa ń yí padà fún oṣù mẹ́fà. Kò sí ibi tó dára láti fi oògùn mi pamọ́. Mo tún ń padà ibi tí mo ń jẹun.
Clinical_Translation_English: Housing insecurity with rotational accommodation and inability to store temperature-sensitive or controlled medications safely, directly compromising treatment adherence and requiring social housing referral and community pharmacy blister pack dispensing arrangement.

Doctor_English: Have you been working very long hours, and do you feel this is affecting your physical or mental health?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo máa ń ṣiṣẹ́ fún wákàtí mẹ́rìnlá ní ọjọ́ kan, ọjọ́ mẹ́fà ní ọ̀sẹ̀. Mo ti bẹ̀rẹ̀ sí máà gbọ́ àárẹ̀ àti orí fífọ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Occupational overwork of eighty-four hours per week with emerging fatigue and headache, constituting a workplace health risk associated with cardiovascular disease, burnout, and impaired immune function requiring occupational health assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you been exposed to any workplace bullying or harassment that has been causing you stress?
Patient_Yoruba: Olórí iṣẹ́ mi máa ń yà mí sọ̀tọ̀ nínú àwọn ìpàdé àti ń fún mi ní iṣẹ́ tó fún wa. Mo bẹ̀rù láti lọ iṣẹ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Workplace bullying manifesting as exclusion and unreasonable workload demands causing occupational anxiety and avoidance, a recognised occupational health issue requiring documentation, occupational health referral, and legal advice on workplace rights.

Doctor_English: Do you have anyone at home who depends on you for care, such as an elderly parent or a child with disability?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ń tọ́jú ìyá mi tó ní àrùn ọpọlọ àti ọmọ mi kan tó ní àìṣeéṣe ara. Mo kò ní àkókò fún ara mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Dual carer burden for a parent with dementia and a child with disability with no personal time allocation, indicating significant carer stress associated with burnout, depression, and physical health neglect requiring carer support services and respite care.

Doctor_English: Have you recently immigrated or moved to a new area, and do you feel isolated or unsupported?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo gbéjáde ìlú mi wá Èkó ní ọdún kan sẹ́yìn. Mi ò ní ẹbí níbí. Àwọn ọ̀rẹ́ tuntun mi kò mọ̀ àìsàn mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Recent internal migration to Lagos with social isolation, absence of family support, and lack of social network aware of the patient's health conditions, creating vulnerability for delayed healthcare seeking and inadequate support during illness.

Doctor_English: Have you been able to attend school regularly, and has illness been causing you to miss lessons?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ti padànu ọjọ́ ìkọ́lé mẹ́wàá ní oṣù kan nítorí àìsàn. Olùkọ́ mi sọ pé mo máa ń ṣàṣeyọrí tí mo bá wà ní kíláàsì.
Clinical_Translation_English: Ten school days missed in one month due to recurrent illness with documented academic impact, indicating a chronic health condition affecting educational outcomes and requiring integrated school health programme involvement and individualised educational support plan.

Doctor_English: Have you started your periods yet, and do you have any concerns about them being irregular or painful?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo bẹ̀rẹ̀ oṣù mi ní ọdún kan sẹ́yìn ṣùgbọ́n kò ń wáyé ní ìgbà tó yẹ. Irora rẹ̀ le tó jẹ́ kí n dúró sí ilé ní ọjọ́ kan.
Clinical_Translation_English: Secondary amenorrhoea with irregular cycles and dysmenorrhoea causing school absence in an adolescent one year post-menarche, warranting evaluation for polycystic ovarian syndrome, hyperprolactinaemia, and endometriosis.

Doctor_English: Have you been feeling pressure from peers to drink alcohol, smoke, or use drugs?
Patient_Yoruba: Àwọn ọ̀rẹ́ mi máa ń pè mi láti mu ọtí àti mu sìgá nígbà tí a bá jọ. Mo ti gbìyànjú lẹ́ẹ̀mejì ṣùgbọ́n mi ò fẹ́ tẹ̀síwájú.
Clinical_Translation_English: Adolescent experiencing peer pressure for substance use with two prior exposure events but expressed ambivalence, indicating an opportunity for motivational interviewing, substance use education, and resilience-building intervention before dependence develops.

Doctor_English: Have you had any sexual experiences, and do you feel you were always able to say no if you wanted to?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ti ní ìbálòpọ̀ pẹ̀lú ọkùnrin kan ní ẹ̀ẹ̀kan. Mi ò dára pẹ̀lú rẹ̀ ṣùgbọ́n mi ò sọ bẹ́ẹ̀ nítorí mo bẹ̀rù.
Clinical_Translation_English: Adolescent disclosure of a coerced sexual encounter without expression of refusal due to fear, requiring a trauma-informed response, safeguarding assessment, STI screening, emergency contraception consideration if recent, and referral to youth sexual health services.

Doctor_English: Have you been feeling very unhappy about the way your body looks, and has this affected your eating?
Patient_Yoruba: Mi ò fẹ́ ara mi. Mo rí ara mi bí ẹni tó pọ̀ jù bí mo tilẹ̀ tẹ̀nìí. Mo ti dín jíjẹ mi kù láti gbìyànjú láti yí ara mi padà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Body dysmorphic cognitions with restriction of food intake in an adolescent who is objectively underweight but perceives themselves as overweight, indicating early anorexia nervosa requiring urgent eating disorder assessment and family-based therapy.

Doctor_English: Have you been getting enough sleep, and what time do you usually go to bed and wake up?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo máa ń sùn ní ìdajì òru nítorí fóònù mi àti ìkẹ́kọ̀ọ́. Mo máa ń jí ní àárọ̀ ìmọ́lẹ̀ fún ìkọ́lé. Mo ń sùn wákàtí márùn-ún nìkan.
Clinical_Translation_English: Adolescent sleep deprivation of five hours nightly secondary to screen time and academic pressure, below the recommended eight to ten hours and associated with impaired cognition, mood dysregulation, obesity risk, and immune dysfunction.

Doctor_English: Have you ever thought about hurting yourself, and do you have a plan to do so?
Patient_Yoruba: Ní ọ̀sẹ̀ kan sẹ́yìn, mo rò pé bí mo bá sọ ẹ̀mí mi, àwọn ọ̀rẹ́ mi kò ní mọ̀ mọ́. Mo ti rò nípa ọ̀nà kan ṣoṣo.
Clinical_Translation_English: Active suicidal ideation with a specific plan in an adolescent, constituting a psychiatric emergency requiring immediate risk assessment, emergency department referral, removal of access to means, and family notification with safeguarding considerations.

Doctor_English: Have you been using social media a lot, and has it been making you feel worse about yourself?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo máa ń lo Instagram fún wákàtí mẹ́fà ní ọjọ́ kan. Nígbà tí mo bá rí àwọn ọ̀rẹ́ mi lórí rẹ̀, mo máa ń ní ìmọ̀lára pé mi ò tó wọn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Excessive social media use of six hours daily with social comparison and low self-esteem, a pattern associated with adolescent depression and anxiety disorders requiring digital wellbeing education and cognitive-behavioural intervention.

Doctor_English: Have you been bullied at school or online, and how long has this been going on?
Patient_Yoruba: Àwọn ọmọ mẹ́ta ní kíláàsì mi máa ń yà mí sọ̀tọ̀ àti fi ọ̀rọ̀ bùrúkú sílẹ̀ nípa mi lórí WhatsApp fún oṣù méjì.
Clinical_Translation_English: Prolonged cyberbullying and social exclusion over two months causing significant psychological distress, a recognised risk factor for adolescent depression, self-harm, and suicidal ideation requiring school safeguarding team involvement and psychological support.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any bulging near your groin that gets bigger when you stand or strain?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìdì mi ọ̀tún ń wú nígbà tí mo bá dúró tàbí gbé ohun tó wúwo. Mo lè fi ọwọ́ tẹ̀ ẹ́ padà nígbà tí mo bá dùbúlẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Reducible right inguinal hernia enlarging on standing and straining with spontaneous reduction on recumbency, requiring elective surgical repair to prevent the complication of incarceration or strangulation.

Doctor_English: Have you had sudden severe pain around your navel that has now moved to the right lower side?
Patient_Yoruba: Irora bẹ̀rẹ̀ ní àárín ikùn mi ní òwúrọ̀. Ní báyìí, ó ti rìn wá sí apá ọ̀tún ìsàlẹ̀ tó sì ń bani lóru. Mo ń ríru pẹ̀lú.
Clinical_Translation_English: Classical migratory pain from the periumbilical region to the right iliac fossa with nausea, the hallmark presentation of acute appendicitis requiring urgent surgical assessment, blood count, and CT abdomen.

Doctor_English: Have you had any surgery before, and did you develop a painful lump at the operation scar afterwards?
Patient_Yoruba: A ṣe iṣẹ́ abẹ ìdọ̀tí fún mi ní ọdún méjì sẹ́yìn. Ní báyìí, èso kan tó ń dùn wà ní ìhà ọgbẹ́ iṣẹ́ abẹ náà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Incisional hernia at a previous laparotomy site presenting as a painful reducible lump, a recognised surgical complication requiring elective repair with mesh reinforcement to prevent strangulation.

Doctor_English: Have you been having episodes of severe colicky pain in your right upper abdomen after eating fatty foods?
Patient_Yoruba: Lẹ́yìn tí mo bá jẹ oúnjẹ tó ní ọ̀rá, irora tó le ń wáyé ní ìhà ọ̀tún ìsàlẹ̀ àyà mi tó ń tan sí ẹ̀hin mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Post-prandial right upper quadrant colic with radiation to the back, the characteristic presentation of biliary colic from cholelithiasis, requiring abdominal ultrasound and surgical cholecystectomy referral.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any swelling at the back of your knee that is soft and fluctuant?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìhà ẹ̀hin orúkún mi ọ̀tún ń wú, ó rọ nígbà tí a bá fi ọwọ́ kan rẹ̀. Ó ń bani lóru nígbà tí mo bá tẹ orúkún mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Posterior knee swelling that is soft and compressible, consistent with a popliteal cyst (Baker's cyst) often associated with intra-articular pathology such as meniscal tear or osteoarthritis requiring knee MRI.

Doctor_English: Have you had any rectal bleeding with a lump that comes out when you go to the toilet and needs to be pushed back?
Patient_Yoruba: Ẹjẹ̀ pupa ń jáde nígbà tí mo bá ń ṣe ìgbẹ́, àti nǹkan kan ń jáde nínú ìdọ̀tí mi tí mo máa ń fi ọwọ́ tẹ̀ padà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Prolapsing haemorrhoids with rectal bleeding and manual reduction required, consistent with grade three haemorrhoidal disease requiring proctoscopy to confirm and surgical haemorrhoidectomy or banding consideration.

Doctor_English: Have you been having severe pain in your scrotum that started suddenly without any injury?
Patient_Yoruba: Irora tó le gan-an wáyé nínú ìdọ̀tí mi ọ̀tún lójijì ní àárọ̀ àná. Kò sí ìjàǹbá. Ìdọ̀tí mi tún ti wú àti ga.
Clinical_Translation_English: Sudden severe unilateral scrotal pain with swelling and high-riding testis in the absence of trauma, the classic presentation of testicular torsion — a urological emergency where detorsion within six hours is required to salvage the testis.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any ulcers or sores on your leg that have been present for months without healing?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọgbẹ́ kan wà ní ẹsẹ̀ ìsàlẹ̀ mi ọ̀tún tó wà fún oṣù mẹ́fà. Ó ń jáde omi, kò ń sàn pẹ̀lú oògùn tí mo rà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Chronic venous leg ulcer of six months duration with serous discharge and failure of self-treatment, requiring wound assessment, Doppler studies to exclude arterial disease, compression bandaging, and wound care specialist referral.

Doctor_English: Have you had pain in your calf after a long flight or period of immobility that is now spreading upward?
Patient_Yoruba: Lẹ́yìn ìrìn-àjò ọkọ̀ òfuurufú wakati mẹ́wàá, ẹsẹ̀ òsì mi bẹ̀rẹ̀ sí dùn àti wú. Irora ti ń rìn gaju síwájú sí i.
Clinical_Translation_English: Progressive post-flight lower limb pain and swelling with proximal extension, high-risk presentation for iliofemoral deep vein thrombosis requiring urgent compression ultrasound and anticoagulation to prevent pulmonary embolism.

Doctor_English: Have you been passing urine very slowly with stopping and starting, and feeling that your bladder is never empty?
Patient_Yoruba: Ito mi ń jáde díẹ̀díẹ̀ pẹ̀lú dídáwọ̀ dúró àti bẹ̀rẹ̀ lẹ́ẹ̀kan sí i. Lẹ́yìn tí mo bá ṣe ito, mo ń jẹ pé ìkùn ito mi kò ṣòfò.
Clinical_Translation_English: Obstructive lower urinary tract symptoms with hesitancy, intermittent stream, and incomplete emptying sensation in a male patient, consistent with benign prostatic hyperplasia requiring digital rectal examination, PSA measurement, and urodynamic assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed a painless soft lump on the back of your wrist that appears and disappears?
Patient_Yoruba: Èso rọ kan wà lórí ẹ̀hin ọwọ́ mi ọ̀tún tó máa ń han àti sọ̀nù. Ó máa ń pọ̀ nígbà tí mo bá lo ọwọ́ mi lọ́pọ̀lọpọ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Fluctuating soft dorsal wrist lump varying with activity, consistent with a ganglion cyst arising from the scapholunate ligament, which may be managed conservatively, aspirated, or excised depending on symptom severity.

Doctor_English: Have you had any difficulty with swallowing that is getting progressively worse over weeks?
Patient_Yoruba: Gbígbé oúnjẹ di ìṣòro díẹ̀díẹ̀ fún mi ní oṣù méjì. Kíákíá bẹ̀rẹ̀ pẹ̀lú ohun tó le, ní báyìí omi tún ń ṣòro.
Clinical_Translation_English: Progressive dysphagia over two months from solids to liquids, the alarming symptom sequence of oesophageal malignancy or severe stricture requiring urgent upper gastrointestinal endoscopy and biopsy.

Doctor_English: Have you been having pain and stiffness in your hips and shoulders that is worse after rest?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìbàdí mi àti èjìká mi máa ń dùn àti jáko lẹ́yìn ìsùn. Ó máa ń yẹ̀ lẹ́yìn wákàtí kan tàbí méjì ti ìgbésẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Bilateral shoulder and hip girdle pain with prolonged morning stiffness improving with activity in a patient over fifty, the characteristic presentation of polymyalgia rheumatica requiring ESR, CRP, and trial of low-dose corticosteroid.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed your fingers turning white then blue then red when exposed to cold?
Patient_Yoruba: Nígbà tí ọwọ́ mi bá fi ohun tutù mú, ìlà ọwọ́ mi di funfun kíákíá, lẹ́yìn náà búlúù, lẹ́yìn náà pupa tó ń dùn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Triphasic colour change of the digits with cold exposure representing the complete Raynaud's phenomenon sequence of vasospasm, deoxygenation, and reactive hyperaemia, requiring investigation for secondary causes including systemic sclerosis and lupus.

Doctor_English: Have you developed any tightening or thickening of the skin on your hands or face?
Patient_Yoruba: Awọ ara ọwọ́ mi ti di kúnjú àti lile ní oṣù mẹ́fà. Ojú mi tún ti di kúnjú, ẹnu mi sì ti di kéré díẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Progressive skin induration affecting the hands and face with microstomia over six months, characteristic features of systemic sclerosis requiring autoantibody testing including anti-Scl-70 and anti-centromere antibodies and organ function assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you been having dry eyes and dry mouth together, and do you have difficulty swallowing dry food?
Patient_Yoruba: Ojú mi máa ń gbẹ gan-an, pàápàá ní àárọ̀ owúrọ̀. Ẹnu mi tún gbẹ, àti gbígbé oúnjẹ gbígbẹ ń ṣòro fún mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Sicca syndrome with bilateral xerophthalmia and xerostomia causing dysphagia to dry food, the cardinal features of Sjögren's syndrome requiring anti-Ro and anti-La antibody testing and ophthalmological assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any rash on your cheeks and nose that looks like a butterfly, made worse by sunlight?
Patient_Yoruba: Àpá pupa kan wà lórí ẹrẹ̀kẹ́ mi méjèéjì tó ń kọjá orí imu mi. Ó máa ń burú sí i nígbà tí mo bá wà níta ní ìmọ̀lẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Photosensitive malar rash with butterfly distribution sparing the nasolabial folds, the hallmark cutaneous feature of systemic lupus erythematosus requiring ANA, anti-dsDNA, complement levels, and urine protein assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you been having lower back pain and stiffness that is worst first thing in the morning and improves with exercise?
Patient_Yoruba: Ẹ̀hin ìsàlẹ̀ mi máa ń dùn àti jáko ní àárọ̀ owúrọ̀, tó ń pẹ́ fún wákàtí méjì. Ìdárayá ń mú ki ó yẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Inflammatory back pain with morning stiffness exceeding one hour improving with exercise, the hallmark of axial spondyloarthropathy such as ankylosing spondylitis, requiring HLA-B27 testing, MRI sacroiliac joints, and rheumatology referral.

Doctor_English: Have you had episodes of sudden severe joint pain, redness, and swelling that resolved within a week?
Patient_Yoruba: Irora tó le, pupa, àti wiwú wáyé ní ìpín ọwọ́ orùka mi lójijì ní alẹ́. Ó parí ní ọjọ́ márùn-ún láìsí oògùn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Self-resolving acute monoarthritis of the first metatarsophalangeal joint with spontaneous nocturnal onset and resolution within five days, the classic pattern of gouty arthritis requiring serum uric acid, joint aspiration for crystals, and dietary modification.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any small, painful red bumps or nodules under your skin near your joints?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo rí àwọn èso kéékèéké tó ń dùn ní ìsàlẹ̀ awọ ara mi ní àtẹ̀lẹ ìpín ọwọ́ mi àti ẹ̀hin orúkún mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Subcutaneous nodules at pressure points over joint extensor surfaces, highly characteristic of rheumatoid arthritis with seropositive disease, indicating significant joint damage risk and requiring aggressive disease-modifying antirheumatic drug therapy.

Doctor_English: Have you been experiencing pain in multiple joints that moves from one joint to another over days?
Patient_Yoruba: Irora máa ń wáyé ní ìpín ọwọ́ mi, lẹ́yìn ọjọ́ méjì ó máa ń lọ sí orúkún, lẹ́yìn náà sí ìbàdí. Ó ń yí padà ní ìgbà-ìgbà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Migratory polyarthritis moving sequentially between joints over days, consistent with acute rheumatic fever, gonococcal arthritis, or viral arthritis, each requiring distinct investigation including ASOT, throat swab, and STI screen.

Doctor_English: Have you been having repeated episodes of a sore throat, and how many times in the past year?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ti ní ọfun tó ń dùn lẹ́ẹ̀mẹ́fà ní ọdún yìí. Àwọn mẹ́ta nínú wọn ń bani lóru tó ń dẹ́kun mi lọ iṣẹ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Six episodes of pharyngitis in one year with three causing work absence, meeting the Paradise criteria for recurrent tonsillitis and warranting ENT referral for tonsillectomy assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any change in your voice that has been getting progressively worse over months?
Patient_Yoruba: Ohùn mi ti di bẹ̀rẹ̀ díẹ̀ díẹ̀ fún oṣù mẹ́fà. Àwọn ọ̀rẹ́ mi sọ pé wọn kò gbọ́ mi dáradára mọ́ ní àpéjọ.
Clinical_Translation_English: Progressive dysphonia over six months causing communicative difficulty, requiring urgent laryngoscopy to exclude laryngeal carcinoma, vocal cord palsy, or benign vocal cord pathology such as nodules or polyps.

Doctor_English: Have you been having nosebleeds that are difficult to stop, and how long do they usually last?
Patient_Yoruba: Ẹjẹ̀ máa ń jáde nínú imu mi lẹ́ẹ̀mejì ní ọ̀sẹ̀, ó máa ń pẹ́ fún ìṣẹ́jú mẹ́ẹ̀ẹ́dógójì kí ó tó dúró.
Clinical_Translation_English: Recurrent epistaxis twice weekly with prolonged duration of forty-five minutes, requiring anterior rhinoscopy to identify a Kiesselbach's plexus source, alongside coagulation screen and blood pressure measurement to exclude systemic causes.

Doctor_English: Have you had any ringing in your ears alongside dizziness and hearing loss all happening at the same time?
Patient_Yoruba: Etí òsì mi ń rò, ori mi ń yípo, àti gbígbọ́ mi ti dínkù ní ẹ̀gbẹ́ kan náà. Wọ́n máa ń wáyé papọ̀ fún wákàtí díẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: The Ménière's triad of unilateral tinnitus, episodic vertigo, and sensorineural hearing loss on the same side occurring together, pathognomonic of Ménière's disease requiring audiometry, electrocochleography, and ENT assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you been snoring and stopping breathing at night, and do you fall asleep easily during the day?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìyàwó mi sọ pé ìrọ̀lẹ́ mi gbóná àti mo máa ń dáwọ mí dúró. Ní ọ̀sán, mo máa ń sùn nígbà tí mo bá jókòó.
Clinical_Translation_English: Obstructive sleep apnoea with witnessed apnoeas, loud snoring, and excessive daytime somnolence, the classic STOP-BANG positive triad requiring overnight polysomnography and CPAP therapy initiation.

Doctor_English: Have you had any difficulty hearing in noisy environments, or do people have to repeat themselves often?
Patient_Yoruba: Ní àyíká tó ń ariwo, mi ò máa ń gbọ́ ọ̀rọ̀ dáradára. Àwọn ọ̀rẹ́ mi máa ń tún ọ̀rọ̀ sọ fún mi ní ìgbà-ìgbà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Difficulty hearing speech in noise with frequent requests for repetition, suggesting mild to moderate sensorineural hearing loss, possibly age-related or noise-induced, requiring pure tone audiometry for quantification and hearing aid assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any lump or swelling in front of your ear that is painful when you eat?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìwọ̀n kan wà níwájú etí mi ọ̀tún tó ń dùn nígbà tí mo bá jẹun tàbí ṣí ẹnu mi. Ó ti pọ̀ sí i ní oṣù kan.
Clinical_Translation_English: Painful parotid swelling exacerbated by eating due to salivary flow stimulation, consistent with parotitis from bacterial infection, salivary duct calculus, or parotid neoplasm requiring ultrasound and, if malignancy is suspected, fine needle aspiration cytology.

Doctor_English: Have you been having repeated ear infections since childhood, and has your hearing been affected?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ti máa ń ní àkóràn etí láti ìgbà tí mo wà lọ́mọdé. Ìgbà díẹ̀, omi máa ń jáde nínú etí mi. Gbígbọ́ mi kò dára bíi ìgbà àárọ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Recurrent otitis media since childhood with chronic suppurative discharge and progressive hearing deterioration, indicating chronic suppurative otitis media with possible cholesteatoma requiring CT temporal bone and ENT surgical assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you been having severe facial pain or pressure around your cheeks and forehead that is worse when bending forward?
Patient_Yoruba: Ẹrẹ̀kẹ́ mi àti iwájú orí mi ń dùn àti kún bíi ìtẹ̀wọ́. Ó máa ń burú sí i nígbà tí mo bá tẹ̀ orí mi sísàlẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Facial pressure pain with frontal and maxillary predominance worsening on forward flexion of the head, the classic presentation of acute sinusitis requiring clinical assessment and consideration of antibiotics if bacterial superinfection is suspected.

Doctor_English: Have you had any difficulty breathing through your nose on one side for as long as you can remember?
Patient_Yoruba: Ẹ̀gbẹ́ ọ̀tún imu mi kò ṣòfo rárá láti ìgbà mo wà lọ́mọdé. Mo máa ń mí nípasẹ̀ ẹnu mi ní alẹ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Lifelong unilateral nasal obstruction with obligate mouth breathing at night, consistent with a deviated nasal septum or congenital choanal stenosis requiring nasal endoscopy and consideration of surgical correction.

Doctor_English: Have you been told that your illness cannot be cured, and has anyone spoken to you about what you would like to happen?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà sọ fún mi pé àrùn mi kò lè sàn mọ́. Kò sí ẹni tó sọ̀rọ̀ pẹ̀lú mi nípa ohun tí mo fẹ́ kí wọ́n ṣe.
Clinical_Translation_English: Terminal diagnosis without documented advance care planning discussion, representing a critical gap in palliative care that must be addressed through a sensitive goals-of-care conversation to establish the patient's wishes, values, and priorities.

Doctor_English: Are you in pain most of the time, and is your current pain medication giving you enough relief?
Patient_Yoruba: Irora wà fún mi fún ọjọ́-ọjọ́. Oògùn tí wọ́n fún mi ń mú u yẹ̀ díẹ̀ ṣùgbọ́n kò lọ pátápátá. Mo máa ń jí ní alẹ́ nítorí rẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Inadequate analgesia with nocturnal pain breakthrough despite current medication, indicating the need for analgesic ladder reassessment, consideration of opioid titration, and palliative care team involvement for optimal pain management.

Doctor_English: Have you thought about where you would like to be cared for if your illness gets much worse?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo fẹ́ kú nínú ilé mi pẹ̀lú àwọn ẹbí mi, kì í ṣe nínú ilé ìwòsàn. Ṣùgbọ́n mi ò mọ̀ bóyá yóò ṣeéṣe.
Clinical_Translation_English: Documented preference for home death with family but uncertainty about feasibility, requiring a palliative care home assessment, carer support planning, community nursing input, and liaison with the family regarding symptom management at home.

Doctor_English: Have you had any difficulty breathing or feeling like you cannot get enough air, even when you are resting?
Patient_Yoruba: Mí mi kúrú ní ìgbà tí mo bá jókòó àti nígbà tí mo bá dùbúlẹ̀. Ó ń bani lóru púpọ̀. Mo máa ń bẹ̀rù ní alẹ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Refractory dyspnoea at rest causing significant distress and nocturnal fear in a palliative patient, requiring low-dose opioids for breathlessness palliation, benzodiazepines for anxiety, and a fan directed at the face to stimulate trigeminal airflow receptors.

Doctor_English: Have you been experiencing nausea that stops you from eating, and have you vomited frequently?
Patient_Yoruba: Ríru ń dẹ́kun mi láti jẹun fún ọjọ́ mẹ́wàá. Mo ègbọn lẹ́ẹ̀mẹ́fà ní ọjọ́ kan. Ara mi ti dín kù gan-an.
Clinical_Translation_English: Intractable nausea with frequent vomiting causing significant weight loss and nutritional deterioration in a palliative patient, requiring antiemetic optimisation, review of opioid-induced nausea, and consideration of subcutaneous medication delivery if oral route is compromised.

Doctor_English: Is there anything specific you are worried about as your illness progresses, such as pain or being a burden?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo bẹ̀rù irora tó ń bọ̀. Mo tún bẹ̀rù pé ìdílé mi máa ń ṣàánú mi. Mi ò fẹ́ jẹ́ ẹrù fún wọn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Fear of uncontrolled pain and caregiver burden in a terminally ill patient, common existential concerns in palliative care requiring psychological support, clear communication about symptom management capabilities, and carer wellbeing assessment.

Doctor_English: Have your religious or spiritual beliefs been a source of comfort, or have they been a source of struggle during this illness?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìgbàgbọ́ mi ń ràn mí lọ́wọ́ ní ìgbà-ìgbà ṣùgbọ́n ní ìgbà mìíràn mo ní ìbéèrè tó ń ṣeni lẹ́nu nípa ìdí tí Ọlọ́run fi jẹ́ kí èyí ṣẹlẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Spiritual ambivalence with faith as both a coping resource and a source of existential questioning, indicating the need for chaplaincy or spiritual care referral alongside standard palliative support in the Nigerian sociocultural context.

Doctor_English: Have you been able to say the things you want to say to your family, and is there anything left unresolved?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ti sọ fún àwọn ọmọ mi pé mo nífẹ̀ẹ́ wọn. Ṣùgbọ́n ọmọ ẹgbọ́n mi àti èmi ti ní ìforígbárí fún ọdún mẹ́wàá tí a kò tíì yanjú.
Clinical_Translation_English: Completed emotional disclosure to children with an unresolved sibling estrangement of ten years constituting unfinished relational business, a common source of end-of-life distress amenable to family mediation or chaplaincy facilitated reconciliation.

Doctor_English: Have you been experiencing any hot flushes, vaginal dryness, or difficulty sleeping since your periods stopped?
Patient_Yoruba: Lẹ́yìn tí oṣù mi dáwọ̀ wáyé ní ọdún kan sẹ́yìn, ìgbóná ń wáyé fún mi ní ìgbà-ìgbà, ìbò mi ti gbẹ, àti sùn mi ti ṣòro.
Clinical_Translation_English: Postmenopausal vasomotor symptoms with genitourinary syndrome of menopause including vaginal atrophy and insomnia, requiring hormone replacement therapy discussion including risks and benefits in the context of the patient's individual cardiovascular and breast cancer risk profile.

Doctor_English: Have you had a mammogram done, and when was the last one?
Patient_Yoruba: Mi ò tíì ṣe ìdánwò ọmú kankan rí. Mi ò mọ̀ pé àwọn obìnrin bíi mi nílò rẹ̀. Dọ́kítà kò sọ fún mi rí.
Clinical_Translation_English: No history of mammographic screening in a woman of screening age without clinician-initiated referral, indicating a gap in breast cancer surveillance requiring immediate scheduling and patient education on the importance of routine mammography.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any pelvic pain or pressure that is worse during your period?
Patient_Yoruba: Irora ìdọ̀tí mi máa ń bani lóru gan-an ní àkókò oṣù mi. Ó ń tàn sínú ẹ̀hin mi àti ẹsẹ̀ mi. Mo ti lo oògùn irora lọ́pọ̀lọpọ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Severe cyclical pelvic pain radiating to the back and lower limbs with analgesic dependence, the characteristic symptom complex of endometriosis requiring diagnostic laparoscopy and hormonal or surgical management.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any nipple discharge, and what colour is it?
Patient_Yoruba: Omi ti bẹ̀rẹ̀ síí jáde nínú ọmú mi ọ̀tún lẹ́ẹ̀mẹ́ta, àwọ̀ funfun-líle. Mi ò lóyún àti kò ń fun ọmọ ọmu.
Clinical_Translation_English: Spontaneous unilateral nipple discharge in a non-lactating, non-pregnant woman, requiring prolactin level measurement to exclude hyperprolactinaemia, alongside ductoscopy or mammography to exclude intraductal papilloma or malignancy.

Doctor_English: Have you had a hysterectomy before, and do you know why it was done?
Patient_Yoruba: A yọ ìkùn ọmọ mi ní ọdún mẹ́fà sẹ́yìn nítorí fibroid tó pọ̀. Àwọn ẹ̀yìn mi ṣì wà nínú ara mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Previous hysterectomy for symptomatic fibroids with ovarian conservation, relevant to current HRT eligibility, cardiovascular risk profile, and the fact that cervical screening is no longer required while ovarian cancer surveillance remains important.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any urinary leakage during intercourse or immediately after?
Patient_Yoruba: Ito máa ń jáde ní ìgbà ìbálòpọ̀ àti lẹ́yìn rẹ̀ ní ìgbà-ìgbà. Ó ń jẹ ẹlẹ́yà mi àti ó ti ń dẹ́kun ìbálòpọ̀ wa.
Clinical_Translation_English: Coital urinary incontinence causing sexual avoidance and relationship strain, indicating stress or mixed urinary incontinence requiring pelvic floor physiotherapy, urodynamic assessment, and, if conservative measures fail, surgical intervention.

Doctor_English: Have you had any abnormal smear results before, and were you followed up appropriately?
Patient_Yoruba: Ní ọdún mẹ́ta sẹ́yìn, a sọ fún mi pé àbájáde ìdánwò ìkùn mi kò dára. A pè mi padà ṣùgbọ́n mi ò lọ nítorí àyà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Abnormal cervical cytology three years ago with failure to attend colposcopy follow-up due to fear, representing a significant risk for undetected cervical intraepithelial neoplasia progression, requiring immediate rescreening and colposcopy referral.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any changes in your vulval skin — itching, thickening, or white discolouration?
Patient_Yoruba: Awọ ara ní àgbègbè ìbò mi ti yí padà si funfun àti kúnjú. Ín-ín ń ṣe mí lọ́pọ̀lọpọ̀ tó mú kí n gbá ara mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Vulval skin changes with leucoplakia and thickening causing intractable pruritus, consistent with lichen sclerosus — a condition associated with vulval squamous cell carcinoma risk — requiring vulvoscopy, biopsy, and potent topical corticosteroid treatment.

Doctor_English: Have you been using any vaginal douches or perfumed products inside the vagina?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo máa ń fọ ìbò mi pẹ̀lú omi tó ní òórùn lọ́pọ̀lọpọ̀ nítorí mo gbà gbọ́ pé ó máa ń mú u mọ́ jù. Mo ṣe bẹ́ẹ̀ ní ọ̀sẹ̀ kan.
Clinical_Translation_English: Weekly intravaginal douching with perfumed products based on a misconception about vaginal hygiene, a practice that disrupts the normal Lactobacillus-dominant vaginal microbiome and predisposes to bacterial vaginosis, candidiasis, and ascending pelvic infection.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any pain during examination or when using tampons?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìdánwò ìbò mi máa ń ṣe mí irora gan-an. Mo máa ń bẹ̀rù rẹ̀ dé ìwọn tí mo máa ń yẹra fún ìbẹ̀wò dọ́kítà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Significant examination-related pain and medical avoidance behaviour consistent with vaginismus or vulvodynia, requiring a sensitive, phased examination approach, pelvic floor physiotherapy, and psychological support to address pain-avoidance cycles.

Doctor_English: Do you work with any chemicals, solvents, or pesticides, and do you use protective equipment?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ń lo àwọn kẹ́míkà fún iṣẹ́ oko mi lọ́pọ̀lọpọ̀. Mi ò máa ń wọ ibọwọ́ tàbí ohun ìdáàbòbò. Ó gbóná nínú aṣọ wọnú.
Clinical_Translation_English: Chronic unprotected agricultural pesticide exposure without personal protective equipment, creating risk for organophosphate toxicity, peripheral neuropathy, and long-term carcinogenesis requiring occupational health assessment and agricultural safety education.

Doctor_English: Have you been exposed to loud noise at work for many years, and do you wear ear protection?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ṣiṣẹ́ ní ilé iṣẹ́ tó ń ṣe irin fún ọdún mẹ́wàá. Ìró gbóná. Mi ò máa ń wọ ohun ìdáàbòbò etí.
Clinical_Translation_English: Ten years of occupational noise exposure in a metal fabrication facility without hearing protection, a major cause of noise-induced sensorineural hearing loss requiring audiometric surveillance and immediate implementation of hearing conservation measures.

Doctor_English: Have you been having skin rashes specifically on your hands that are worse at work and better on weekends?
Patient_Yoruba: Àpá tó ń yọ wà lórí ọwọ́ mi méjèéjì. Ó máa ń burú ní àwọn ọjọ́ ìkọ́lé àti yẹ̀ ní ìsinmi. Mo ń ṣiṣẹ́ ní ọjà ẹja.
Clinical_Translation_English: Occupational contact dermatitis with characteristic work-related exacerbation and weekend improvement in a fish market worker, indicating exposure to fish proteins, preservatives, or cleaning agents requiring patch testing and allergen identification.

Doctor_English: Have you been sitting at a computer for most of the working day, and do you have neck or wrist pain?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo jókòó lórí kọ̀mpútà fún wákàtí mẹ́wàá ní ọjọ́ kan. Ọrùn mi àti ọwọ́ mi ń dùn fún oṣù mẹ́fà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Chronic neck pain and wrist pain secondary to prolonged computer use without ergonomic adjustment, consistent with work-related musculoskeletal disorder requiring workstation assessment, physiotherapy, and micro-break education.

Doctor_English: Have you been exposed to asbestos, silica dust, or any other industrial mineral dusts at work?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ṣiṣẹ́ ní ilé iṣẹ́ tó ń kọ́ ile fún ọdún mẹ́ẹ̀ẹ́dọ́gbọ̀n. Erùpẹ̀ ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ wà níbẹ̀, a kò sì máa ń wọ ohun ìdáàbòbò.
Clinical_Translation_English: Twenty-five years of construction dust exposure without respiratory protection, creating significant cumulative risk for silicosis, asbestosis, mesothelioma, and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, requiring chest CT and pulmonary function testing.

Doctor_English: Have you been involved in any workplace accidents or injuries, and were they reported formally?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo gba ọgbẹ́ lórí iṣẹ́ ní oṣù méjì sẹ́yìn nígbà tí ẹrọ kan fi ọwọ́ mi pẹ̀ mọ́. A kò ṣe ìjábọ̀ rẹ̀ nítorí olórí iṣẹ́ mi sọ pé kò pọ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Unreported machinery-related hand crush injury two months ago at employer's direction, indicating a workplace safety violation, potential medico-legal implications, and the patient's right to formal incident reporting and workers' compensation assessment.

Doctor_English: Do you stand for most of your working day, and have you been having leg pain or varicose veins?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo dúró fún wákàtí mẹ́jọ ní ọjọ́ kan nínú iṣẹ́ mi. Àwọn iṣan ọ̀nà ẹjẹ̀ tó wú ti fara hàn lórí ẹsẹ̀ mi méjèéjì.
Clinical_Translation_English: Prolonged occupational standing with bilateral varicose vein development, a common consequence of chronic venous insufficiency from gravitational venous pooling, requiring duplex ultrasound and compression hosiery prescription.

Doctor_English: Have you been working night shifts, and has this affected your sleep, appetite, or mood?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo máa ń ṣiṣẹ́ alẹ́ fún ọdún méjì. Ìsùn mi ti ṣòro, ìfẹ́kúfẹ̀ẹ́ mi ti yí padà, àti inú mi máa ń bí mi ni ìgbà-ìgbà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Chronic shift work disorder with sleep disturbance, appetite dysregulation, and irritability after two years of night shift work, associated with increased metabolic syndrome, cardiovascular disease, and mental health risk requiring occupational health intervention.

Doctor_English: Have you been involved in any work requiring heavy lifting, and have you had any back injuries?
Patient_Yoruba: Iṣẹ́ mi nílò gbígbe àwọn àpótí tó wúwo lọ́pọ̀lọpọ̀ ní ọjọ́ kan. Mo gba ọgbẹ́ ẹ̀hin lẹ́ẹ̀mẹ́ta ní ọdún méjì.
Clinical_Translation_English: Recurrent occupational back injuries from manual handling over two years without formal lifting technique training, indicating a preventable workplace injury pattern requiring manual handling assessment, ergonomic intervention, and physiotherapy.

Doctor_English: Have you been exposed to radiation at work, and are you monitored with a dosimeter badge?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ṣiṣẹ́ ní ẹ̀ka ìdánwò X-ray ní ilé ìwòsàn fún ọdún mẹ́fà. Àmì dosimeter ni wọ́n fún mi ṣùgbọ́n mi ò mọ̀ ohun tó ń ṣe.
Clinical_Translation_English: Six years of occupational ionising radiation exposure with dosimetry badge worn but results not communicated to the worker, indicating a radiation safety governance failure requiring cumulative dose review, health surveillance, and radiation protection training.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any progressive swelling of your leg or arm that has been getting larger over months?
Patient_Yoruba: Ẹsẹ̀ ọsì mi ti ń pọ̀ sí i díẹ̀díẹ̀ fún oṣù mẹ́fà. Ó ti pọ̀ tó jẹ́ kí bàtà mi má a jẹ mí. Kò ń dùn rárá.
Clinical_Translation_English: Progressive painless unilateral lower limb lymphoedema over six months in a patient from a malaria-endemic region, consistent with lymphatic filariasis caused by Wuchereria bancrofti, requiring nocturnal blood film for microfilaria and antifilarial therapy.

Doctor_English: Have you ever had a swelling under your skin that moves around, or have you seen a worm moving under the skin?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo rí nǹkan tó ń rìn sínú awọ ara mi ní ìgbà-ìgbà. Ó ń dùn díẹ̀ tó sì ń rìn lọ nígbà tí a bá gbìyànjú láti mú u.
Clinical_Translation_English: Migratory subcutaneous swelling with a sensation of movement under the skin, pathognomonic of loiasis caused by Loa loa, a filarial parasite endemic in Central and West Africa transmitted by deer fly bites, requiring Giemsa-stained blood film.
