Clinical_Translation_English: Low back pain radiating through the buttock and down the leg in the sciatic nerve distribution, consistent with lumbar disc herniation at L4/L5 or L5/S1 compressing the nerve root, requiring MRI lumbar spine and physiotherapy assessment.

Doctor_English: Is the breast lump in the upper outer area, near the nipple, or in the armpit?
Patient_Yoruba: Èso náà wà ní ìhà ìgbẹ́yìn ìsàlẹ̀ ọmú mi òsì, jìnnà sí ọ̀tọ̀ ọmú. Ko wà ní àgbègbè ẹ̀gbẹ̀ ọwọ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Lower outer quadrant breast lump, a less common location than the upper outer quadrant where most breast cancers occur, but requiring the same urgent triple assessment protocol with clinical examination, ultrasound or mammography, and core biopsy.

Doctor_English: Have you been told to fast before this blood test, and how many hours have you been without food?
Patient_Yoruba: Wọ́n sọ fún mi pé kí n má jẹun ṣáájú ìdánwò. Mo ti àwẹ̀ fún wákàtí mẹ́wàá. Omi péré ni mo mu.
Clinical_Translation_English: Appropriate ten-hour fast prior to fasting lipid profile and glucose testing, confirming valid pre-analytical conditions for accurate metabolic panel interpretation and cardiovascular risk assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you brought all your medications with you today so we can review what you are taking?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo mú àwọn oògùn mi wá, ṣùgbọ́n àwọn méjì tí mi ò mọ̀ orúkọ wọn. Dọ́kítà mìíràn ni ó fún mi ní wọn ní ilé ìwòsàn àdúgbò.
Clinical_Translation_English: Incomplete medication knowledge with two unidentified drugs from an external prescriber, highlighting polypharmacy risk and the importance of brown bag medication review to identify duplications, interactions, and inappropriate prescribing.

Doctor_English: Have you had any reaction to latex gloves or rubber products, such as balloons or condoms?
Patient_Yoruba: Nígbà tí wọ́n bá fi ibọwọ́ rọ́bà kan mi ní ilé ìwòsàn, ọwọ́ mi máa ń yọ àti bínú. Mo ti gbàgbé láti sọ fún dọ́kítà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Undisclosed latex allergy with prior contact urticaria requiring documentation before any surgical procedure, gynaecological examination, or dental procedure to prevent intraoperative anaphylaxis and ensure latex-free equipment preparation.

Doctor_English: Have you signed a consent form for this procedure, and do you fully understand what is going to happen?
Patient_Yoruba: Wọ́n fún mi ní fọ́ọ̀mù kan láti fọwọ́ sí. Mo fọwọ́ sí i ṣùgbọ́n mi ò kà á nítorí ọ̀rọ̀ Gẹ̀ẹ́sì ṣòro fún mi. Mi ò tíì béèrè ìbéèrè kankan.
Clinical_Translation_English: Consent obtained without adequate comprehension due to language barrier and health literacy limitation, constituting invalid informed consent and requiring a Yoruba-language explanation of the procedure, risks, benefits, and alternatives before proceeding.

Doctor_English: Have you eaten or drunk anything since midnight, including water, sweets, or chewing gum?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo mu omi díẹ̀ ní àárọ̀ ìmọ́lẹ̀ nítorí èbì mi pọ̀. Mo tún jẹ ọ̀pẹ̀ kéékèéké ṣáájú kí n tó wá.
Clinical_Translation_English: Pre-operative fasting protocol violation with water and food intake on the morning of surgery, requiring anaesthetic team notification to assess aspiration risk and determine whether to proceed, delay, or modify the anaesthetic technique.

Doctor_English: Have you had any problems with anaesthesia before, such as waking up during an operation or feeling very sick afterwards?
Patient_Yoruba: Ní iṣẹ́ abẹ àkọ́kọ́ mi, wọ́n sọ pé mo jí nígbà tí wọ́n ṣì ń ṣiṣẹ́ lórí mi. Mo ń gbọ́ ohùn wọn ṣùgbọ́n kò lè sọ rẹ̀. Ó jẹ mí ẹ̀rù.
Clinical_Translation_English: Prior intraoperative awareness under anaesthesia constituting a critical anaesthetic history requiring depth of anaesthesia monitoring with BIS or entropy during all subsequent procedures and pre-operative psychological support for anaesthesia-related trauma.

Doctor_English: Have you been told you have a difficult airway or that a previous anaesthetist had trouble putting a breathing tube in?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà àánì ní ilé ìwòsàn mìíràn sọ pé ọ̀nà mímí mi ṣòro. Ó gbìyànjú lẹ́ẹ̀mẹ́fà ṣáájú kí ó tó ṣẹ̀ṣẹ̀ ṣaṣeyọrí.
Clinical_Translation_English: Previously documented difficult intubation requiring six attempts, constituting a critical airway alert requiring advance notification to the anaesthetic team, awake fibreoptic intubation planning, and a difficult airway cart at the bedside before any sedation is administered.

Doctor_English: Have you had a blood transfusion before, and did you have any reaction such as shivering, fever, or rash during or after it?
Patient_Yoruba: A ṣe mi àjẹjẹ̀ lẹ́ẹ̀mejì. Ní ìgbà kejì, mo bẹ̀rẹ̀ ní ìgbọn àti ibà ní ìgbà tí ẹjẹ̀ ń wọ ara mi. Wọ́n dáwọ̀ rẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Previous febrile non-haemolytic transfusion reaction requiring cessation, mandating pre-medication with antipyretics and antihistamines before future transfusions, leukodepleted blood product use, and careful monitoring at the bedside during transfusion.

Doctor_English: Have you been told you have a heart condition that would require antibiotics before dental or surgical procedures?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà ọkàn mi sọ pé mo nílò oògùn àkóràn ṣáájú gbogbo iṣẹ́ abẹ nítorí àrùn àwọ ọkàn mi. Mo gbàgbé láti sọ fún dọ́kítà rẹ̀yìí.
Clinical_Translation_English: Undisclosed cardiac valvular disease requiring antibiotic endocarditis prophylaxis before invasive procedures, a critical safety omission that must be addressed before proceeding with any planned dental or surgical intervention.

Doctor_English: How long have you been waiting to be seen today, and have your symptoms changed since you arrived?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ti dúró fún wákàtí mẹ́ta. Irora àyà mi ti bẹ̀rẹ̀ sí tan sí apá òsì mi lẹ́yìn tí mo dé. Ó le sí i ju ìgbà tí mo wà síta lọ.
Clinical_Translation_English: Symptom deterioration during a three-hour wait with new radiation of chest pain to the left arm, indicating a possible acute coronary syndrome evolution requiring immediate triage escalation to a resuscitation area rather than continued waiting.

Doctor_English: Do you have any advance directive or living will that tells us what treatment you would or would not want?
Patient_Yoruba: Mi ò tíì rò nípa ìyẹn rárá. Mo kò mọ̀ pé ó ṣeéṣe. Ṣùgbọ́n bí mo bá di àìsí, mi ò fẹ́ kí wọ́n máa gbé mi láàárọ̀ pẹ̀lú ẹrọ.
Clinical_Translation_English: No existing advance directive with expressed preference against mechanical life support, requiring formal advance care planning documentation witnessed and recorded in the medical notes before any acute deterioration renders the patient unable to participate.

Doctor_English: Have you brought someone with you today who can help translate or support you during the consultation?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọmọ mi wà pẹ̀lú mi níta. Ó sọ Gẹ̀ẹ́sì dáradára àti mọ̀ àrùn mi. Mo fẹ́ kí ó wọlé pẹ̀lú mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Patient accompanied by a bilingual adult child requesting their presence during consultation, which should be facilitated while ensuring the patient is also asked about any concerns they wish to raise privately, protecting confidentiality within the supported consultation.

Doctor_English: Have you been given a diagnosis today that was unexpected or that you are struggling to understand?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà sọ pé mo ní àrùn jẹjẹrẹ. Mo ti ń gbọ́ ọ̀rọ̀ rẹ̀ ṣùgbọ́n mi ò tíì gbà á. Mo bẹ̀rù pupọ̀ àti mi ò mọ̀ ohun tó máa ṣẹlẹ̀ sí mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Acute psychological response to a new cancer diagnosis with disbelief and fear, requiring a structured breaking bad news approach with adequate time, written information, emotional support, follow-up appointment within forty-eight hours, and oncology nurse specialist contact details.

Doctor_English: Have you been discharged from hospital before and then had to come back within a few days for the same problem?
Patient_Yoruba: Wọ́n jí mi kúrò ní ilé ìwòsàn ní ọjọ́ mẹ́ta sẹ́yìn. Irora mi kò parí, àti báyìí ó ti le sí i. Mo wá padà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Unplanned readmission within three days of discharge with worsening of the original presenting complaint, indicating premature discharge or inadequate treatment, requiring root cause analysis of the discharge decision and escalated management of the current presentation.

Doctor_English: Have you been given a follow-up appointment after today, and do you know what it is for?
Patient_Yoruba: Wọ́n fún mi ní kàdì pẹ̀lú ọjọ́ tó kọjá. Ọjọ́ ìpàdé mi ti kọjá ní ọ̀sẹ̀ méjì. Ko sí ẹni tó pè mi padà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Missed follow-up appointment without recall notification two weeks past the scheduled date, representing a care gap that may result in failure to act on pending investigation results, medication review needs, or disease monitoring, requiring immediate rescheduling and result review.

Doctor_English: Have you ever been admitted to a psychiatric ward, and did you find the experience helpful or harmful?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo wọ ilé ìwòsàn ọpọlọ lẹ́ẹ̀kan ní ọdún mẹ́ta sẹ́yìn. Mo gbà pé ó ràn mí lọ́wọ́ ṣùgbọ́n ìrírí náà ń ṣe mí ìbànújẹ́ nígbà tí mo bá rò nípa rẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Prior inpatient psychiatric admission experienced as helpful but traumatic, relevant to current treatment planning as prior admission may create a barrier to accepting voluntary inpatient care if needed, requiring trauma-informed approach to psychiatric management.

Doctor_English: Have you ever left hospital against medical advice, and what were the circumstances?
Patient_Yoruba: Bẹ́ẹ̀ ni, mo jáde kúrò ilé ìwòsàn ní àárọ̀ ọjọ́ kan nítorí àwọn ọmọ mi kò ní ẹni tó máa tọ́jú wọn. Dọ́kítà sọ pé kò wùn ú.
Clinical_Translation_English: Discharge against medical advice due to childcare responsibilities rather than disagreement with treatment, a social barrier to care completion requiring social work referral, childcare support identification, and non-judgmental re-engagement to address the underlying cause.

Doctor_English: Have you been told your test result is abnormal and been referred somewhere, but you have not gone yet?
Patient_Yoruba: Wọ́n fún mi ní lẹ́tà ìtọ́ka sí ìwé ọpọlọ ní ọdún kan sẹ́yìn. Mi ò lọ rárá. Mo gbà pé yóò dára fúnra rẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Unfollowed psychiatric referral one year ago for an abnormal result, representing a significant care gap where the underlying condition has been unmanaged, requiring understanding of the barriers to attendance and facilitated re-referral with support.

Doctor_English: Do you have a medical alert bracelet or card for any serious condition, and have you brought it today?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ní ìwé tó sọ pé mo ní àtọ̀gbẹ̀ àti àléèjì pẹ̀nísílíìnì ṣùgbọ́n mo gbàgbé rẹ̀ sílẹ̀. Mo máa ń gbàgbé rẹ̀ ní ìgbà-ìgbà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Medical alert card for diabetes and penicillin allergy not carried to the appointment, creating a risk for inadvertent beta-lactam prescribing in an emergency when the patient may be unable to communicate their allergy, reinforcing the importance of MedicAlert documentation in hospital records.

Doctor_English: Have you had your height and weight measured recently, and do you know your body mass index?
Patient_Yoruba: Wọ́n wọn mi ní ọdún kan sẹ́yìn. Dọ́kítà sọ pé mo wúwo jù ṣùgbọ́n kò sọ iye rẹ̀ tàbí ohun tó túmọ̀ sí.
Clinical_Translation_English: Obesity identified one year ago without BMI communication or structured weight management referral, a missed preventive opportunity requiring BMI recalculation, waist circumference measurement, and referral to a structured weight management programme.

Doctor_English: Have you been seen by a specialist before for this condition, and were you given any treatment plan?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo lọ sí dọ́kítà àgbà fún àrùn ẹ̀gbẹ̀ mi ní ọdún méjì sẹ́yìn. Ó fún mi ní ètò ìtọ́jú ṣùgbọ́n mi ò tẹ̀lé e.
Clinical_Translation_English: Specialist-devised management plan from two years ago not adhered to by the patient, requiring exploration of the barriers to adherence — which may include cost, side effects, health beliefs, or complexity — before repeating the referral cycle.

Doctor_English: Have you been told you need a joint replacement, and what has stopped you from having the operation?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà sọ pé a nílò láti rọ́pò ìpín orúkún mi fún ọdún kan ṣùgbọ́n mi ò fẹ́ iṣẹ́ abẹ. Mo bẹ̀rù iṣẹ́ abẹ àti mo gbà pé ìdárayá yóò ràn mí lọ́wọ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Declined joint replacement surgery due to surgical fear and belief in conservative management, requiring a structured shared decision-making conversation addressing surgical risks and benefits, anaesthetic options, and the comparative evidence between surgical and conservative management.

Doctor_English: Have you ever had a serious infection after a medical procedure, such as wound infection or sepsis?
Patient_Yoruba: Lẹ́yìn iṣẹ́ abẹ ìdọ̀tí mi ní ọdún méjì sẹ́yìn, ọgbẹ́ mi ń jẹ̀ ẹjẹ̀ fún ọ̀sẹ̀ méjì. Wọ́n sọ pé ó jẹ́ àkóràn. A gbé mi wọ ilé ìwòsàn padà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Post-operative wound infection requiring readmission following a previous abdominal procedure, a relevant surgical history indicating possible methicillin-resistant organism colonisation, immunodeficiency, or diabetes as risk factors requiring MRSA screening before planned surgery.

Doctor_English: Have you recently had a fall at home that you did not tell anyone about because you were embarrassed?
Patient_Yoruba: Bẹ́ẹ̀ ni, mo ṣubú nínú ìwẹ̀ ní ọ̀sẹ̀ mẹ́ta sẹ́yìn. Mo kò sọ fún ẹnikẹ́ni nítorí ìdọ̀tí. Mo dúró lórí ilẹ̀ fún wákàtí méjì kí ó tó lè dìde.
Clinical_Translation_English: Undisclosed fall at home with a two-hour period of being unable to rise, indicating a long lie which is associated with dehydration, pressure injury, hypothermia, and rhabdomyolysis, requiring assessment for these complications alongside formal falls risk evaluation.

Doctor_English: Has anyone spoken to you about what would happen to your care if your heart stopped — would you want resuscitation attempted?
Patient_Yoruba: Kò sí ẹni tó sọ̀rọ̀ pẹ̀lú mi nípa ìyẹn. Ó jẹ́ ìbéèrè tó ṣeni lẹ́nu. Mo fẹ́ gbogbo ohun tí wọ́n lè ṣe láti gba mi là.
Clinical_Translation_English: No prior DNACPR discussion in a patient with serious illness who expresses a wish for full resuscitation, requiring a sensitive goals-of-care conversation to explore this wish in the context of the clinical prognosis, likely outcomes of CPR, and the patient's values and priorities.

Doctor_English: Has the child been immunised against meningitis, and has there been any case in the school recently?
Patient_Yoruba: Àjesara ọpọlọ àárọ̀ kò sí nínú ìwé àjesara ọmọ mi. Ọmọ kan ní kíláàsì rẹ̀ ti ní àrùn tó jọra ní ọ̀sẹ̀ méjì sẹ́yìn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Unvaccinated child with recent school-based meningococcal contact, requiring urgent chemoprophylaxis assessment, immediate vaccination, and parental education on danger signs warranting emergency attendance.

Doctor_English: Is the child passing urine normally, and have you noticed any swelling around the eyes in the morning?
Patient_Yoruba: Ojú ọmọ mi máa ń wú ní àárọ̀ owúrọ̀ fún ọ̀sẹ̀ méjì. Ito rẹ̀ tún ti di rórò. Mo ti rí ẹ̀jẹ̀ nínú rẹ̀ lẹ́ẹ̀kan.
Clinical_Translation_English: Periorbital oedema with haematuria in a child constituting the classic presentation of post-streptococcal glomerulonephritis or nephrotic syndrome, requiring urine protein, creatinine, complement levels, and ASOT.

Doctor_English: Has the child been snoring loudly and mouth breathing, and has the school said they are inattentive?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọmọ mi ń ṣe ìró ìrọ̀lẹ́ gan-an tó ń jí mi dìde. Ó ń mí nípasẹ̀ ẹnu ní ọjọ́-ọjọ́. Olùkọ́ rẹ̀ sọ pé kò ṣe àfikún mọ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Paediatric obstructive sleep apnoea with adenotonsillar hypertrophy causing nocturnal snoring, obligate mouth breathing, and school inattention consistent with sleep-related neurocognitive impairment, requiring tonsillectomy and adenoidectomy assessment.

Doctor_English: Has your child ever had a blood glucose test, and do any of your close relatives have diabetes?
Patient_Yoruba: Bàbá ọmọ mi àti ẹ̀gbọ́n mi méjì ní àtọ̀gbẹ̀. Ọmọ mi kò tíì ṣe ìdánwò suga kankan rí bí ó tilẹ̀ jẹ́ pé ó ti pọ̀ sí i àti ń fẹ omi lọ́pọ̀lọpọ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Paediatric patient with strong family history of type 2 diabetes, weight gain, and polydipsia, requiring fasting glucose, HbA1c, and oral glucose tolerance test to exclude type 2 diabetes in an obese child with multiple risk factors.

Doctor_English: Has the baby been crying inconsolably for more than three hours a day for no apparent reason?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọmọ ikoko mi ń sọkún fún wákàtí mẹ́ta tàbí mẹ́rin ní ọjọ́ kan láìsí ìdí. A ti gbìyànjú gbogbo ohun ṣùgbọ́n kò dáwọ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Infantile colic with excessive inconsolable crying exceeding three hours daily, a diagnosis of exclusion requiring assessment to rule out intussusception, hair tourniquet, corneal abrasion, incarcerated hernia, and non-accidental injury before attributing to functional colic.

Doctor_English: Has the child been having any muscle cramps or twitching, particularly after illness with vomiting and diarrhoea?
Patient_Yoruba: Lẹ́yìn ìgbẹ́ gbuuru àti ègbọn ọmọ mi, iṣan ara rẹ̀ bẹ̀rẹ̀ sí mì. Ó ń sọkún àti ọwọ́ rẹ̀ ń gúnjú ní ìgbà-ìgbà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Post-gastroenteritis muscle cramps and carpopedal spasm indicating electrolyte derangement, particularly hypocalcaemia or hypomagnesaemia secondary to diarrhoeal losses, requiring urgent electrolyte panel and correction.

Doctor_English: Has the child lost a significant amount of weight recently despite eating normally?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọmọ mi jẹun dáradára ṣùgbọ́n ó ti tẹ̀nìí ní oṣù méjì. Ó tún máa ń rẹ̀ àti ní ìbà kéékèéké ní ìgbà-ìgbà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Unexplained weight loss in a child despite adequate oral intake with fatigue and low-grade fever, raising concern for malignancy, tuberculosis, inflammatory bowel disease, or undiagnosed diabetes, requiring thorough systemic evaluation.

Doctor_English: Has your child ever had a seizure related to a high fever before, and has it happened more than once?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọmọ mi ti gbọn nítorí ibà lẹ́ẹ̀mẹ́rin ní ọdún méjì. Lẹ́ẹ̀kan, ó gbọn fún ìṣẹ́jú mẹ́wàá tó ń bani lóru.
Clinical_Translation_English: Recurrent febrile seizures with one prolonged episode exceeding five minutes, meeting criteria for complex febrile seizures requiring EEG, MRI brain, and paediatric neurology assessment to exclude structural pathology and determine anticonvulsant prophylaxis need.

Doctor_English: Does the child frequently complain of growing pains at night in their legs, and does rubbing help?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọmọ mi máa ń ji ní alẹ́ ó ń sọkún pé ẹsẹ̀ rẹ̀ ń dùn. Nígbà tí mo bá fọwọ́ pa ẹsẹ̀ rẹ̀, ó máa ń dára. Ko ń wáyé ní ọ̀sán.
Clinical_Translation_English: Bilateral nocturnal leg pain relieved by massage in a school-aged child without daytime symptoms, consistent with benign growing pains, but requiring clinical assessment to exclude leukaemia, juvenile arthritis, or stress fracture before this diagnosis is accepted.

Doctor_English: Has the child had an eye test at school, and were they told they need glasses or that one eye is lazy?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìdánwò ojú ní ilé ẹ̀kọ́ fihàn pé ojú ọsì ọmọ mi àìlera. Wọ́n fún wa ní lẹ́tà ṣùgbọ́n a kò lọ dọ́kítà ojú.
Clinical_Translation_English: School vision screening identifying unilateral visual impairment with failure to attend ophthalmological follow-up, creating risk for amblyopia from untreated refractive error, strabismus, or anisometropia during the critical developmental window requiring immediate orthoptic assessment.

Doctor_English: Has the child been having difficulty reading or keeping up with schoolwork since starting school?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọmọ mi ń ṣàkíyèsí ìkọ̀wé àti kíkà lórí. Ó ń lo ìgbà pípẹ́ ju àwọn ọmọ mìíràn lọ ṣùgbọ́n kò máa ń parí. Wọ́n sọ pé ó ń yẹra fún kíkà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Learning difficulty with disproportionate effort required for reading, avoidance behaviour, and failure to complete written work, consistent with dyslexia or a specific learning disorder, requiring psychoeducational assessment and individualised educational support.

Doctor_English: Has anyone told you that your child holds a pencil or scissors differently from other children?
Patient_Yoruba: Olùkọ́ sọ pé ọmọ mi ń mu ìpẹ̀ àti ọbẹ gígé ní àwọn ọwọ́ méjèéjì ní ìgbà-ìgbà. Ó ṣì ń lo ọwọ́ òsì àti ọ̀tún lẹ́ẹ̀kọ̀ọ̀kan.
Clinical_Translation_English: Mixed handedness with non-dominant pencil grip noted by teachers, potentially indicating an underlying fine motor developmental disorder, mild hemiplegia, or developmental coordination disorder requiring occupational therapy and neurological assessment.

Doctor_English: Has the child been unusually thirsty and urinating frequently, and has there been any bedwetting recently?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọmọ mi fẹ omi tó pọ̀ gan-an fún ọ̀sẹ̀ méjì. Ó ń ṣe ito lọ́pọ̀lọpọ̀ àti sọ ito nínú ibùsùn lẹ́ẹ̀mẹ́fà ní ọ̀sẹ̀ kan.
Clinical_Translation_English: The triad of polydipsia, polyuria, and secondary enuresis over two weeks in a child is a type 1 diabetes presentation until proven otherwise, requiring immediate capillary glucose measurement and urgent paediatric admission for fluid and insulin management.

Doctor_English: Has the child had any episodes of pale skin, fast breathing, and extreme tiredness that resolved on their own?
Patient_Yoruba: Lẹ́ẹ̀mẹ́ta ní ọdún yìí, ọmọ mi di funfun, mí rẹ̀ yára, ó sì di àárẹ̀ gan-an. Ó máa ń dára lẹ́yìn wákàtí díẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Recurrent episodes of pallor, tachypnoea, and profound fatigue with spontaneous resolution in a child, consistent with haemolytic crises in sickle cell disease or G6PD deficiency, requiring haemoglobin electrophoresis and G6PD assay.

Doctor_English: Has the child been having any involuntary eye movements, unusual posturing, or episodes of stiffening?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọmọ mi máa ń da ara rẹ̀ bíi ìgbà-ìgbà, ojú rẹ̀ ń wo ní ẹ̀gbẹ́ kan. Ó ń dúró fún ìṣẹ́jú díẹ̀ lẹ́yìn náà ó máa ń padà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Tonic eye deviation with abnormal posturing and brief episodes of altered awareness, consistent with focal seizures from a cortical epileptogenic focus, requiring EEG, MRI brain, and paediatric neurology review.

Doctor_English: Have you had any difficulty getting or maintaining an erection for more than three months?
Patient_Yoruba: Fún oṣù mẹ́fà, mo ti ń rí ìṣòro ní ìbálòpọ̀. Ó ń ṣe mí ìbànújẹ́ àti ó ti bẹ̀rẹ̀ sí nípa ìbálòpọ̀ wa.
Clinical_Translation_English: Erectile dysfunction for six months with relationship impact, requiring assessment for vascular, neurological, hormonal, and psychological aetiologies including testosterone, lipid profile, fasting glucose, blood pressure, and validated IIEF questionnaire scoring.

Doctor_English: Have you had any abnormal bleeding between your periods or after sexual intercourse?
Patient_Yoruba: Ẹjẹ̀ máa ń jáde lẹ́yìn ìbálòpọ̀ fún oṣù mẹ́fà. Ó kéré ṣùgbọ́n ó ń ṣẹlẹ̀ gbogbo ìgbà. Mo bẹ̀rù.
Clinical_Translation_English: Post-coital bleeding for six months, a red flag symptom for cervical carcinoma or severe cervicitis, requiring urgent speculum examination, cervical smear if overdue, biopsy of any visible lesion, and STI screening.

Doctor_English: Have you been told you have polycystic ovaries, and are you trying to manage your weight or hormones?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà ojú omi fihàn pé ẹ̀yìn mi ní ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ àpò omi kéékèéké. Mo ní àìdára oṣù àti irun orí mi ń bọ̀. Mo wúwo jù.
Clinical_Translation_English: Polycystic ovarian syndrome with menstrual irregularity, androgenic alopecia, and obesity, requiring hormonal profile, fasting insulin, lipid screening, and structured lifestyle programme alongside pharmacological management of specific features.

Doctor_English: Have you had any investigations for recurrent miscarriages, and have you been referred to a specialist?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ti sọ oyún nù lẹ́ẹ̀mẹ́ta ní ìdajì àkọ́kọ́ oyún. Dọ́kítà sọ pé a máa ń padà wá ṣùgbọ́n ko tọ́ka wa sí ibi tó péye.
Clinical_Translation_English: Three consecutive first trimester losses without investigation or specialist referral, meeting criteria for recurrent miscarriage requiring antiphospholipid antibody testing, thrombophilia screen, parental karyotype, uterine cavity assessment, and endocrine evaluation.

Doctor_English: Have you experienced any pain or lumps in your testicles, and have you ever checked yourself regularly?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo rí èso kan nínú ìdọ̀tí ọ̀tún mi tí kò ń dùn fún oṣù kan. Mi ò mọ̀ pé àwọn ọkùnrin nílò ṣíṣe ìdánwò ara wọn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Painless testicular lump of one month duration without prior self-examination education, a presentation consistent with testicular malignancy — the most common solid tumour in young men — requiring urgent scrotal ultrasound, tumour markers, and urology referral.

Doctor_English: Have you been having irregular periods since stopping the contraceptive pill, and for how long?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo dáwọ̀ lo ìṣọ́ ìdásí oṣù mẹ́fà sẹ́yìn. Oṣù mi kò padà rárá. Mi ò tíì lóyún àti mi ò ní àìsàn mìíràn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Post-pill amenorrhoea for six months exceeding the expected three-month recovery period, requiring exclusion of premature ovarian insufficiency, hyperprolactinaemia, hypothyroidism, and pregnancy before attributing to post-hormonal suppression.

Doctor_English: Have you had any burning or discharge from your nipple that is not related to breastfeeding?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọmú mi ọ̀tún ń jó mi àti omi àwọ̀ pàálu ń jáde nínú rẹ̀. Mi ò ń fun ọmọ ọmu rárá. Ó bẹ̀rẹ̀ ní oṣù kan sẹ́yìn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Unilateral spontaneous serous nipple discharge with pain in a non-lactating woman, requiring ductoscopy to exclude intraductal papilloma or ductal carcinoma in situ, alongside mammography and clinical examination as part of triple assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you had a hysteroscopy or laparoscopy before to investigate your fertility or pelvic pain?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ní iṣẹ́ kan tí wọ́n wo ìnú ikùn mi ní ọdún méjì sẹ́yìn. Wọ́n sọ pé wọn rí endometriosis ṣùgbọ́n wọ́n kò ṣe nǹkan kankan.
Clinical_Translation_English: Laparoscopically confirmed endometriosis two years ago without definitive treatment, indicating undertreated pelvic pathology contributing to current fertility challenges, requiring medical or surgical management and fertility specialist referral.

Doctor_English: Have you been experiencing any pelvic pressure or a feeling of something falling out of your vagina?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ní ìmọ̀lára bíi ẹni pé nǹkan kan wà ní ẹnu ìbò mi tí ó fẹ́ jáde jáde. Ó máa ń burú nígbà tí mo bá dúró fún ìgbà pípẹ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Vaginal prolapse symptoms with heaviness and sensation of protrusion worsening with prolonged standing, consistent with pelvic organ prolapse requiring POP-Q staging, pelvic floor physiotherapy, and consideration of ring pessary or surgical repair.

Doctor_English: Have you been screened for group B streptococcus in this pregnancy, and do you know what it means?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà sọ pé kí n ṣe ìdánwò kan fún àkóràn ní oṣù tó kẹhìn oyún. Mi ò mọ̀ ohun tó jẹ́ tàbí ìdí tó fi ṣe pàtàkì.
Clinical_Translation_English: Group B Streptococcus screening test recommended without patient education on its significance, requiring explanation of the risk of neonatal GBS sepsis, the role of intrapartum antibiotic prophylaxis, and the importance of communicating carrier status during labour.

Doctor_English: Have you been using mosquito coils or spray indoors at night, and how long has this been going on?
Patient_Yoruba: A máa ń sun àwọn kòkòrò ẹfọn ní ìgbà alẹ́ ní yàrá wa. A ti ń ṣe bẹ́ẹ̀ fún ọdún méjì. Fèrèsé kò sí.
Clinical_Translation_English: Prolonged indoor use of mosquito coils in an unventilated room for two years, creating significant pyrethroid and particulate inhalation exposure associated with respiratory tract irritation, headache, and potential neurotoxicity in children.

Doctor_English: Have you been drinking large amounts of energy drinks or caffeine-containing beverages daily?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo máa ń mu ọ̀pẹ̀lọpọ̀ ìmú agbára fún ìkọ́lé àti ìdánwò mi. Mo máa ń mu ọlọ́pọ̀lọpọ̀ ní ọjọ́ kan bíi mẹ́fà tàbí mẹ́jọ.
Clinical_Translation_English: Excessive energy drink consumption of six to eight cans daily during examination period, creating risk for caffeine toxicity, tachyarrhythmia, hypertension, and anxiety, with potential for caffeine dependence requiring gradual reduction and sleep hygiene intervention.

Doctor_English: Have you been sitting examinations recently, and have you been experiencing significant stress or panic attacks?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìdánwò ilé ẹ̀kọ́ mi ti ń wà fún oṣù méjì. Mo ti ní ìbẹ̀rù tó le lójijì lẹ́ẹ̀mẹ́fà nígbà tí mo ń kọ̀. Ó ń dẹ́kun ìkọ̀ mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Examination-triggered panic attacks with physical symptoms causing assessment disruption, indicating panic disorder in an academic context requiring cognitive-behavioural therapy, breathing techniques, and consideration of examination accommodation support.

Doctor_English: Have you been eating regularly, or have you been skipping meals to study or save money?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo máa ń jẹun lẹ́ẹ̀kan ní ọjọ́ kan ní ìgbà ìdánwò nítorí àkókò kò tó mi. Ní ìgbà mìíràn, owó kò tó fún oúnjẹ méjì.
Clinical_Translation_English: Combination of time pressure and financial constraint causing single meal per day during examination period, creating hypoglycaemia risk, impaired cognitive function, malnutrition, and refeeding syndrome risk if dietary restriction is prolonged.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any chest pain or racing heart after taking a herbal supplement someone recommended?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọ̀rẹ́ mi fún mi ní àwọn ìrọ̀bí egbogi tó sọ pé yóò mú ìmóore mi gbéṣẹ̀. Mo bẹ̀rẹ̀ ní palpitation àti irora àyà lẹ́yìn ọjọ́ méjì.
Clinical_Translation_English: Cardiovascular symptoms developing two days after commencing an unverified herbal supplement for cognitive enhancement, raising concern for stimulant-containing products such as ephedra, guarana, or synephrine causing adrenergic effects, requiring ECG and supplement identification.

Doctor_English: Have you been experiencing any hair loss that you think might be related to stress or your diet?
Patient_Yoruba: Irun mi ti ń bọ̀ lọ́pọ̀lọpọ̀ fún oṣù mẹ́fà. Mo ti padà iṣẹ́ mi àti ibínú ẹbí nígbà yẹn. Oúnjẹ mi tún kò dára.
Clinical_Translation_English: Telogen effluvium with diffuse hair shedding following six months of psychosocial stressors and nutritional inadequacy, a reactive hair loss pattern that typically reverses within six months of addressing the precipitant, requiring thyroid function, ferritin, and zinc levels.

Doctor_English: Have you been experiencing any unusual bleeding from your gums when using a miswak or toothbrush?
Patient_Yoruba: Ẹjẹ̀ máa ń jáde nínú eyín mi nígbà tí mo bá lo ọpẹ tàbí ọṣẹ eyín. Mo ti rò pé ó jẹ́ nítorí tí mo ń gbá pọ̀ jù.
Clinical_Translation_English: Gingival bleeding with both traditional miswak and toothbrush use, a common presenting complaint in Nigerian patients that may indicate gingivitis, periodontitis, thrombocytopaenia, or vitamin C deficiency, requiring dental assessment and haematological screening.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any problems with your vision when reading the Quran or Bible, particularly small print?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ń rí ìṣòro láti kà ìwé ìsìn mi nígbà àárọ̀ owúrọ̀ tàbí alẹ́. Ìmọ̀lẹ̀ tó kéré ń mú ki ó ṣòro sí i. Àwọn ọ̀rọ̀ kéékèéké ń pọ̀ sí i.
Clinical_Translation_English: Difficulty reading small religious text in low light conditions with progressive worsening, consistent with presbyopia from age-related loss of accommodation, requiring refraction for reading glasses alongside assessment for early cataract or macular degeneration.

Doctor_English: Have you been told that you snore very loudly and that your children have started sleeping in another room?
Patient_Yoruba: Àwọn ọmọ mi ti lọ sùn ní yàrá mìíràn nítorí ìró ìrọ̀lẹ́ mi. Ìyàwó mi sọ pé mi ò mí fún àárọ̀ nígbà-ìgbà. Ó bẹ̀rù fún mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Witnessed apnoeas with household-disrupting snoring severe enough to displace children from the bedroom, indicating significant obstructive sleep apnoea requiring overnight pulse oximetry or polysomnography and CPAP therapy assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you been experiencing any electric shock-like pain shooting down your neck when you bend your head forward?
Patient_Yoruba: Nígbà tí mo bá tẹ orí mi wá siwájú, irora bíi ìgbóná onítànná ń rọ láti ọrùn mi sísàlẹ̀ ẹ̀hin mi. Ó bẹ̀rẹ̀ fún oṣù méjì.
Clinical_Translation_English: Lhermitte's sign with electric shock sensation radiating down the spine on neck flexion, a pathognomonic feature of cervical cord demyelination seen in multiple sclerosis or cervical spondylotic myelopathy, requiring urgent MRI cervical spine and neurology referral.

Doctor_English: Has anyone in your community been treating you differently since you received your diagnosis?
Patient_Yoruba: Àwọn ìdílé ọkọ mi ti bẹ̀rẹ̀ sí yà mí sọ̀tọ̀ lẹ́yìn tí wọ́n gbọ́ pé mo ní àrùn mi. Wọ́n máa ń lo àwọn ohun ìjẹun mi tọ̀tọ̀. Ó ń ṣe mí ìbànújẹ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Stigma-related social isolation and separate utensils following a diagnosis, a documented barrier to disclosure and treatment adherence in HIV, tuberculosis, and mental illness that requires community education, psychosocial support, and patient rights advocacy.

Doctor_English: Have you been praying and fasting specifically for healing from this illness, and has it brought you comfort?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ti ń gbàdúrà àti àwẹ̀ fún ìwòsàn lẹ́yìn àrùn mi. Ó ń fun mi ní ìtùnú ṣùgbọ́n àrùn kò ti yẹ̀. Mo ń tẹ̀síwájú mu oògùn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Concurrent spiritual practices and medical treatment in a patient finding comfort in faith while maintaining medication adherence, a positive integration of spiritual and biomedical care that should be supported and documented as part of holistic patient-centred care.

Doctor_English: Have you been told by a family elder that you should not take certain medications because they are forbidden?
Patient_Yoruba: Bàbá ẹ̀gbọ́n mi sọ pé a kò gbọdọ̀ jẹ oògùn kan tí dọ́kítà ń fún wa nítorí ó sọ pé àwọn baba wa kò lo rẹ̀. Mo ti dáwọ̀ mu u.
Clinical_Translation_English: Elder-advised medication refusal based on cultural tradition causing treatment discontinuation, requiring a culturally sensitive conversation involving respected family members, traditional healer engagement where possible, and explanation of the clinical consequences of non-treatment.

Doctor_English: Has your wife or husband been supportive of your decision to seek treatment at the hospital?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọkọ mi kò gba mí láàyè láti wá. Ó sọ pé onísègùn ló dára fún àrùn mi. Mo wá lẹ́hìn mímọ̀ rẹ̀ nítorí irora mi le jù.
Clinical_Translation_English: Partner opposition to hospital attendance with the patient presenting covertly due to pain severity, indicating an intimate partner health control dynamic requiring sensitive documentation and exploration of safety and reproductive autonomy concerns.

Doctor_English: Have you been told your child has autism, and have you been able to access any support services?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà ọmọ sọ pé ọmọ mi ní autism ní ọdún kan sẹ́yìn. A kò rí ẹgbẹ́ ìrànlọ́wọ́ kankan. Ilé ẹ̀kọ́ náà sọ pé ko ní àyè.
Clinical_Translation_English: Autism spectrum disorder diagnosed one year ago without access to support services or inclusive education, indicating a significant gap in disability services in the Nigerian context requiring advocacy, parent training, and referral to available specialist developmental therapy.

Doctor_English: Have you been experiencing discrimination at the hospital — feeling rushed, dismissed, or not listened to?
Patient_Yoruba: Ní ìgbà-ìgbà, mo ń jẹ pé dọ́kítà kò gbọ́ ohun tí mo ń sọ. Mo sì ń jẹ pé nítorí àwọ ara mi tàbí ìsọ̀rọ̀ mi, a kò gbà mi lọ́kàn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Patient-reported experience of dismissal and possible implicit bias in clinical encounters, requiring acknowledgement, apology, and documentation, alongside systemic reflection on equitable care delivery and communication training for clinical staff.

Doctor_English: Have you been avoiding coming to the hospital because of past negative experiences with healthcare workers?
Patient_Yoruba: Ní ọdún méjì sẹ́yìn, ọmọ mi kú ní ilé ìwòsàn yìí. Mo ti ń yẹra fún rẹ̀ láti ìgbà náà. Irora mi ni ó mú mi wá lónìí.
Clinical_Translation_English: Healthcare avoidance following bereavement at the facility two years ago, a profound grief and institutional trust barrier requiring compassionate acknowledgement, grief support referral, and assurance of quality improvement since that experience.

Doctor_English: Have you been using a mobile health application to track your symptoms, and would you like to share that data?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ń lo ètò fóònù kan láti tọpa ìbà mi àti irora mi fún oṣù mẹ́fà. Mo ní data tó pọ̀. Mo fẹ́ kí dọ́kítà rí i.
Clinical_Translation_English: Patient-generated health data from a symptom tracking application over six months, representing a valuable longitudinal clinical record that should be integrated into the consultation, demonstrating the role of digital health tools in patient engagement and clinical decision support.

Doctor_English: Have you received any WhatsApp health advice from family or friends that you have been following?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọ̀rẹ́ mi fi ìpínrọ̀ ìwòsàn sí mi lórí WhatsApp nípa oògùn tó sọ pé yóò mú suga mi lọ. Mo ti ń tẹ̀lé ọ̀rọ̀ rẹ̀ fún oṣù méjì.
Clinical_Translation_English: Self-treatment based on unverified WhatsApp health advice for diabetes management over two months, illustrating the significant role of social media misinformation in treatment decisions in Nigeria and the need for clear counterinformation and digital health literacy promotion.

Doctor_English: Has the cost of your medication changed recently, and have you been rationing your tablets to make them last longer?
Patient_Yoruba: Iye oògùn mi ti pọ̀ sí i ní oṣù méjì sẹ́yìn nítorí ìlọsíwájú iyebíye. Mo ti bẹ̀rẹ̀ sí mu tábùlẹ́tì kan ní ọjọ́ kan dípò méjì.
Clinical_Translation_English: Medication rationing due to price inflation causing halved dosing of a twice-daily medication, creating subtherapeutic drug levels with risk of treatment failure, resistance development in infections, or loss of disease control in chronic conditions.

Doctor_English: Have you ever been turned away from a health facility because you could not pay, or asked to pay before being seen?
Patient_Yoruba: Ní ilé ìwòsàn mìíràn, wọ́n kò gbà mí tí a fi owó sísanwọlé kò tó. Mo ní láti lọ kọjá ní irora nítorí wọn kò tọ́jú mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Financial gatekeeping causing refusal of emergency care pending payment, a violation of the right to emergency medical treatment and a documented cause of preventable mortality in the Nigerian health system, requiring documentation and patient rights advocacy.

Doctor_English: Have you been worried about the confidentiality of your diagnosis, and has this affected what you have told us today?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo bẹ̀rù pé àrùn mi yóò jáde sí ẹbí mi tàbí ìdílé ọkọ mi. Àwọn ọmọ osùpá wà nínú agbègbè wa. Nítorí ìyẹn, mi ò sọ ohun gbogbo.
Clinical_Translation_English: Incomplete disclosure due to confidentiality concerns related to community social networks within the clinic, a significant barrier to accurate history-taking in stigmatised conditions such as HIV, tuberculosis, and mental illness, requiring explicit confidentiality assurance and private consultation space.

Doctor_English: Have you been sharing your HIV medication with a partner who has not yet been diagnosed or started treatment?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọkọ mi mọ̀ pé mo ní àrùn HIV ṣùgbọ́n ko fẹ́ lọ ṣe ìdánwò. Mo máa ń fún un ní oògùn mi díẹ̀ nígbà-ìgbà.
Clinical_Translation_English: ART sharing with an undiagnosed partner, creating subtherapeutic dosing for the index patient, resistance risk, and delayed diagnosis for the partner, requiring partner testing facilitation, disclosure support, and explanation of the clinical and public health consequences.

Doctor_English: Have you been told that your illness is a punishment or a curse, and has this affected your willingness to take treatment?
Patient_Yoruba: Àwọn ènìyàn ní abúlé wa sọ pé àrùn mi jẹ́ ìjìyà tí Ọlọ́run fi fún mi nítorí ìwà mi. Mo ti bẹ̀rẹ̀ sí gbà gbọ́ bẹ́ẹ̀ àti dáwọ̀ mu oògùn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Medication non-adherence secondary to fatalistic illness attribution, a documented barrier to treatment engagement in Nigerian patients requiring spiritual care integration, collaborative engagement with religious leaders where appropriate, and compassionate corrective health education.

Doctor_English: Have you been receiving any financial support from a government programme or health fund for your treatment?
Patient_Yoruba: Kò sí ìrànlọ́wọ́ ìjọba tí mo mọ̀ nípa rẹ̀ fún àrùn mi. Mo ń san owó gbogbo nínú àpò mi. Ó ti fẹ́ pa mi run ní ọrọ̀-ajé.
Clinical_Translation_English: Catastrophic health expenditure without awareness of available government health financing schemes, requiring social work referral and information on NHIS, state indigent programmes, and disease-specific support funds to reduce out-of-pocket expenditure and improve treatment access.

Doctor_English: Have you ever been diagnosed with asthma, and do you use an inhaler regularly?
Patient_Yoruba: A sọ fún mi pé mo ní àrùn ẹ̀gbẹ̀ ní ọdún mẹ́wàá sẹ́yìn. Mo ní inhaler ṣùgbọ́n mi ò lo rẹ̀ déédéé. Mo máa ń lo nìkan nígbà tí ó le.
Clinical_Translation_English: Known asthmatic with a reliever-only inhaler used as required rather than a preventer regimen, indicating uncontrolled asthma with risk of severe exacerbation requiring stepwise British Thoracic Society guideline-based management review.

Doctor_English: Does cold air or exercise trigger your breathing difficulties, and how quickly does it recover?
Patient_Yoruba: Nígbà tí mo bá ṣeré ní ìgbà òtútù, mí mi ń kúrú ní ìṣẹ́jú mẹ́wàá. Ó máa ń dára lẹ́yìn ìsinmi fún ìṣẹ́jú ogún.
Clinical_Translation_English: Exercise-induced bronchoconstriction triggered by cold air with twenty-minute recovery, requiring pre-exercise bronchodilator use, spirometry with bronchodilator reversibility, and consideration of inhaled corticosteroid prophylaxis.

Doctor_English: Have you been exposed to bird droppings, feathers, or mouldy hay in your work or home environment?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ń tọ́jú adìẹ ní ọgbà mi. Mo ń wọ ìgbẹ́ wọn ní gbogbo owúrọ̀ láìsí ẹ̀wù ìdáàbòbò. Ikọ́ mi bẹ̀rẹ̀ lẹ́yìn ọ̀sẹ̀ mẹ́fa.
Clinical_Translation_English: Organic dust exposure from poultry droppings without respiratory protection developing into a cough six weeks later, consistent with hypersensitivity pneumonitis (bird fancier's lung), requiring precipitin antibodies, HRCT chest, and antigen avoidance.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed that your breathing is worse in certain rooms of your house, such as the bedroom?
Patient_Yoruba: Mí mi máa ń kúrú sí i nínú ìyàrá ìsùn mi ju àwọn yàrá mìíràn lọ. Ní ita ilé, ó máa ń dára. Mo rò pé ó jẹ́ nítorí ìbòjú ìrọ̀lẹ́ mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Room-specific dyspnoea worst in the bedroom with outdoor improvement, consistent with exposure to house dust mites, mould spores, or pet dander in the sleeping environment, requiring allergen avoidance measures, impermeable mattress covers, and immunological assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you ever had a lung function test done, and were you told it showed any obstruction or restriction?
Patient_Yoruba: A ṣe ìdánwò ẹ̀dọ̀fóró mi ní ọdún méjì sẹ́yìn. Wọ́n sọ pé kò ṣe àárọ̀ ṣùgbọ́n mi ò gbà ìtumọ̀ pẹ̀lú.
Clinical_Translation_English: Prior spirometry without patient education on results, representing a missed opportunity for early COPD or asthma management, requiring repeat spirometry with post-bronchodilator reversibility testing and full results explanation.

Doctor_English: Does the breathing difficulty come on suddenly at night, waking you from deep sleep?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo máa ń jí ní àárọ̀ alẹ́ ó ń fẹ mí. Ó bẹ̀rẹ̀ lójijì. Nígbà tí mo bá jókòó títí di àárọ̀, ó máa ń yẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Paroxysmal nocturnal dyspnoea causing sudden nocturnal awakening relieved by sitting upright, the pathognomonic symptom of acute pulmonary oedema from left ventricular failure requiring urgent echocardiography and diuretic therapy.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any blood clots in your sputum larger than a teaspoon amount?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ti ègbọn ẹjẹ̀ tó pọ̀ bíi ìkòkò kéékèéké lẹ́ẹ̀mejì ní ọ̀sẹ̀ kan. Ó ń bani lóru gan-an. Mo bẹ̀rù lọ́pọ̀lọpọ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Massive haemoptysis exceeding teaspoon volume on two occasions in one week, a respiratory emergency requiring immediate hospitalisation, bronchoscopy for localisation, and consideration of bronchial artery embolisation or surgical intervention.

Doctor_English: Has anyone ever told you that your oxygen levels are low, and have you ever needed oxygen at home?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà wọn atẹ́gunfẹ́ mi ní ọdún kan sẹ́yìn, ó sọ pé ó wà ní ìwọ̀n àádọ́rùn-ún lára ọgọ́rùn-ún. Kò fún mi ní àwọn atẹ́gunfẹ́ ní ilé.
Clinical_Translation_English: Resting oxygen saturation of ninety percent without long-term oxygen therapy prescribed, indicating possible undertreated chronic hypoxaemia from COPD or interstitial lung disease, requiring arterial blood gas, pulmonary function tests, and home oxygen assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you had a chest X-ray recently, and were you told anything was seen on it?
Patient_Yoruba: A ṣe X-ray àyà mi ní oṣù mẹ́fà sẹ́yìn. Wọ́n sọ pé nǹkan kan wà ṣùgbọ́n kò sọ ohun tó jẹ́. Wọ́n fún mi ní lẹ́tà ṣùgbọ́n mi ò tẹ̀lé e.
Clinical_Translation_English: Abnormal chest X-ray finding with inadequate patient communication and lost to follow-up, a potentially critical radiological finding such as a pulmonary nodule, mass, or infiltrate requiring immediate urgent recall and CT chest for characterisation.

Doctor_English: Does breathing through a piece of cloth or covering your mouth in dusty areas make the cough worse or better?
Patient_Yoruba: Nígbà tí mo bá bo ẹnu mi pẹ̀lú àṣọ ní àyíká erùpẹ̀, ikọ́ mi ń dínkù síwájú sí i. Ó ń ràn mí lọ́wọ́ ní ọjà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Cloth mask reducing dust-triggered cough, confirming occupational or environmental particulate aetiology and indicating the protective benefit of properly fitted dust masks in the workplace, which should be formally prescribed as part of occupational health management.

Doctor_English: Have you been told you might have pneumonia before, and how were you treated?
Patient_Yoruba: Ní ọdún méjì sẹ́yìn, dọ́kítà sọ pé mo ní pneumonia. A fún mi ní oògùn ní ilé, kò sí ìwọ X-ray. Mo dára lẹ́yìn ọ̀sẹ̀ méjì.
Clinical_Translation_English: Prior pneumonia treated empirically without chest X-ray confirmation or post-treatment follow-up imaging, creating risk of unresolved consolidation, malignancy presenting as pneumonia, or a structural lung abnormality predisposing to recurrent infection.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed your fingers or toes looking blue or swollen at the tips?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìlà ọwọ́ àti ẹsẹ̀ mi ti ń wú díẹ̀ ní orí wọn. Mo tún rí àwọ̀ búlúù díẹ̀ nígbà tí mo bá tutù.
Clinical_Translation_English: Digital clubbing with peripheral cyanosis, clubbing being a sign of chronic hypoxaemia from suppurative lung disease, interstitial fibrosis, cyanotic heart disease, or malignancy, requiring chest CT, spirometry, and cardiac assessment.

Doctor_English: Has anyone in your household been diagnosed with lung disease, and do they smoke indoors?
Patient_Yoruba: Bàbá mi ní àrùn ẹ̀dọ̀fóró àti ń mu sìgá nínú ilé. A ń gbé ní yàrá kéékèéké. A ń mí èéfín rẹ̀ ní gbogbo ọjọ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Chronic indoor secondhand smoke exposure from a household member with lung disease in a crowded dwelling, constituting a significant respiratory hazard for all household members and requiring smoking cessation intervention and ventilation improvement.

Doctor_English: Have you been told you have a pleural effusion, and do you know what that means?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà sọ pé omi wà ní àyíká ẹ̀dọ̀fóró mi ṣùgbọ́n kò ṣàlàyé ìdí rẹ̀. Mo ń bẹ̀rù nítorí mi ò mọ̀ ohun tó túmọ̀ sí.
Clinical_Translation_English: Pleural effusion diagnosed without patient education on aetiology or significance, requiring explanation and investigation of the cause including transudate versus exudate differentiation, malignancy, tuberculosis, cardiac failure, and hypoalbuminaemia.

Doctor_English: Have you ever had a pneumothorax, and were you told you are at risk of it happening again?
Patient_Yoruba: Ẹ̀dọ̀fóró mi fọ́ ní ọdún mẹ́ta sẹ́yìn. Wọ́n fi abẹ́rẹ̀ sínú àyà mi láti mú afẹ́fẹ́ jáde. A kò sọ fún mi pé ó lè ṣẹlẹ̀ lẹ́ẹ̀kan sí i.
Clinical_Translation_English: Prior spontaneous pneumothorax managed with needle aspiration without patient education on recurrence risk of approximately thirty percent, contraindications to air travel and diving, and the threshold for surgical pleurodesis after recurrent episodes.

Doctor_English: Have you ever had a biopsy of your stomach or gut, and were you told you have any inflammation or infection?
Patient_Yoruba: A ṣe ìdánwò ìfun mi ní ọdún méjì sẹ́yìn. Wọ́n sọ pé kòkòrò H. pylori wà nínú ikùn mi ṣùgbọ́n mi ò pari oògùn náà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Helicobacter pylori infection confirmed endoscopically with incomplete eradication therapy, creating risk of peptic ulcer relapse, gastric cancer development, and antibiotic resistance, requiring repeat breath test and second-line triple or quadruple eradication therapy.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any difficulty controlling your bowels, leading to leakage or soiling?
Patient_Yoruba: Ní oṣù mẹ́fà, ìgbẹ́ máa ń jáde láìsí ìfẹ́ mi ní ìgbà-ìgbà. Ó ń jẹ ẹlẹ́yà mi gan-an. Mo ti dáwọ̀ ìgbàfọkànsín dúró.
Clinical_Translation_English: Faecal incontinence causing social withdrawal over six months, a symptom commonly under-reported due to embarrassment, associated with obstetric sphincter injury, rectal prolapse, or neuropathy, requiring anorectal manometry and endoanal ultrasound.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any food coming back up into your mouth hours after eating without any nausea?
Patient_Yoruba: Oúnjẹ máa ń padà wá sí ẹnu mi wákàtí kan tàbí méjì lẹ́yìn jíjẹun. Ko ní ìrora tàbí ríru. Ó máa ń jẹ mí ẹnu.
Clinical_Translation_English: Effortless regurgitation of undigested food hours post-prandially without nausea, the characteristic symptom of a pharyngeal pouch (Zenker's diverticulum) or achalasia, requiring barium swallow and upper endoscopy.

Doctor_English: Have you been passing very pale or clay-coloured stools for more than a week?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìgbẹ́ mi ti di funfun-funfun fún ọ̀sẹ̀ méjì. Ko jọ ìgbẹ́ àárọ̀ mi. Mo tún rí itọ mi di dúdú.
Clinical_Translation_English: Acholic stools with dark urine for two weeks, the classic cholestatic jaundice combination indicating biliary obstruction from choledocholithiasis, cholangiocarcinoma, or pancreatic head carcinoma requiring urgent MRCP and hepatobiliary assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any mass or hardness in your abdomen that you can feel yourself?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo rí ohun tó le ní ẹ̀gbẹ́ ọ̀tún ikùn mi tó ń pọ̀ fún oṣù mẹ́fà. Kò ń dùn ṣùgbọ́n ó wa ní ìgbà gbogbo.
Clinical_Translation_English: Self-palpated right-sided abdominal mass of six months duration without pain, requiring urgent CT abdomen to characterise, as a hard non-tender right iliac fossa mass in an adult must exclude colorectal carcinoma, ovarian pathology, or mesenteric lymphadenopathy.

Doctor_English: Have you been losing weight and feeling full very quickly, associated with upper abdominal discomfort?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ń yó ní ìbẹ̀rẹ̀ jíjẹun, àti irora kéékèéké wà ní àárín ikùn mi. Mo ti tẹ̀nìí ní oṣù mẹ́fà láìgbìyànjú.
Clinical_Translation_English: Early satiety, epigastric discomfort, and unintentional weight loss over six months constituting alarm symptoms for gastric carcinoma or linitis plastica, requiring urgent upper endoscopy with multiple biopsies and CT staging.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any change in the colour of your vomit — has it ever been green or looked like coffee grounds?
Patient_Yoruba: Ohun tí mo ègbọn di àwọ̀ ilẹ̀ kọfì ní lẹ́ẹ̀mejì. Ní àkọ́kọ́ ó jẹ́ oúnjẹ, lẹ́yìn náà ó yí padà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Haematemesis with coffee-ground material in two vomiting episodes following initial food vomiting, indicating active upper gastrointestinal bleeding, most likely from a peptic ulcer or Mallory-Weiss tear, requiring urgent endoscopy and haemostasis.

Doctor_English: Have you had any abdominal surgery involving the bowel that might have left adhesions?
Patient_Yoruba: A ṣe iṣẹ́ abẹ fún mi fún appendicitis ní ọdún mẹ́wàá sẹ́yìn. Lẹ́yìn náà, mo ti ní irora ikùn ní ìgbà-ìgbà tó ń jẹ ìdẹ́rùbamọ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Prior appendicectomy with recurrent episodic abdominal pain over ten years, consistent with small bowel obstruction from post-operative adhesions, requiring CT abdomen during an acute episode to confirm and surgical adhesiolysis assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you been told you have inflammatory bowel disease, and are you on any maintenance treatment?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà sọ pé mo ní Crohn's disease ní ọdún mẹ́ta sẹ́yìn. A fún mi ní oògùn fún ọ̀sẹ̀ díẹ̀ ṣùgbọ́n mi ò tẹ̀síwájú nítorí owó.
Clinical_Translation_English: Crohn's disease without maintenance therapy for three years following initial treatment, creating significant risk of uncontrolled transmural inflammation, stricture formation, fistula, and nutritional deficiency requiring gastroenterology re-engagement and biologic or immunomodulator therapy assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you ever had a liver biopsy, and do you know the result or what it showed?
Patient_Yoruba: A ṣe ìdánwò àsọ ẹ̀dọ̀ mi ní ọdún kan sẹ́yìn. Wọ́n fún mi ní àbájáde lẹ́tà ṣùgbọ́n mi ò kà á. Kò sí ẹni tó ṣàlàyé rẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Liver biopsy result not read or explained to the patient, representing a serious communication failure that may have left significant fibrosis, hepatitis activity, or cirrhosis unaddressed, requiring immediate record retrieval and structured results disclosure.

Doctor_English: Have you been experiencing any bloating that makes your abdomen visibly distended after eating a normal meal?
Patient_Yoruba: Lẹ́yìn oúnjẹ, ikùn mi máa ń wú bíi ọmọ oyún. Ó máa ń bẹ̀rẹ̀ ní ìṣẹ́jú ogún. Ìrọra àti afẹ́fẹ́ ń jáde lẹ́yìn náà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Marked post-prandial bloating with visible distension and relief on flatus passage, consistent with small intestinal bacterial overgrowth, carbohydrate malabsorption, or irritable bowel syndrome requiring hydrogen breath test and dietary assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you ever been told your liver enzymes are raised, and have you been investigated for the cause?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìdánwò ẹjẹ̀ mi fihàn pé ẹ̀dọ̀ mi ni ìṣòro ní ọdún kan sẹ́yìn. Kò sí ẹni tó ṣe ìdánwò mìíràn. Mo kò mu ọtí.
Clinical_Translation_English: Elevated liver enzymes without investigation in a non-drinker, requiring hepatitis B and C serology, autoimmune hepatitis panel, iron studies for haemochromatosis, ceruloplasmin for Wilson's disease, and abdominal ultrasound for fatty liver disease.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any episodes of severe vomiting that are difficult to control, lasting several days?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ègbọn lọ́pọ̀lọpọ̀ fún ọjọ́ mẹ́ta ní oṣù kọ̀ọ̀kan. Ko jẹ́ ohun tí mo jẹ. Ó máa ń lọ fúnra rẹ̀ lẹ́yìn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Cyclic vomiting syndrome with stereotyped monthly episodes lasting three days without dietary trigger and spontaneous resolution, a functional gastrointestinal disorder requiring ondansetron for acute attacks and tricyclic antidepressant prophylaxis.

Doctor_English: Have you ever swallowed any foreign object accidentally or deliberately, and has it passed out in your stool?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọmọ mi gbé ọ̀pẹ̀ kan gbé ní ọjọ́ méjì sẹ́yìn. Kò ní irora ṣùgbọ́n a ń ṣọra fun ìgbẹ́ rẹ̀. Mi ò rí rẹ̀ tíì.
Clinical_Translation_English: Coin ingestion two days ago without spontaneous passage, requiring plain abdominal X-ray to locate the coin, and if in the oesophagus urgent endoscopic retrieval, if beyond the pylorus watchful waiting with stool inspection and repeat imaging if symptomatic.

Doctor_English: Have you been having hiccups that last for more than forty-eight hours without stopping?
Patient_Yoruba: Híhùn mi ti wà fún ọjọ́ mẹ́ta láìdáwọ̀. Ko dára pẹ̀lú omi, àárọ̀, tàbí àyè. Ó ti dẹ́kun jíjẹun mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Persistent hiccups exceeding forty-eight hours causing feeding difficulty, indicating intractable hiccups from a pathological cause such as phrenic nerve irritation, gastric distension, central nervous system lesion, or metabolic disturbance, requiring CT chest and abdomen and neurological review.

Doctor_English: Have you ever had a stroke or mini-stroke, and were you given any blood-thinning medication afterwards?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ní ìjàǹbá ọpọlọ kéékèéké ní ọdún méjì sẹ́yìn. Wọ́n fún mi ní aspirin fún oṣù méjì ṣùgbọ́n mo dáwọ̀ mu nítorí ikùn mi ń ṣòro.
Clinical_Translation_English: TIA two years ago with aspirin self-discontinued due to gastrointestinal intolerance, leaving the patient without antiplatelet secondary prevention and at high recurrent stroke risk, requiring proton pump inhibitor co-prescription and antiplatelet therapy reinstatement.

Doctor_English: Have you ever been told you have a hole in your heart or a structural heart problem since childhood?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà ọmọ sọ pé ọmọ mi ní iho nínú ọkàn rẹ̀ ní ọjọ́-orí ọdún kan. Wọ́n sọ pé yóò pa mọ́ fúnra rẹ̀ ṣùgbọ́n mi ò ṣe ìdánwò lẹ́yìn náà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Childhood diagnosis of a ventricular or atrial septal defect without adult follow-up echocardiography, creating risk of undetected haemodynamic progression, arrhythmia, paradoxical embolism, or pulmonary hypertension requiring urgent adult congenital heart disease clinic review.

Doctor_English: Do you get any swelling of your face, lips, or throat after taking your blood pressure tablet?
Patient_Yoruba: Lẹ́yìn tí mo bẹ̀rẹ̀ oògùn ìpàná ẹjẹ̀ tuntun, ète mi àti ọfun mi máa ń wú ní ìgbà-ìgbà. Mo bẹ̀rù ìgbà náà.
Clinical_Translation_English: ACE inhibitor-induced angioedema with recurrent pharyngeal and labial swelling, a potentially life-threatening adverse drug reaction mandating immediate drug cessation, substitution with an ARB, and prescription of an adrenaline auto-injector pending allergy review.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed that one of your legs feels heavier or more tired after walking compared to the other?
Patient_Yoruba: Ẹsẹ̀ ọ̀tún mi máa ń rẹ̀ mi ju ẹsẹ̀ òsì lọ nígbà tí mo bá ń rìn. Ó ti ń buru sí i ní oṣù mẹ́fà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Unilateral leg fatigue on walking with progressive worsening, suggesting unilateral peripheral arterial disease affecting the right iliac or femoral artery, requiring ankle-brachial pressure index measurement and vascular surgery referral.

Doctor_English: Have you been told you have an irregular heartbeat that needs monitoring or treatment?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà sọ pé ọkàn mi máa ń lu ní ọ̀nà tó yàtọ̀. Wọ́n fún mi ní oògùn àti sọ pé kí n padà wá ṣùgbọ́n mi ò lọ.
Clinical_Translation_English: Atrial fibrillation diagnosed and partially treated without follow-up, creating risk of inadequate anticoagulation for stroke prevention, subtherapeutic rate control, and undetected paroxysmal AF episodes, requiring immediate rhythm assessment and CHA2DS2-VASc scoring.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any pulsating sensation or mass in your abdomen, particularly near your navel?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo máa ń ní ìmọ̀lára bíi ọkàn ń lu ní àárín ikùn mi. Ó ń bani lóru nígbà tí mo bá dùbúlẹ̀. Mo rí rẹ̀ fún oṣù mẹ́fà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Pulsatile epigastric mass with six months history in a patient with cardiovascular risk factors, raising urgent concern for an abdominal aortic aneurysm requiring immediate ultrasound measurement and vascular surgery referral if the diameter exceeds five centimetres.

Doctor_English: Have you been getting any chest pain that is relieved immediately by glyceryl trinitrate spray?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ní ìfọ̀n GTN fún ọdún méjì. Irora àyà mi máa ń lọ ní ìṣẹ́jú méjì lẹ́yìn tí mo bá lo. Mo ń nílò rẹ̀ ní ìgbà-ìgbà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Nitrate-responsive chest pain confirming a vasospastic or ischaemic cardiac aetiology, with episodic use over two years indicating either stable angina with infrequent symptoms or worsening disease if usage is increasing, requiring exercise stress testing and cardiology review.

Doctor_English: Have you had any episodes of your heart suddenly stopping and needing resuscitation that you know of?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọdún kan sẹ́yìn, mo ṣubú ní ọjà. Àwọn ènìyàn tẹ àyà mi. A gbé mi wọ ilé ìwòsàn pẹ̀lú àwọn ẹrọ àti a fún mi ní ìgbọ̀ọ̀rọ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Cardiac arrest survivor with external defibrillation and ambulance transport one year ago, requiring implantable cardioverter-defibrillator assessment, electrophysiology study for arrhythmia characterisation, and formal cardiac rehabilitation programme enrolment.

Doctor_English: Do you have any pacemaker or cardiac device implanted, and when was it last checked?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ní ẹrọ kéékèéké nínú àyà mi tí a fi sínú ọdún mẹ́fà sẹ́yìn. A ṣe ìdánwò rẹ̀ ní ọdún méjì sẹ́yìn. Ko sí ìṣayẹ̀wò lẹ́yìn náà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Cardiac implantable electronic device overdue for interrogation by two years, creating risk of undetected battery depletion, lead failure, inappropriate therapy delivery, or undocumented arrhythmia episodes, requiring urgent device clinic appointment.

Doctor_English: Have you been told your heart valves are leaking or narrow, and has this been monitored recently?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà sọ pé àwọ ọkàn mi kan ń jó nígbà tí mo bí ọmọ. Kò sí ìdánwò mìíràn lẹ́yìn ọdún mẹ́fà náà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Valvular regurgitation identified six years ago without subsequent echocardiographic surveillance, creating risk of undetected haemodynamic progression to heart failure or indication for surgical valve repair or replacement, requiring immediate echocardiography.

Doctor_English: Are you currently under the care of more than one doctor, and do they know about each other?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ń lọ sí dọ́kítà mẹ́ta ní àwọn ilé ìwòsàn tó yàtọ̀. Kò sí ẹni nínú wọn tó mọ̀ nípa àwọn mìíràn. Mo gba àwọn oògùn ọ̀tọ̀ọ̀tọ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Care fragmentation with three unco-ordinated prescribers, creating polypharmacy risk, potential drug duplications, and contradictory management plans, requiring a nominated lead clinician, shared care record, and medication reconciliation.

Doctor_English: Have you ever had a full health check or MOT done, and do you know all your key health numbers?
Patient_Yoruba: Mi ò tíì ṣe ìdánwò ìlera pípé rárá. Mi ò mọ̀ ìwọ̀n ẹjẹ̀ mi, suga, tàbí ọ̀rá. Mo jẹ́ ọmọ ọdún àádọ́ta.
Clinical_Translation_English: No comprehensive health assessment in a fifty-year-old with unknown cardiovascular risk factors, indicating the need for a full health MOT including blood pressure, fasting glucose, lipid profile, BMI, and cancer screening eligibility review.

Doctor_English: Have you been feeling generally unwell for several months without a specific diagnosis being made?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ti ń nímọ̀lára àìgbádùn fún oṣù mẹ́fà. Àwọn ìdánwò mẹ́ta ti ṣẹlẹ̀ ṣùgbọ́n kò sí àbájáde pàtàkì. Mo mọ̀ pé nǹkan kan ń ṣẹlẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Medically unexplained symptoms persisting for six months with three inconclusive investigations, requiring a patient-centred approach to explore psychosocial contributions, functional illness, undisclosed symptoms, and a systematic diagnostic safety netting review.

Doctor_English: Have you been told you have more than one long-term condition, and do you find it hard to manage them all?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ní àtọ̀gbẹ̀, ìpàná ẹjẹ̀ gíga, àti àrùn ẹ̀gbẹ̀. Mo mu oògùn mẹ́wàá ní ọjọ́ kan. O ń ṣe mí ìdàrúdàpọ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Triple comorbidity with ten daily medications indicating complex multimorbidity, requiring a structured multimorbidity consultation using a personalised care approach that prioritises the patient's own treatment goals over single-disease guideline targets.

Doctor_English: Have you had all your recommended health screenings for your age group this year?
Patient_Yoruba: Mi ò mọ̀ àwọn ìdánwò tí a ṣe fún ọjọ́-orí mi. Dọ́kítà mi kò sọ fún mi nípa wọn. Mo jẹ́ obìnrin ọmọ ọdún àádọ́ta àti ọrin-ọdún.
Clinical_Translation_English: A woman in her mid-fifties without awareness of age-appropriate screening including breast mammography, cervical smear, bowel cancer screening, blood pressure, and cholesterol testing, indicating a preventive health gap requiring structured screening invitation and health promotion.

Doctor_English: Do you have a regular doctor who knows your full medical history, or do you see different doctors each time?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo máa ń rí dọ́kítà tó yàtọ̀ ní ìgbà kọ̀ọ̀kan. Kò sí ẹni tó mọ ìtàn àrùn mi pátápátá. Mo ń bẹ̀rẹ̀ láti ìbẹ̀rẹ̀ ní gbogbo ìpàdé.
Clinical_Translation_English: Lack of continuity of care with repeated history-taking from scratch, associated with delayed diagnosis, missed follow-up of chronic conditions, and patient dissatisfaction, highlighting the need for a registered primary care physician relationship.

Doctor_English: Have you been to the emergency department more than three times in the past year for the same problem?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ti wọ ilé ìwòsàn ìpàdédè lẹ́ẹ̀mẹ́rìnlá ní ọdún kan fún irora orí mi. Wọ́n máa ń tọ́jú mi kí wọ́n sì jí mi lọ. Ko dára sí i.
Clinical_Translation_English: High emergency department utilisation for headache without outpatient management plan, indicating a care gap where chronic headache should be managed proactively in primary or neurology care with a personalised emergency action plan rather than repeat acute presentations.

Doctor_English: Have you been taking aspirin every day, and was this recommended by a doctor or did you start it yourself?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo bẹ̀rẹ̀ mu aspirin fúnra mi nítorí ẹbí mi ní àrùn ọkàn. Mi ò béèrè lọ́wọ́ dọ́kítà. Mo ti ń mu fún ọdún méjì.
Clinical_Translation_English: Self-initiated aspirin use for primary cardiovascular prevention without medical advice, a practice no longer routinely recommended in primary prevention due to bleeding risk outweighing benefit in those without established cardiovascular disease, requiring individualised risk-benefit discussion.

Doctor_English: Have you been told your kidneys, liver, or other organs may be affected by one of your long-term conditions?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà sọ pé àtọ̀gbẹ̀ mi lè nípa lórí àwọn ẹ̀dọ̀ ito mi. Kò sọ mìíràn. Mi ò ṣe ìdánwò ẹ̀dọ̀ ito mi fún ọdún méjì.
Clinical_Translation_English: Diabetic nephropathy warning without renal function surveillance for two years, a critical monitoring gap given that early nephropathy is reversible with tight glycaemic control and ACE inhibitor therapy but progresses silently to end-stage renal disease without detection.

Doctor_English: Have you been told you need to lose weight for a specific health reason, and have you been given any support to do so?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà sọ pé kí n padàkù fún àtọ̀gbẹ̀ mi ṣùgbọ́n kò tọ́ka mi sí ètò tàbí ẹni tó lè ràn mí lọ́wọ́. Mo gbìyànjú fúnra mi ṣùgbọ́n kò ṣiṣẹ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Medical weight loss advice without structured support for diabetes management, representing an incomplete intervention, as brief advice alone achieves minimal weight loss compared to structured behavioural programmes with dietitian support and pharmacotherapy consideration.

Doctor_English: Have you had your immunisations updated as an adult, particularly for flu or pneumococcal disease?
Patient_Yoruba: Mi ò gba àjesara agbalagba kankan rí. Mi ò mọ̀ pé àwọn agbalagba nílò àjesara. Ibà ń jẹ mi ní gbogbo ọdún.
Clinical_Translation_English: No adult immunisation history with annual influenza illness, indicating missed annual influenza vaccination and one-time pneumococcal vaccination eligibility, particularly important in patients with diabetes, chronic lung or heart disease, or over sixty-five years.

Doctor_English: Have you had a medication review in the past year where someone checked all your tablets together?
Patient_Yoruba: Kò sí ẹni tó ṣe àyẹ̀wò gbogbo oògùn mi papọ̀ fún ọdún méjì. Mo máa ń gba àwọn atunṣe tuntun láìsí ẹnìkan tó ṣàyẹ̀wò àwọn tó ti wà.
Clinical_Translation_English: No medication review for two years in a patient with multiple prescribers and polypharmacy, indicating a significant patient safety risk requiring a structured medicines optimisation review to identify deprescribing opportunities, interactions, and unnecessary medications.

Doctor_English: Have you been told you have a family history of a condition that means you should be screened more often?
Patient_Yoruba: Ẹ̀gbọ́n mi àgbàlagbà ni àrùn jẹjẹrẹ ifun ní ọjọ́-orí ogójì. Dọ́kítà kò sọ ohun ti èyí túmọ̀ sí fún mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: First-degree relative with colorectal cancer at forty years, classifying the patient as high familial risk requiring colonoscopy from age thirty or ten years before the affected relative's diagnosis, whichever is earlier, with five-yearly surveillance thereafter.

Doctor_English: Have you been feeling that your treatment is not working, and have you told your doctor this?
Patient_Yoruba: Oògùn mi kò dára sí i ju ìgbà tí mo bẹ̀rẹ̀ lọ. Mo kò sọ fún dọ́kítà nítorí mi ò fẹ́ jẹ ẹni tó ń ṣàánú. Ó ń ṣe mí ìbànújẹ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Unexpressed therapeutic inefficacy due to fear of being perceived as a difficult patient, a communication barrier that delays treatment optimisation and leads to ongoing morbidity, requiring explicit reassurance that reporting treatment failure is both appropriate and necessary.

Doctor_English: Have you been advised to make lifestyle changes, and how successful have you been in sticking to them?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà sọ pé kí n dáwọ̀ sìgá, jẹun dáradára, àti ṣeré. Mo dáwọ̀ sìgá fún oṣù mẹ́fà ṣùgbọ́n mo padà sí i. Oúnjẹ mi kò yí padà rárá.
Clinical_Translation_English: Partial lifestyle modification success with smoking relapse and unchanged diet, indicating the need for motivational interviewing, behavioural change support, nicotine replacement reassessment, and dietitian referral rather than repeated directive advice.

Doctor_English: Has the pain been there continuously since it started, or have there been days when you were completely free of it?
Patient_Yoruba: Irora mi ti wà ní ìgbà gbogbo fún ọjọ́ mẹ́wàá. Ko sí ọjọ́ tí mo ti ní ìsinmi rárá. Ó le ní ọjọ́-ọjọ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Unremitting constant pain over ten days without pain-free intervals, indicating an organic rather than functional aetiology and requiring systematic investigation including inflammatory markers, imaging, and specialist review.

Doctor_English: Has anyone in your family had a similar problem, and at what age did it start for them?
Patient_Yoruba: Bàbá mi ní àrùn ọkàn ní ọjọ́-orí àádọ́ta-àti-márùn-ún. Ẹ̀gbọ́n mi tún ní irora àyà ní ọjọ́-orí ogójì. Mo bẹ̀rẹ̀ sí ní irora àyà ní ọjọ́-orí mọ́kànlélọ́gbọ̀n.
Clinical_Translation_English: Strong familial premature cardiovascular disease pattern with the patient developing symptoms earlier than affected relatives, indicating a likely hereditary risk requiring genetic lipid disorders screening, fasting lipid profile, and early aggressive risk factor management.

Doctor_English: Does the pain radiate anywhere, and can you trace the path it takes with your finger?
Patient_Yoruba: Bẹ́ẹ̀ ni, irora náà máa ń rọ láti ìhà ọ̀tún ìsàlẹ̀ àyà mi, nípa abẹ́ ìhà àyà, sísàlẹ̀ títí dé ìdì mi ọ̀tún.
Clinical_Translation_English: Right-sided lower chest to right groin radiation tracing the ureteric pathway, the classic referred pain distribution of right ureteric colic from a renal calculus migrating through the ureter, requiring urgent renal ultrasound or CT KUB.

Doctor_English: Does the pain wake you from sleep, and if so, at what time of night does it typically happen?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo máa ń jí ní àárọ̀ àárọ̀ ọjọ́ ìmọ́lẹ̀, bóyá ní wákàtí méjì tàbí mẹ́ta, pẹ̀lú irora ìdọ̀tí tó le. Ó máa ń lọ lẹ́yìn wákàtí kan.
Clinical_Translation_English: Early morning pelvic pain at two to three a.m. with spontaneous resolution over one hour, consistent with cyclical hormonal-mediated pain from endometriosis or interstitial cystitis, requiring pelvic MRI and gynaecology or urology assessment.

Doctor_English: Has the problem been getting steadily worse, staying the same, or has it been getting better on its own?
Patient_Yoruba: Ó ti ń burú síwájú sí i díẹ̀díẹ̀ fún oṣù mẹ́fà. Ko sí ìsinmi kankan. Mo ti gbìyànjú oògùn meji ṣùgbọ́n kò ṣiṣẹ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Monotonically progressive worsening over six months without plateau or remission and failure of two treatment attempts, indicating a progressive organic condition requiring urgent specialist review and systematic investigation rather than continued empirical management.

Doctor_English: Does anything you eat or drink make the pain noticeably better or worse within an hour?
Patient_Yoruba: Wàrà àti oúnjẹ tó ní ọ̀rá ń mú irora àárín àyà mi buru sí i lẹ́yìn ìṣẹ́jú ogún. Omi tútù ń mú ki ó yẹ̀ díẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Dairy and fatty food provocation with cold water relief of mid-sternal pain, a pattern pointing toward oesophageal spasm or GERD exacerbated by fat-induced lower oesophageal sphincter relaxation, requiring oesophageal manometry and pH monitoring.

Doctor_English: Can you rate your pain on most days, and tell me the best day and worst day scores in the past week?
Patient_Yoruba: Irora mi máa wà ní mẹ́fà lọ́jọ́ tó dára jù. Ní ọjọ́ tó burú jù, ó wà ní mẹ́sàn-án. Mo kò ní ọjọ́ kan tó wà ní ìwọ̀n méjì tàbí mẹ́ta.
Clinical_Translation_English: Pain range of six to nine out of ten over one week with no low-pain days, indicating inadequately controlled chronic pain requiring analgesic ladder review, specialist pain management referral, and psychological pain coping strategy assessment.

Doctor_English: Has the symptom ever completely disappeared for weeks or months at a time before coming back?
Patient_Yoruba: Bẹ́ẹ̀ ni, irora mi máa ń lọ fún oṣù mẹ́ta, lẹ́yìn náà ó máa ń padà. O ti ṣẹlẹ̀ lẹ́ẹ̀mẹ́rin ní ọdún méjì.
Clinical_Translation_English: Relapsing and remitting symptom pattern with three-month remissions over two years, consistent with an episodic inflammatory or autoimmune condition such as multiple sclerosis, Crohn's disease, or episodic cluster headache, requiring biomarker and imaging assessment during a symptomatic phase.

Doctor_English: Does physical activity make the symptom better, worse, or have no effect?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìdárayá ń mú ki mí mi kúrú sí i ṣùgbọ́n lẹ́yìn ìsinmi ó máa ń dára. Nígbà tí mo bá jókòó ní gbogbo ọjọ́, ó ń le sí i.
Clinical_Translation_English: Exercise-induced dyspnoea with rest recovery and worsening with sedentary periods, suggesting deconditioning superimposed on underlying cardiorespiratory disease, requiring cardiopulmonary exercise testing to differentiate cardiac, pulmonary, and deconditioning contributions.

Doctor_English: Has anyone else in your household been having the same symptoms, suggesting a shared cause?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìyàwó mi àti ọmọ mi ọdún mẹ́wàá tún ní irora ikùn àti ìgbẹ́ gbuuru. A jẹ oúnjẹ ìpamọ́ kanna ní ọjọ́ méjì sẹ́yìn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Cluster of gastrointestinal illness across three household members sharing the same stored food two days prior, the epidemiological pattern of a common source food poisoning outbreak requiring stool culture, food sample testing, and public health notification.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any association between your symptoms and your menstrual cycle?
Patient_Yoruba: Àrùn mi máa ń burú ní ọ̀sẹ̀ ṣáájú oṣù mi àti yẹ̀ lẹ́yìn ọjọ́ mẹ́ta ti oṣù. O ti jẹ́ bẹ́ẹ̀ fún ọdún méjì.
Clinical_Translation_English: Premenstrual symptom exacerbation with post-menstrual remission over two years, consistent with premenstrual exacerbation of an underlying condition such as migraine, asthma, endometriosis, or premenstrual dysphoric disorder, requiring prospective symptom diary and hormonal assessment.

Doctor_English: Does the symptom appear only when you are stressed or anxious, or does it happen regardless of your mood?
Patient_Yoruba: Irora mi máa ń bẹ̀rẹ̀ tàbí buru sí i nígbà tí mo bá ń yọ̀ nípa iṣẹ́. Nígbà tí inú mi bá balẹ̀, ó ń yẹ̀ ṣùgbọ́n ko lọ pátápátá.
Clinical_Translation_English: Stress-provoked symptom exacerbation with incomplete rest-period resolution, indicating a biopsychosocial condition where psychological factors modulate but do not exclusively cause the symptom, requiring both physical investigation and cognitive-behavioural therapy.

Doctor_English: Has the symptom been affecting your ability to work, sleep, or take care of your family?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ti dáwọ̀ ṣiṣẹ́ dúró fún ọ̀sẹ̀ mẹ́ta. Ìsùn mi kò dára. Àwọn ọmọ mi ń jẹun nínú ìṣòro nítorí mi ò lè sè.
Clinical_Translation_English: Significant functional impairment across occupational, sleep, and domestic domains over three weeks, indicating severe disease burden requiring both clinical urgency and social support assessment for the patient's dependants.

Doctor_English: Does changing your posture — sitting, standing, or lying down — make the symptom better or worse?
Patient_Yoruba: Irora mi ń yẹ̀ nígbà tí mo bá tẹ̀ iwájú kí n sì fi ọwọ́ gbé orúkún mi. Dùbúlẹ̀ pẹ̀tẹ́lẹ̀ ń mú ki ó buru sí i gan-an.
Clinical_Translation_English: Positional relief with forward lean and worsening with supine positioning, indicating pericarditis where forward flexion reduces pericardial friction, or severe COPD where tripod positioning optimises accessory respiratory muscle function.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed the symptom coming on more quickly than before, such as needing less activity to trigger it?
Patient_Yoruba: Ṣáájú, mo ń rìn fún ìṣẹ́jú mẹ́wàá ṣáájú kí irora tó wáyé. Ní báyìí, ó wáyé ní ìṣẹ́jú méjì. Ó ti ń buru sí i ní oṣù méjì.
Clinical_Translation_English: Reducing exercise threshold for symptom provocation over two months, a clinically significant deterioration pattern indicating progressive disease — whether angina, claudication, or dyspnoea on exertion — requiring urgent specialist reassessment.

Doctor_English: Does taking a deep breath make the pain worse, and do you find yourself breathing shallowly to avoid it?
Patient_Yoruba: Mí jìnnà ń mú irora àyà mi buru sí i. Mo ti bẹ̀rẹ̀ sí mí kéké nítorí ìbẹ̀rù irora. Ó ń mú ki mí mi kúrú.
Clinical_Translation_English: Pleuritic chest pain causing voluntary respiratory splinting with secondary hypoventilation, indicating pleural pathology from pleurisy, pneumonia, pulmonary embolism, or rib fracture, with the splinting itself creating risk for atelectasis and pneumonia.

Doctor_English: Has the symptom been present every single day without exception since it started?
Patient_Yoruba: Bẹ́ẹ̀ ni, orí mi ti ń fọ̀ ní gbogbo ọjọ́ fún oṣù mẹ́fà. Ko sí ọjọ́ kan tí kò ṣẹlẹ̀. Ó máa ń yàtọ̀ nínú bí ó ti le.
Clinical_Translation_English: Daily headache for six months without a single pain-free day meeting the definition of chronic daily headache, requiring classification into chronic migraine, chronic tension-type, or hemicrania continua with indomethacin trial, and investigation to exclude intracranial hypertension.

Doctor_English: Does the symptom have any relationship to what you have been eating or drinking in the previous twenty-four hours?
