Patient_Yoruba: Nígbà tí mo bá jẹ ẹyin tàbí ẹpa, ikùn mi ń ṣe mí ìṣòro ní ọjọ́ tó tẹ̀le. Ó ń ṣẹlẹ̀ gbogbo ìgbà tí mo jẹ wọn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Reproducible gastrointestinal symptoms following egg and peanut ingestion indicating probable IgE-mediated food allergy or food intolerance, requiring skin prick testing, specific IgE measurement, and supervised food challenge under allergy specialist guidance.

Doctor_English: Has the pain ever been so severe that you have attended an emergency department or called for help?
Patient_Yoruba: Lẹ́ẹ̀mẹ́ta ní ọdún yìí, irora mi le tó jẹ́ kí a gbé mi wọ ilé ìwòsàn. Wọ́n máa ń fún mi ní abẹ́rẹ̀ ìtunu lẹ́yìn náà jí mi lọ.
Clinical_Translation_English: Three emergency attendances in one year for pain crisis with parenteral analgesia required, indicating severe uncontrolled chronic pain with crisis pattern warranting specialist pain management referral, chronic opioid therapy review, and psychological pain rehabilitation.

Doctor_English: Is this the worst this problem has ever been, or have you had episodes that were even more severe in the past?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìgbà yìí ni ó le jù tí mo ti ní rí. Àwọn ìgbà tó kọjá, ó máa ń le ṣùgbọ́n kò jẹ́ bíi ìgbà yìí.
Clinical_Translation_English: Peak severity exceeding all prior episodes, a clinical red flag indicating possible new pathology superimposed on a chronic condition, acute decompensation, or a qualitatively different diagnosis, requiring thorough reassessment rather than assumption of the same diagnosis.

Doctor_English: Does the pain seem to be in the bone itself, or more in the surrounding muscles and soft tissue?
Patient_Yoruba: Irora wà jíjìn nínú eegun mi, kì í ṣe ní ita rẹ̀. Nígbà tí a bá tẹ eegun tààrà, irora ń burú sí i.
Clinical_Translation_English: Deep bone tenderness on direct palpation distinguishing bony pathology such as stress fracture, osteomyelitis, or malignant infiltration from superficial myofascial pain, requiring X-ray and MRI for suspected bone pathology.

Doctor_English: Have you tried any home remedies, traditional treatments, or over-the-counter medications for this problem?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo gbìyànjú oògùn irora tí mo rà ní ọjà, àgbo ewé, àti omi gbígbóná. Kò sí nínú wọn tó ṣiṣẹ́ dáradára.
Clinical_Translation_English: Failed self-treatment with analgesics, herbal preparations, and heat therapy, indicating a condition refractory to simple measures and requiring clinical assessment, while the herbal preparations used should be identified for potential drug interaction screening.

Doctor_English: Has the problem been there so long that you have started to accept it as normal for you?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ti ń gbà pé orí fífọ̀ jẹ́ ara ìgbé ayé mi fún ọdún mẹ́wàá. Mi ò mọ̀ pé ó yẹ ki n wá ìrànlọ́wọ́ fun rẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Normalisation of a ten-year chronic symptom with delayed health-seeking behaviour, a pattern that delays diagnosis and allows progressive organ damage in conditions such as chronic hypertension, diabetes, and inflammatory disease, requiring active systematic re-evaluation.

Doctor_English: Does the symptom interfere with your sexual life, and is this something you are comfortable discussing?
Patient_Yoruba: Àrùn mi ti nípa lórí ìbálòpọ̀ wa fún oṣù mẹ́fà. Ọkọ mi ń yọ̀. Mo bẹ̀rù láti sọ nítorí ó jẹ́ ọ̀rọ̀ àṣà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Sexual dysfunction from chronic illness disclosed after explicit permission-giving, illustrating the importance of routine sexual health enquiry in chronic disease management with cultural sensitivity, and requiring both clinical and psychosexual support.

Doctor_English: If you had to describe your overall health right now on a scale of one to ten, what would you say?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo fún ara mi ní mẹ́ta lára ọgọ́rùn-ún. Mo jẹ́ ẹni tó ń gbé fún àwọn ọmọ mi nìkan. Mi ò ní ìmọ̀lára ìlera rárá.
Clinical_Translation_English: Patient-reported global health score of three out of ten with existential purpose restricted to parenting, indicating profound health-related quality of life impairment requiring comprehensive holistic assessment, palliative support if appropriate, and mental health screening.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any improvement in your overall energy levels or mood since starting treatment?
Patient_Yoruba: Lẹ́yìn oṣù kan lórí oògùn tuntun, àárẹ̀ mi ti dínkù díẹ̀. Inú mi tún ń dára díẹ̀. Ṣùgbọ́n ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ àmì ṣì wà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Partial treatment response with improved energy and mood but persistent residual symptoms after one month, indicating the need for dose optimisation, adequate treatment duration assessment, and consideration of adjunctive therapy rather than premature treatment change.

Doctor_English: Has the problem affected your confidence or willingness to go out in public?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ti dáwọ̀ jáde ilé nítorí ìbẹ̀rù pé àrùn mi yóò ṣẹlẹ̀ ní gbangba. Mo ń gbé ìgbé ayé àdéhùn nínú ilé.
Clinical_Translation_English: Agoraphobic avoidance secondary to chronic illness fear of public symptom occurrence, a significant psychological complication of chronic disease requiring psychological support, graded exposure therapy, and reassurance about emergency management strategies.

Doctor_English: Have you been told to avoid certain activities, foods, or environments because of your condition?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà sọ pé kí n yẹra fún iṣẹ́ àjàkálẹ̀ àti jíjẹ ẹran pupa ṣùgbọ́n mi ò tẹ̀lé e nítorí iṣẹ́ mi nílò gbígbe àwọn ohun tó wúwo.
Clinical_Translation_English: Occupational and dietary restriction advice not followed due to work requirements, illustrating the tension between clinical recommendations and social determinants of health, requiring an occupational health assessment and realistic individually tailored activity guidance.

Doctor_English: Has the symptom been affecting different parts of your body at different times rather than staying in one place?
Patient_Yoruba: Irora yí padà. Oṣù kan ó wà ní orúkún mi, oṣù tó tẹ̀le ó lọ sí ìpín ọwọ́ mi. Báyìí ó wà ní ẹ̀hin mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Migratory pain involving knees, hand joints, and now the back in a sequential pattern, consistent with reactive arthritis, palindromic rheumatism, or early rheumatoid arthritis, requiring acute phase reactants, rheumatoid factor, anti-CCP, and rheumatology referral.

Doctor_English: Does anyone close to you think your symptoms are more serious than you do, and has this caused tension?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìyàwó mi fẹ́ kí n wá ilé ìwòsàn ní ọdún méjì sẹ́yìn ṣùgbọ́n mo kọ. O ń pariwo nípa rẹ̀. Inú mi bí i. Mo wá lónìí nítorí irora le jù.
Clinical_Translation_English: Two-year delay in healthcare presentation against a partner's advice, finally presenting due to symptom severity, illustrating the health-protective role of social support while highlighting the need to explore the patient's own illness perception and barriers to care.

Doctor_English: Is the symptom purely physical, or do you think there is a psychological or emotional component to it?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo gbà pé àárẹ̀ mi bẹ̀rẹ̀ lẹ́yìn ìṣẹ̀lẹ̀ tó ṣeni lẹ́nu ní iṣẹ́. Ó ṣeéṣe pé wọ́n ní ibatan. Ṣùgbọ́n irora naa jẹ gidi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Patient insight into psychosomatic contribution to fatigue following a workplace trauma, demonstrating health literacy and readiness for integrated biopsychosocial management, requiring both physical investigation to exclude organic disease and psychological therapy.

Doctor_English: Have you had this symptom since childhood, or is it something that has started later in life?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ti ní àrùn awọ ara mi láti ọmọ ọdún mẹ́fa. Ó máa ń burú ní àwọn ìgbà-ìgbà àti yẹ̀ ní ìgbà mìíràn. Kò tíì lọ pátápátá rí.
Clinical_Translation_English: Lifelong atopic condition from age six with episodic exacerbations and incomplete remissions, consistent with atopic eczema persisting into adulthood, which is often more refractory than childhood disease and requires step-up therapy with dupilumab or systemic immunosuppression.

Doctor_English: Do you feel you have been fully listened to today, and is there anything you wanted to say that you have not yet said?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo nímọ̀lára pé a gbọ́ mi lónìí. Ṣùgbọ́n ọ̀rọ̀ kan ṣì wà tí mi ò sọ — mo ti ń rò nípa ìdádúró ìgbé ayé mi nígbà-ìgbà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Closing disclosure of passive suicidal ideation after feeling heard, demonstrating the critical importance of the ICE closing question in consultations — Ideas, Concerns, Expectations — as a safety net for undisclosed red flag symptoms, requiring immediate risk assessment.

Doctor_English: Have your symptoms changed at all since you first came in today, while waiting to be seen?
Patient_Yoruba: Bẹ́ẹ̀ ni, irora àyà mi ti bẹ̀rẹ̀ sí tan sí apá òsì mi àti ẹrẹ̀kẹ́ mi ní wákàtí kan kẹ́yìín. Ó le sí i ju ìgbà tí mo dé lọ.
Clinical_Translation_English: Symptom evolution during the waiting period with new radiation of chest pain to the left arm and jaw, constituting progression to a higher-risk ACS presentation requiring immediate triage escalation, ECG, troponin, and emergency cardiology review.

Doctor_English: Is there anything about your diagnosis or treatment that you are finding it hard to accept?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ti mọ̀ nípa àrùn HIV mi fún oṣù mẹ́fà ṣùgbọ́n mi ò tíì sọ fún ẹnikẹ́ni. Mo ń gbé pẹ̀lú ìpamọ́ ìdẹ́rùbamọ̀ yìí nìkan.
Clinical_Translation_English: Six months of undisclosed HIV diagnosis carried alone without social support, indicating the psychological burden of HIV stigma in Nigeria and the urgent need for peer support referral, disclosure counselling, ART initiation, and long-term mental health support.

Doctor_English: Before we finish today, is there anything else that has been worrying you that we have not yet discussed?
Patient_Yoruba: Bẹ́ẹ̀ ni, ìbẹ̀rù nìkan pé ọmọ mi tó jẹ́ ọdún mẹ́wàá ń gbọ́ àwọn ọ̀rọ̀ mi nígbà tí mo bá ń sọ̀rọ̀ nítorí irora. Mo bẹ̀rù nípa ipa rẹ̀ lórí rẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Parental concern about a child's secondary trauma from witnessing parental pain, raised only at consultation close, illustrating the value of a structured closing question and the need for child psychological wellbeing assessment alongside the patient's own clinical management.

Doctor_English: Have you had any previous admissions for mental health reasons, and how did you find the care you received?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo wọ ilé ìwòsàn ọpọlọ ní ọdún mẹ́ta sẹ́yìn. Ìtọ́jú náà ràn mí lọ́wọ́ ṣùgbọ́n ìrírí ìmọ́kùnlé jẹ mí lọ́pọ̀lọpọ̀. Mo ń gbìyànjú láti yẹra fún àtúnwọlé.
Clinical_Translation_English: Prior inpatient psychiatric admission experienced as therapeutically helpful but traumatic in terms of restraint or containment experiences, creating a barrier to voluntary admission if needed and requiring trauma-informed care planning for any future episode.

Doctor_English: Have you been told your bone density is low, and have you been on any treatment for osteoporosis?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà sọ pé eegun mi fẹ̀ẹ́ lẹ́yìn ìdánwò DEXA ní ọdún méjì sẹ́yìn. A fún mi ní àwọn oògùn kálsíọ̀mù ṣùgbọ́n mi ò mu wọn déédéé.
Clinical_Translation_English: Confirmed osteoporosis on DEXA scan two years ago with calcium supplementation non-adherence, creating ongoing fracture risk particularly of the hip and vertebral spine, requiring bisphosphonate therapy initiation and fall prevention assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you been diagnosed with sleep apnoea, and are you using your CPAP machine every night?
Patient_Yoruba: A fún mi ní ẹrọ CPAP ní ọdún kan sẹ́yìn ṣùgbọ́n mo lo rẹ̀ lẹ́ẹ̀mejì tàbí mẹ́ta ní ọ̀sẹ̀ nìkan. Ó ń ṣe mí ìdẹ́rùbamọ̀ àti mi ò lè farada rẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: CPAP non-adherence with fewer than three nights per week usage in a patient with obstructive sleep apnoea, creating persistent nocturnal hypoxaemia and cardiovascular risk, requiring mask fitting review, pressure optimisation, and CPAP tolerance support.

Doctor_English: Have you had any chest infections requiring antibiotics in the past twelve months?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ti mu oògùn àkóràn fún àrùn àyà lẹ́ẹ̀mẹ́fà ní ọdún kan. Gbogbo wọn ràn mí lọ́wọ́ ṣùgbọ́n ó máa ń padà ní ọ̀sẹ̀ díẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Six antibiotic courses for chest infections in one year with rapid relapse after each course, indicating an underlying structural lung disease such as bronchiectasis, immunodeficiency, or poorly controlled COPD requiring CT chest, immunoglobulin levels, and pulmonologist review.

Doctor_English: Does the pain feel exactly the same every time, or does it vary in character between episodes?
Patient_Yoruba: Irora mi máa ń yàtọ̀. Ní ìgbà-ìgbà ó dùn bíi gún, ní ìgbà mìíràn ó dùn bíi ìtẹ̀wọ́. Èyí ń ṣeni lẹ́nu mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Variable pain character between episodes including both stabbing and pressure-like qualities, suggesting two concurrent pain sources or a single source with variable referred pain patterns, requiring a systematic pain diary and specialist assessment.

Doctor_English: Has your appetite changed recently — are you eating more or less than usual?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìfẹ́kúfẹ̀ẹ́ mi ti pọ̀ sí i ní oṣù mẹ́fà. Mo jẹ lọ́pọ̀lọpọ̀ ṣùgbọ́n mo ń tẹ̀nìí síwájú sí i. Ó ń jẹ mí ìbànújẹ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Paradoxical weight loss despite polyphagia in a patient with increased appetite, the classic presentation of uncontrolled hyperthyroidism or type 1 diabetes where caloric intake cannot compensate for hypermetabolic state or urinary glucose loss respectively.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any problems with your memory that are affecting your work or daily life?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìgbàgbé mi ti ń burú sí i. Mo ti gbàgbé àpéjọ pàtàkì mẹ́ta ní oṣù kan àti gbàgbé orúkọ àwọn alabára mi tuntun. O ń nípa lórí iṣẹ́ mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Occupationally significant memory impairment with forgotten appointments and inability to recall new names, exceeding normal forgetting and requiring neuropsychological testing, thyroid function, B12, folate, and neuroimaging to identify a treatable cause.

Doctor_English: Has the child been tested for worms recently, and do they play in soil or sand regularly?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọmọ mi ń ṣeré nínú iyẹ̀lẹ̀ ni ọ̀sẹ̀-ọ̀sẹ̀. A kò fún un ni oògùn ìjẹ fún ọdún kan. Ó ń gbá ìdí rẹ̀ ní alẹ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Soil exposure without recent anthelmintic treatment and nocturnal perianal pruritis in a child, indicating active helminth infestation requiring a single dose of mebendazole or albendazole and household deworming.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any difficulty concentrating during prayers or religious activities?
Patient_Yoruba: Ní ìpàdé ìjọ, inú mi ń sá lọ. Mo kò lè tẹ̀síwájú gbàdúrà. Ó ń ṣe mí ẹlẹ́yà nítorí ìgbàgbọ́ mi ṣe pàtàkì fún mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Concentration impairment affecting religious practice, a domain of spiritual wellbeing often not enquired about in routine consultations, which may reflect depression, anxiety, cognitive decline, or medication side effects requiring a holistic biopsychosocial spiritual assessment.

Doctor_English: Has your child's teacher ever raised concerns about behaviour, attention, or learning?
Patient_Yoruba: Olùkọ́ ọmọ mi ti pè mi lẹ́ẹ̀mẹ́fà nítorí ọmọ mi ń ṣiṣẹ́ nínú kíláàsì, kò sì lè jókòó dídúró. Ó ń hùwà bí ẹni tò ń jóná ní gbogbo ìgbà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Teacher-reported inattention, hyperactivity, and impulsivity on six occasions, meeting the cross-setting diagnostic threshold for ADHD, requiring formal Conners rating scales from both teacher and parent, developmental history, and ADHD specialist assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you been drinking alcohol to help you sleep, and how many units do you have before bed?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo máa ń mu ọtí ní alẹ́ láti ràn mí lọ́wọ́ láti sùn. Mo máa ń mu ìkòkò méjì tàbí mẹ́ta ṣáájú ìsùn. O ti ń pọ̀ sí i.
Clinical_Translation_English: Escalating alcohol use as a sleep aid with two to three bottles nightly, indicating alcohol-dependent insomnia where alcohol disrupts sleep architecture causing rebound insomnia on cessation, perpetuating a dependence cycle requiring CBT-I and graduated alcohol reduction.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any new freckles or spots appearing on your face or hands as you age?
Patient_Yoruba: Àwọn ìwọ̀ dúdú tuntun ti fara hàn lórí ọwọ́ mi àti ojú mi ní ọdún mẹ́ta. Wọ́n kéré ṣùgbọ́n wọ́n ń pọ̀ sí i ní ìgbà-ìgbà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Multiple new pigmented lesions appearing over three years on photo-exposed areas, requiring dermoscopic evaluation to differentiate solar lentigines from early melanoma, seborrhoeic keratoses, or lentigo maligna, particularly in patients with significant sun exposure history.

Doctor_English: Have you been told your vitamin D level is low, and are you taking any supplements?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìdánwò mi fihàn pé Vitamin D mi kéré ní ọdún kan sẹ́yìn. Dọ́kítà fún mi ní àwọn oògùn ṣùgbọ́n mo dáwọ̀ mu lẹ́yìn oṣù mẹ́ta nítorí owó.
Clinical_Translation_English: Confirmed vitamin D deficiency with supplementation self-discontinued after three months due to cost, leaving musculoskeletal symptoms and immune dysregulation potentially uncorrected, requiring cost-effective supplementation alternatives and monitoring of bone health.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any problems with swallowing your tablets, such as them getting stuck?
Patient_Yoruba: Àwọn tábùlẹ́tì mi máa ń dúró ní ọfun mi nígbà-ìgbà tí kò sì rọ sísàlẹ̀. Mo ń lo omi ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ ṣùgbọ́n ó ṣì ń ṣẹlẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Tablet dysphagia with pill-trapping in the oesophagus, a risk factor for pill-induced oesophagitis particularly with bisphosphonates, NSAIDs, and potassium, requiring swallowing assessment, standing upright during ingestion, and consideration of liquid formulations.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed that your symptoms are worse in winter and better in summer?
Patient_Yoruba: Àárẹ̀ mi àti ìbànújẹ́ mi máa ń burú síiwájú sí i ní ìgbà òtútù. Ní ìgbà òoru, mo máa ń nímọ̀lára tó dára. Ó ti jẹ́ bẹ́ẹ̀ fún ọdún mẹ́fà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Seasonal pattern of depression and fatigue worsening in winter and remitting in summer over six years, consistent with seasonal affective disorder, requiring light therapy, psychotherapy, and consideration of antidepressant pharmacotherapy during winter months.

Doctor_English: Has anyone ever accused you of exaggerating your symptoms, and how has this made you feel?
Patient_Yoruba: Àwọn dọ́kítà méjì sọ pé kò sí nǹkan tó ń ṣe mi, pé irora mi wà ní orí mi. O ń ṣe mí ìbànújẹ́ àti inú mi bí. Mo mọ̀ pé àrùn mi jẹ gidi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Medical gaslighting experience with prior dismissal of genuine symptoms, eroding therapeutic trust and potentially delaying diagnosis of a functional or medically unexplained condition, requiring validation, systematic investigation, and a patient-centred collaborative diagnostic approach.

Doctor_English: Have you been told your thyroid is overactive, and are you on any treatment for it?
Patient_Yoruba: A sọ fún mi pé tairọ́ọ̀dì mi ṣiṣẹ́ jù ní ọdún méjì sẹ́yìn. Mo bẹ̀rẹ̀ oògùn ṣùgbọ́n mo dáwọ̀ nítorí àwọn àmì tó ń ṣe mí. Àwọn àmì padà báyìí.
Clinical_Translation_English: Hyperthyroidism with self-discontinued antithyroid medication due to side effects and now symptomatic relapse, requiring thyroid function recheck, consideration of alternative antithyroid drug, radioiodine ablation, or surgical thyroidectomy for definitive management.

Doctor_English: Have you been taking ibuprofen or other anti-inflammatory tablets regularly for a long time?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo mu ibuprofen ní gbogbo ọjọ́ fún ọdún méjì fún irora ẹ̀gbẹ̀ mi. Mi ò mọ̀ pé ó lè ṣe ikùn mi ìpalara. Inú mi ń ṣe mí ní báyìí.
Clinical_Translation_English: Chronic daily NSAID use for two years with emerging gastrointestinal symptoms, indicating NSAID-induced peptic ulceration or gastritis requiring immediate NSAID cessation or substitution with a COX-2 inhibitor plus proton pump inhibitor, and H. pylori testing.

Doctor_English: Have you had any bone or joint infections in the past, and were they treated with a long course of antibiotics?
Patient_Yoruba: Eegun ẹsẹ̀ mi ní àkóràn ní ọdún mẹ́fà sẹ́yìn. Mo gba oògùn IV fún ọ̀sẹ̀ mẹ́fà ní ilé ìwòsàn. O sàn ṣùgbọ́n irora ṣì wà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Prior osteomyelitis treated with intravenous antibiotics with persistent residual pain, raising concern for chronic osteomyelitis with sequestrum formation or avascular necrosis at the affected site, requiring MRI and bone biopsy to exclude active recurrence.

Doctor_English: Has the problem been there since you had an operation, injection, or procedure done?
Patient_Yoruba: Irora bẹ̀rẹ̀ lẹ́yìn abẹ́rẹ̀ ní ẹ̀gbẹ́ ẹ̀hin mi ní ọdún méjì sẹ́yìn. Dọ́kítà sọ pé ó jẹ́ abẹ́rẹ̀ àtọ̀gbẹ̀ ẹ̀gbẹ̀ ẹ̀hin. Irora kò lọ rárá.
Clinical_Translation_English: Persistent pain following an epidural steroid injection two years ago, which may indicate post-procedural complications including epidural haematoma, infection, arachnoiditis, or failure of the underlying disc disease to respond, requiring MRI spine and pain specialist review.

Doctor_English: Do you ever feel that your illness has taken over your identity, and that you are more than just a patient?
Patient_Yoruba: Ní ìgbà-ìgbà, mo ń jẹ pé gbogbo ẹni mọ̀ mi gẹ́gẹ́ bí ẹni tó ṣàìsàn nìkan. Mo ti jẹ́ olùkọ́, ìyá, àti ọ̀rẹ́. Mo fẹ́ gbà àwọn ìdánimọ̀ wọnni padà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Illness-dominated identity with expressed desire to reclaim occupational, parental, and social roles, indicating the need for a rehabilitation and return-to-function approach that goes beyond biomedical symptom management to support psychological recovery and social reintegration.

Doctor_English: Have you been given information about a clinical trial or research study for your condition?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà kan béèrè bóyá mo fẹ́ kópa nínú ìwádìí kan fún àrùn mi. Mo fẹ́ mọ̀ sí i ṣáájú kí n tó pinnu. Mo kò ní ìtọ́níṣọ̀ tó pọ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Potential clinical trial participant seeking information before consenting, requiring full disclosure of trial objectives, randomisation, placebo use, withdrawal rights, and data protection under ICH-GCP guidelines, with written information provided in Yoruba if possible.

Doctor_English: Have you had any imaging done recently such as an MRI, CT scan, or ultrasound, and have you been given the results?
Patient_Yoruba: A ṣe CT ìdánwò fún mi ní oṣù méjì sẹ́yìn. Wọ́n sọ pé a máa ń sọ àbájáde fún mi ṣùgbọ́n kò sí ẹni tó pè mi. Mo ń dúró àti ń yọ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Unreported CT scan result two months after imaging with the patient waiting anxiously, constituting a significant duty of care breach requiring immediate result disclosure, apology for the delay, and a clear plan for any further investigation or management required.

Doctor_English: Have you ever had an adverse reaction in hospital, such as a medication error or a fall during admission?
Patient_Yoruba: Ní ìpàdédè ìgbẹ́yìn mi, wọ́n fún mi ní oògùn tó yàtọ̀ sí ti mi. Mo ṣubú tún lẹ́yìn tí wọ́n fún mi ní oògùn orun tó pọ̀. A kò kọ ẹ̀kọ́ àbájáde kankan.
Clinical_Translation_English: Two adverse events during a prior admission including a wrong medication and an oversedation-related fall without documented incident reporting, indicating a patient safety reporting culture failure requiring retrospective review and patient apology under a duty of candour framework.

Doctor_English: Have you been receiving any home visits from a nurse or healthcare worker, and has this been helpful?
Patient_Yoruba: Nọ̀ọ̀sì abúlé máa ń wá ilé mi lẹ́ẹ̀mẹ́ta ní oṣù. O máa ń ṣe àyẹ̀wò àti fún mi ní ìmọ̀. Ó ràn mí lọ́wọ̀ gan-an ṣùgbọ́n ó ti dáwọ̀ wá fún oṣù méjì.
Clinical_Translation_English: Community nursing visits discontinued for two months with patient finding them valuable, representing a gap in community care continuity for a housebound or complex patient requiring service restoration referral and community matron involvement.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any difficulty breathing through your nose, or has your sense of smell changed?
Patient_Yoruba: Imu mi ọ̀tún ti dí fún oṣù mẹ́fà. Mi ò ń gbọ́ òórùn dáradára mọ́. Ó ń nípa lórí ìtọ́ oúnjẹ mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Unilateral nasal obstruction with anosmia and altered taste over six months, requiring nasal endoscopy to exclude nasal polyps, deviated septum, or unilateral intranasal mass including rare sinonasal malignancy.

Doctor_English: Have you been told your iron is low, and are you a vegetarian or do you avoid red meat?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo jẹ oúnjẹ tí kò ní ẹran fún ọdún mẹ́ta. Dọ́kítà sọ pé irin mi kéré. Mi ò jẹ ẹja pẹ̀lú nítorí ìsìn mi. Mo jẹ ẹyin àti ewé nìkan.
Clinical_Translation_English: Iron deficiency in a strict vegetarian avoiding both meat and fish for three years on religious grounds, indicating inadequate non-haem iron intake requiring dietitian referral for plant-based iron optimisation, vitamin C co-ingestion advice, and oral iron supplementation.

Doctor_English: Has your child ever had an operation or procedure under general anaesthetic, and were there any problems?
Patient_Yoruba: A ṣe iṣẹ́ abẹ fún ọmọ mi fún àmì tonsil rẹ̀ ní ọdún méjì sẹ́yìn. Ó jí déédéé lẹ́yìn àánì. Kò sí ìṣòro kankan.
Clinical_Translation_English: Uncomplicated tonsillectomy under general anaesthesia two years ago, relevant as paediatric anaesthetic history for any future procedure planning, confirming previous normal airway, recovery, and absence of prior adverse anaesthetic reactions.

Doctor_English: Have you been using any pain patches, gels, or topical creams for your pain, and are they helping?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ń lo ìpàrà irora lórí orúkún mi ní ọjọ́-ọjọ́ fún oṣù mẹ́fà. Ó ń ràn mí lọ́wọ́ díẹ̀ ṣùgbọ́n irora ń padà lẹ́yìn wákàtí díẹ̀. Mo nílò ohun tó dára jù.
Clinical_Translation_English: Partially effective topical analgesia for knee osteoarthritis with short duration of action, requiring step-up to intra-articular corticosteroid injection, hyaluronate, or physiotherapy-guided exercise programme alongside topical therapy optimisation.

Doctor_English: Have you had any complications from diabetes, such as changes in your feet, eyes, or kidneys?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà ìdánwò ojú sọ pé ẹ̀jẹ̀ kéékèéké ti fara hàn nínú ojú mi. Ẹsẹ̀ mi tún máa ń hu bíi àbẹ̀rẹ̀. Ìdánwò ẹ̀dọ̀ ito mi ti pẹ́ fún ọdún kan.
Clinical_Translation_English: Multiple diabetic microvascular complications with background diabetic retinopathy, peripheral neuropathy, and overdue nephrology surveillance, indicating advanced diabetes requiring urgent multidisciplinary review including ophthalmology, podiatry, nephrology, and diabetology.

Doctor_English: Have you been told that you need to monitor your blood pressure at home, and are you doing this regularly?
Patient_Yoruba: A fún mi ní ẹrọ ìwọ̀n ẹjẹ̀ ní ọdún kan sẹ́yìn ṣùgbọ́n mi ò lo rẹ̀ rárá. Kò sí ẹni tó kọ́ mi bí a ṣe ń lo rẹ̀. Ó ṣì wà nínú àpótí rẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Home blood pressure monitor prescribed but unused due to absence of patient education, requiring blood pressure measurement technique training, twice-daily morning and evening reading protocol, and review of home readings to optimise antihypertensive therapy.

Doctor_English: Have you been experiencing any nipple discharge or breast tenderness that is not related to your menstrual cycle?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọmú mi méjèéjì máa ń dùn mi ní ìgbà-ìgbà tó kò ní ibatan pẹ̀lú oṣù. Kò sí omi tó ń jáde. Ó ti wà fún oṣù mẹ́fà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Non-cyclical bilateral breast tenderness for six months, more consistent with musculoskeletal costochondritis or medication side effect such as antipsychotic-induced hyperprolactinaemia than with malignancy, but requiring clinical examination to confirm the non-suspicious character.

Doctor_English: Do you have any concerns about your heart that you have not yet mentioned?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ń yọ̀ nípa ọkàn mi ṣùgbọ́n kò sí ẹni tó béèrè mi. Ẹ̀gbọ́n mi kú nítorí ọkàn ní ọdún méjì sẹ́yìn. Mo bẹ̀rù.
Clinical_Translation_English: Undisclosed cardiac anxiety following sibling cardiac death, requiring explicit acknowledgement and cardiological reassurance alongside investigation.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any unusual dreams or nightmares that disturb your sleep?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo máa ń lá àlá tó ń ṣeni lẹ́nu nígbà gbogbo fún oṣù mẹ́fà. Àwọn àlá náà jẹ́ nípa àwọn ìṣẹ̀lẹ̀ ìgbàkígbà tí mo ní. Mo máa ń jí pẹ̀lú ọkàn tó ń lu yára.
Clinical_Translation_English: Recurrent traumatic nightmares with hyperarousal on waking for six months consistent with PTSD re-experiencing symptoms requiring trauma-focused CBT and EMDR therapy assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any problems with your balance when standing up quickly from a chair?
Patient_Yoruba: Nígbà tí mo bá jáde nínú àga yára, orí mi máa ń yípo fún ìṣẹ́jú díẹ̀. Ó ti ṣẹlẹ̀ lẹ́ẹ̀mẹ́fà ní ọ̀sẹ̀ kan. Mo bẹ̀rù ìṣubú.
Clinical_Translation_English: Postural dizziness on standing consistent with orthostatic hypotension, requiring lying and standing blood pressure measurement and medication review.

Doctor_English: Has anyone in your family had an aneurysm or a blood vessel that burst in the brain?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìyá mi kú lójijì ní ọjọ́-orí àádọ́ta nítorí ẹjẹ̀ ọpọlọ. Dọ́kítà sọ pé àlọ̀ ọpọlọ rẹ̀ ya. Mo ń yọ̀ pé ó lè ṣẹlẹ̀ sí mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: First-degree family history of subarachnoid haemorrhage placing the patient at threefold increased risk, warranting MR angiography screening and neurovascular specialist referral.

Doctor_English: Have you been told you have a hiatus hernia and have you changed your diet because of it?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà sọ pé apá ìsàlẹ̀ ìṣan jíjẹun mi ti wọlé ikùn. Ìgbóná ń jẹ mi lọ́pọ̀lọpọ̀ ṣùgbọ́n mi ò yí àṣà mi padà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Hiatus hernia with reflux symptoms without lifestyle modification, requiring weight reduction, avoiding late meals, and proton pump inhibitor therapy.

Doctor_English: Have you had any problems with your feet specifically related to your diabetes, such as numbness or sores?
Patient_Yoruba: Ẹsẹ̀ mi méjèéjì kò ní ìmọ̀ tó tó. Ọgbẹ́ kéékèéké wà lórí ìpẹ̀sẹ̀ mi ọ̀tún tó wà fún ọ̀sẹ̀ mẹ́fà tí ìyàwó mi tó rí i.
Clinical_Translation_English: Diabetic neuropathic foot ulcer unnoticed for six weeks due to sensory loss, indicating high amputation risk requiring immediate wound assessment, offloading, and urgent podiatry referral.

Doctor_English: Have you been told you carry the sickle cell trait and have you discussed this with your partner?
Patient_Yoruba: Bẹ́ẹ̀ ni, mo ní àrùn ẹ̀jẹ̀ ààyọ àbọ̀. Ìyàwó mi kò mọ̀ ìpò rẹ̀. A ń gbìyànjú láti bí ọmọ àkọ́kọ́ wa.
Clinical_Translation_English: Sickle cell trait carrier attempting conception with partner of unknown status, requiring urgent partner testing and pre-conception genetic counselling.

Doctor_English: Have you had any operations on your eyes such as cataract surgery or laser treatment?
Patient_Yoruba: A ṣe iṣẹ́ abẹ ojú fún cataracts ní ojú méjèéjì ní ọdún méjì sẹ́yìn. Ṣùgbọ́n ojú mi bẹ̀rẹ̀ sí rọ lẹ́ẹ̀kan sí i ní oṣù mẹ́fà sẹ́yìn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Post-cataract surgery visual deterioration raising concern for posterior capsule opacification or raised intraocular pressure requiring prompt ophthalmological review.

Doctor_English: Have you been experiencing any flushing of your face or chest particularly after eating certain foods?
Patient_Yoruba: Ojú mi àti àyà mi máa ń pupa pẹ̀lú ìgbóná lẹ́yìn tí mo bá jẹ ẹran tàbí tí mo bá mu ọtí. Ó ń pẹ́ fún wákàtí kan.
Clinical_Translation_English: Postprandial flushing triggered by meat and alcohol requiring investigation for carcinoid syndrome, rosacea, and polycythaemia vera with twenty-four hour urinary 5-HIAA and full blood count.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any difficulty walking in a straight line or feeling like the floor is moving?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo máa ń rìn bíi ẹni tó mu ọtí nígbà-ìgbà. Ìlẹ̀ ń dà bíi pé ó ń rọ. Ó le ní ìgbà alẹ́ àti nígbà tó dudu.
Clinical_Translation_English: Oscillopsia with disequilibrium worsening in darkness consistent with bilateral vestibular failure or posterior column dysfunction requiring B12 level and MRI.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any pain or stiffness specifically in your jaw when you wake up in the morning?
Patient_Yoruba: Ẹrẹ̀kẹ́ mi di jáko ní gbogbo owúrọ̀ fún ìṣẹ́jú ogún sí ọgọ́fà. Mo ní ìṣòro ṣíṣí ẹnu mi nígbà yẹn. Ó ti ń ṣẹlẹ̀ fún oṣù mẹ́fà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Morning jaw stiffness with restricted mouth opening consistent with temporomandibular joint dysfunction requiring dental splint therapy and physiotherapy.

Doctor_English: Have you been told you have a thyroid nodule and has anyone recommended a biopsy?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà ojú omi rí nǹkan nínú ọrùn mi ní ọdún kan sẹ́yìn. Wọ́n sọ pé o jẹ́ nodule ṣùgbọ́n kò ṣe ìdánwò mọ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Thyroid nodule without biopsy follow-up requiring repeat ultrasound with TIRADS scoring and fine needle aspiration cytology if criteria are met.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any difficulty passing urine since your prostate surgery?
Patient_Yoruba: Lẹ́yìn iṣẹ́ abẹ prọ́stetì mi ní ọdún méjì sẹ́yìn, ito mi di àìtó. Mo ń ṣe ito ní tá tá tá pẹ̀lú ìgbọ̀. Ó ti ń burú sí i.
Clinical_Translation_English: Post-prostatectomy urinary symptoms indicating possible urethral stricture or sphincter weakness requiring uroflowmetry and urology review.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any lumps in your groin that are painful and red?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìdì mi ọsì ti wú, pupa, ó sì gbóná ní ọjọ́ méjì. Irona le gan-an. Mo ní ibà pẹ̀lú.
Clinical_Translation_English: Acute tender erythematous inguinal lymphadenopathy with fever consistent with suppurative lymphadenitis requiring antibiotic therapy and incision if fluctuant.

Doctor_English: Have you been experiencing recurrent boils or skin abscesses and have they been cultured?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ti ní ọgbẹ́ tó ń jẹ̀ ẹjẹ̀ lórí ìdí mi àti ìhà àyà mi lẹ́ẹ̀mẹ́fà ní ọdún kan. Kò sí ẹni tó ṣe ìdánwò nínú wọn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Recurrent furunculosis without microbiological culture raising concern for MRSA colonisation, requiring nasal swab and fasting glucose for diabetes.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any bleeding or spotting after the menopause?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ti pari oṣù fún ọdún mẹ́fà ṣùgbọ́n ẹjẹ̀ kéékèéké jáde ní oṣù méjì sẹ́yìn. Mo bẹ̀rù. Ko sí irora.
Clinical_Translation_English: Postmenopausal bleeding constituting an endometrial carcinoma red flag requiring urgent transvaginal ultrasound and hysteroscopy with biopsy.

Doctor_English: Have you been having headaches that are worse when you cough or sneeze?
Patient_Yoruba: Orí mi máa ń fọ̀ lẹ́sẹ̀kẹsẹ̀ nígbà tí mo bá gbẹ̀ ikọ́ tàbí jáde ìgbẹ́. Irora ń wáyé ní ẹ̀yìn orí mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Valsalva-provoked occipital headache suggesting Chiari malformation requiring MRI brain and cervical spine and neurosurgery referral.

Doctor_English: Have you been told you are anaemic during pregnancy and are you on iron supplements?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà ìbímọ sọ pé ẹjẹ̀ mi kéré ní oṣù kẹfà oyún mi. Ìrọ̀bí irin ń mú ikùn mi ṣòro gan-an. Mo ti dáwọ̀ mu.
Clinical_Translation_English: Third-trimester iron deficiency anaemia with self-discontinued iron due to intolerance, creating foetal growth restriction and peripartum haemorrhage risk.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed that your urine has a very strong sweet smell like nail polish remover?
Patient_Yoruba: Ito mi ní òórùn bíi ọ̀pẹ tàbí olóórun didùn pàtàkì fún ọjọ́ mẹ́ta. Mo tún ń ni ríru àti àárẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Acetone-smelling urine with nausea characteristic of diabetic ketoacidosis requiring immediate blood glucose, ketones, and urgent DKA management.

Doctor_English: Have you been having recurrent mouth ulcers that come back every few weeks?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọgbẹ́ ẹnu mi máa ń wáyé ní ìgbà-ìgbà fún ọdún méjì. Wọn máa ń wáyé lẹ́ẹ̀mẹ́fà ní ọdún. Wọn máa ń dùn fún ọjọ́ mẹ́wàá.
Clinical_Translation_English: Recurrent aphthous stomatitis requiring investigation for ferritin, B12, folate, and coeliac antibodies to identify a treatable nutritional deficiency.

Doctor_English: Have you had any problems with your appetite since starting your new medication?
Patient_Yoruba: Lẹ́yìn tí mo bẹ̀rẹ̀ oògùn tuntun, ìfẹ́kúfẹ̀ẹ́ mi ti lọ pátápátá. Mo jẹun díẹ̀ kan ní ọjọ́ kan. Mo ti tẹ̀nìí ní oṣù kan.
Clinical_Translation_English: Drug-induced anorexia with significant weight loss over one month requiring medication review, dose adjustment, and nutritional assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any itching all over your body that gets worse after a hot bath?
Patient_Yoruba: Ín-ín mi ń ṣe mi gbogbo ara lẹ́yìn ìwẹ̀ gbígbóná. Ó bẹ̀rẹ̀ ní ìṣẹ́jú méjì lẹ́yìn ìwẹ̀. Ko sí àpá lórí ara mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Aquagenic pruritus without skin lesions triggered by hot water, a distinctive pattern associated with polycythaemia vera requiring full blood count and JAK2 mutation testing.

Doctor_English: Have you been told your liver is enlarged, and do you know why?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà tẹ ikùn mi ní ọdún kan sẹ́yìn, ó sọ pé ẹ̀dọ̀ mi pọ̀ jù. Kò ṣe ìdánwò mọ́. Mi ò mu ọtí rárá.
Clinical_Translation_English: Hepatomegaly without investigation in a non-drinker requiring liver ultrasound, hepatitis B and C serology, and liver function tests to determine the aetiology.

Doctor_English: Have you been experiencing any visual floaters that have increased suddenly in the past few days?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ti máa ń rí àwọn nǹkan tó ń fò nínú ojú mi ṣùgbọ́n ní ọjọ́ méjì sẹ́yìn, wọ́n pọ̀ sí i lójijì. Ìmọ̀lẹ̀ tún ń yọ̀ ní etí ojú mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Sudden increase in floaters with photopsia constituting an emergency requiring same-day retinal assessment to exclude posterior vitreous detachment complicated by retinal tear.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any problems with your swallowing that make you cough or choke during meals?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo máa ń kúkú nígbà tí mo bá gbé oúnjẹ. Ó ti ṣẹlẹ̀ lẹ́ẹ̀mẹ́fà ní ọ̀sẹ̀ kan. Omi tún ń mú mi kúkú ní ìgbà-ìgbà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Oropharyngeal dysphagia with aspiration on solids and liquids requiring videofluoroscopic swallowing study and nutritional assessment to reduce aspiration pneumonia risk.

Doctor_English: Have you been using your walking aid on the correct side, and has a physiotherapist shown you how?
Patient_Yoruba: Wọ́n fún mi ní ọpá ìrìn lẹ́yìn iṣẹ́ abẹ orúkún mi ṣùgbọ́n kò sí ẹni tó fún mi ní ìtọ́síwájú. Mo máa ń fi ọwọ́ tó ẹsẹ̀ tó ń ṣe mí mu.
Clinical_Translation_English: Walking aid used on the affected side rather than the contralateral side, reducing effectiveness and increasing fall risk, requiring immediate physiotherapy gait re-education.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any new or changing spots on your skin that bleed when you scratch them?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìwọ̀ dúdú kan lórí apá mi ń jẹ̀ ẹjẹ̀ nígbà tí a bá fi àṣọ pa a. Ó ti pọ̀ sí i ní oṣù mẹ́fà. Ó tún di àwọ̀ onírúurú.
Clinical_Translation_English: Bleeding pigmented lesion with growth and colour change over six months fulfilling ABCDE melanoma criteria requiring urgent excision biopsy.

Doctor_English: Have you been told you need a hearing aid, and have you been fitted for one?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà etí sọ pé mo nílò ẹrọ gbígbọ́ ní ọdún kan sẹ́yìn. Kò sí ìtọ́síwájú lẹ́yìn ìdánwò náà. Gbígbọ́ mi ti burú sí i.
Clinical_Translation_English: Prescribed hearing aid without fitting or follow-up over one year with progressive hearing deterioration, requiring audiology recall for hearing aid fitting and aural rehabilitation.

Doctor_English: Have you had any episodes where you suddenly could not move or speak for a few minutes?
Patient_Yoruba: Lẹ́ẹ̀meji, mo wà ní ìpòkò fún ìṣẹ́jú díẹ̀, kò lè sọ ọ̀rọ̀ tàbí gbé apá mi. Ó lọ fúnra rẹ̀ ṣùgbọ́n mo bẹ̀rù gan-an.
Clinical_Translation_English: Transient episodes of speech arrest and limb weakness with complete resolution, constituting TIAs requiring urgent carotid imaging, antiplatelet therapy, and stroke prevention assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you been experiencing any pain during or after sex that has been affecting your relationship?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìbálọpọ̀ ń ṣe mí irora fún oṣù mẹ́fà. Ó ń nípa lórí ìbálọpọ̀ wa. Ọkọ mi mọ̀ ṣùgbọ́n a kò sọ̀rọ̀ nípa rẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Dyspareunia causing relationship strain requiring examination for endometriosis, pelvic inflammatory disease, vaginismus, and atrophic vaginitis with appropriate referral.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any chest pain that occurs specifically after meals and not during exercise?
Patient_Yoruba: Irora àyà mi máa ń wáyé lẹ́yìn oúnjẹ nìkan. Ko ṣẹlẹ̀ nígbà ìdárayá rárá. Ó ń máa ń rọ lẹ́yìn wákàtí kan.
Clinical_Translation_English: Post-prandial chest pain without exertional trigger favouring oesophageal or gastric aetiology over ischaemia, requiring upper endoscopy and oesophageal manometry.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any problems with your memory that your family has noticed before you did?
Patient_Yoruba: Àwọn ọmọ mi sọ pé mo ti gbàgbé àwọn ìṣẹ̀lẹ̀ tuntun. Mo sì tún máa ń beere ìbéèrè kannáà ní ìgbà méjì. Ó bẹ̀rẹ̀ ní ọdún kan sẹ́yìn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Family-observed short-term memory impairment with perseverative questioning over one year, consistent with early Alzheimer's disease requiring MMSE, MoCA, and neuroimaging.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any problems passing urine after your catheter was removed?
Patient_Yoruba: A yọ àpòòtọ́ mi kúrò ní ọjọ́ méjì sẹ́yìn ṣùgbọ́n mi ò tíì ṣe ito rárá. Ó ń ṣe mí irora ní ìsàlẹ̀ ikùn mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Urinary retention post-catheter removal with lower abdominal pain requiring bladder scan for post-void residual and urological assessment for urethral stricture or detrusor areflexia.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any new or worsening pain since you started taking a new medication?
Patient_Yoruba: Lẹ́yìn tí mo bẹ̀rẹ̀ oògùn tuntun fún ẹjẹ̀ mi, irora iṣan ara bẹ̀rẹ̀ ní àwọn ẹsẹ̀ mi méjèéjì. O le ní alẹ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Statin-induced myalgia with bilateral leg pain requiring creatine kinase measurement and consideration of dose reduction or switch to an alternative statin.

Doctor_English: Have you been experiencing any recurrent nosebleeds particularly when the weather is dry or hot?
Patient_Yoruba: Imu mi máa ń ṣàn ní ìgbà ooru àti ìgbà tí ojú ọjọ́ bá gbóná. Ó ti ṣẹlẹ̀ lẹ́ẹ̀mẹ́fà ní oṣù kan. O máa ń dúró lẹ́yìn ìṣẹ́jú mẹ́wàá.
Clinical_Translation_English: Seasonal epistaxis triggered by hot dry weather requiring anterior rhinoscopy for Kiesselbach plexus cauterisation and humidification advice alongside blood pressure and coagulation check.

Doctor_English: Have you been told you have a peptic ulcer and are you taking treatment for it?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà sọ pé ọgbẹ́ wà nínú ikùn mi ní ọdún méjì sẹ́yìn. A fún mi ní oògùn fún oṣù méjì ṣùgbọ́n mi ò tẹ̀lé ìdánwò ti a ní ṣe.
Clinical_Translation_English: Peptic ulcer without post-treatment H. pylori eradication confirmation, creating relapse risk, requiring urea breath test and endoscopic review if symptoms persist.

Doctor_English: Have you been experiencing any unusual hair growth on your chin or upper lip as a woman?
Patient_Yoruba: Irun ti bẹ̀rẹ̀ sí hù lórí abẹ́ ẹnu mi àti aláàárín ẹ̀gbẹ́ ẹ̀gbẹ́ mi ní ọdún kan sẹ́yìn. Mo tún ní àìdára oṣù.
Clinical_Translation_English: Hirsutism with menstrual irregularity suggesting polycystic ovarian syndrome with hyperandrogenism requiring testosterone, LH, FSH, and pelvic ultrasound.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any pain in the back of your heel that gets worse when you push off to walk?
Patient_Yoruba: Ẹ̀hin ìpẹ̀sẹ̀ mi ọ̀tún ń dùn nígbà tí mo bá gbé ẹsẹ̀ mi ga láti rìn. Ó burú sí i lẹ́yìn ìdárayá. O ti wà fún oṣù mẹ́fà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Posterior heel pain worsening with push-off consistent with Achilles tendinopathy requiring eccentric loading physiotherapy and load management.

Doctor_English: Have you been told that you have a spine problem such as a slipped disc or spinal stenosis?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà sọ pé eegun ẹ̀hin mi kan ti rọ ní ọdún méjì sẹ́yìn. Wọ́n fún mi ní oògùn ṣùgbọ́n ko ràn mí lọ́wọ́. Irona tún wà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Lumbar disc prolapse with persistent pain despite medical management requiring physiotherapy assessment, pain specialist referral, and consideration of epidural steroid injection or surgical discectomy.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any episodes of intense burning or pain in your feet at night?
Patient_Yoruba: Ẹsẹ̀ mi méjèéjì máa ń jó bíi iná ní alẹ́. Ó ń jí mi dìde ní àárọ̀. Mo máa ń bọ̀ wọ́n sínú omi tutù láti gba ìtura.
Clinical_Translation_English: Burning foot pain requiring cold water immersion at night, characteristic of small fibre neuropathy from diabetes, alcoholism, or B12 deficiency requiring nerve conduction studies and skin punch biopsy.

Doctor_English: Have you been told your prostate specific antigen is raised and have you had a biopsy?
Patient_Yoruba: PSA mi ga ní ọdún kan sẹ́yìn. Dọ́kítà sọ pé kí n padà wá ṣùgbọ́n mi ò lọ. Mo bẹ̀rù àbájáde.
Clinical_Translation_English: Elevated PSA without prostate biopsy follow-up due to fear, creating risk of delayed prostate cancer diagnosis, requiring reassurance, restaging PSA, and urology referral for MRI-targeted biopsy consideration.

Doctor_English: Have you been experiencing any excessive daytime sleepiness that makes it hard to stay awake at work?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo máa ń sùn ní ọjọ́ ní iṣẹ́ àti nígbà ìpàdé. Olórí iṣẹ́ mi ti ṣàkíyèsí. Ó ń bani lóru mi gan-an.
Clinical_Translation_English: Excessive daytime somnolence causing occupational impairment requiring Epworth Sleepiness Scale scoring, investigation for obstructive sleep apnoea, and metabolic causes including hypothyroidism.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any confusion or disorientation that is worse at certain times of day?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìbí ọmọ tuntun mi jẹ́ ní ọdún mẹ́fà sẹ́yìn. Àgbàdọ̀ mi di àásìkí gan-an ní alẹ́ ṣùgbọ́n ó mọ àwọn ọmọ rẹ̀ ní ọ̀sán.
Clinical_Translation_English: Nocturnal delirium with preserved daytime orientation consistent with sundowning in dementia requiring environmental modification, light therapy, and pharmacological management if behavioural symptoms are severe.

Doctor_English: Have you been given an appointment for a colonoscopy and were you able to attend?
Patient_Yoruba: A fún mi ní ọjọ́ ìpàdé fún ìdánwò ìfun ní ọdún kan sẹ́yìn ṣùgbọ́n mi ò lọ nítorí mo bẹ̀rù àgbámọ̀ náà. Ẹjẹ̀ ṣì ń jáde nínú ìgbẹ́ mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Missed colonoscopy for rectal bleeding due to procedural fear, requiring reassurance about comfort measures, sedation options, and rescheduling as a cancer exclusion priority.

Doctor_English: Have you been experiencing any pains or stiffness that are worse after sitting for long periods?
Patient_Yoruba: Lẹ́yìn jíjókòó fún wákàtí mẹ́fà ní ọfiisi, ẹ̀hin mi àti ìbàdí mi di jáko. Ó máa ń yẹ̀ lẹ́yìn ìrìn kéékèéké. O ti ń ṣẹlẹ̀ fún ọdún kan.
Clinical_Translation_English: Prolonged sitting-induced lumbar stiffness relieved by movement, consistent with mechanical low back pain and hip flexor tightness requiring ergonomic workstation assessment and physiotherapy.

Doctor_English: Have you been taking your diabetes medication before or after food as instructed?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo máa ń mu oògùn suga mi ní àkókò tí mo bá rántí. Ní ìgbà-ìgbà ṣáájú oúnjẹ, ní ìgbà mìíràn lẹ́yìn. Mi ò mọ̀ ìyàtọ̀ rẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Irregular metformin timing without understanding of food relationship, creating suboptimal absorption and increased gastrointestinal side effects, requiring patient education on consistent administration with meals.

Doctor_English: Have you ever been told you have a heart rhythm problem that runs in your family?
Patient_Yoruba: Bàbá mi kú lójijì ní ọjọ́-orí ogójì. Ẹ̀gbọ́n mi méjì tún ní ìṣòro ọkàn. Dọ́kítà sọ pé o lè jẹ́ àrùn ìdílé.
Clinical_Translation_English: Family history of sudden cardiac death and arrhythmia suggesting an inherited channelopathy such as long QT syndrome or Brugada syndrome requiring ECG, genetic testing, and cardiology assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any difficulty recognising familiar places or getting lost in areas you know well?
Patient_Yoruba: Ní ọjọ́ méjì sẹ́yìn, mo sọnù nínú ọjà tí mo ti ń lọ fún ọdún mẹ́wàá. Mo bẹ̀rù àti mi ò mọ̀ bí mo ṣe padà sílé. Ó jẹ́ ìgbà àkọ́kọ́ rẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Topographical disorientation in a familiar environment for the first time, indicating moderate Alzheimer's disease with visuospatial and navigation deficits requiring urgent cognitive assessment and safety review.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any recurrent vomiting first thing in the morning before you eat anything?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo máa ń ègbọn ní owúrọ̀ ṣáájú jíjẹun fún oṣù méjì. Ko sí ríru ṣáájú. Ó máa ń dára lẹ́yìn ìṣẹ́jú ọgọ́fà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Morning vomiting without nausea in a woman of reproductive age requiring pregnancy test, alongside consideration of central causes including raised intracranial pressure and vestibular disease.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any change in the shape or size of your abdomen on one side only?
Patient_Yoruba: Ẹ̀gbẹ́ ọ̀tún ikùn mi ti wú díẹ̀ síwájú sí i fún oṣù mẹ́fà. Ko dùn ṣùgbọ́n ó hàn nígbà tí mo bá wò inú dígí.
Clinical_Translation_English: Unilateral progressive abdominal asymmetry over six months without pain requiring abdominal CT to exclude hepatomegaly, splenomegaly, renal mass, or retroperitoneal tumour.

Doctor_English: Have you been told your blood sugar control has been poor and your HbA1c is very high?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìdánwò HbA1c mi wà ní ọgọ́fà àti méjì ní ọjọ́ mẹ́ta sẹ́yìn. Dọ́kítà sọ pé ó buru jù. Mo ń gbìyànjú ṣùgbọ́n owó oògùn pọ̀ jù.
Clinical_Translation_English: HbA1c of sixty-two indicating uncontrolled diabetes with financial barriers to medication adherence requiring social prescribing, medication cost review, and insulin initiation consideration.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any cramping in your legs that prevents you from sleeping well?
Patient_Yoruba: Iṣan ara ẹsẹ̀ mi méjèéjì máa ń gbọn ní alẹ́ tó ń jí mi. O ń dúró fún ìṣẹ́jú mẹ́wàá. Sísun ẹsẹ̀ ń ràn mí lọ́wọ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Nocturnal leg cramps requiring exclusion of electrolyte imbalance, peripheral artery disease, and medication causes before recommending stretching exercises and magnesium supplementation.

Doctor_English: Have you been told you have an underactive thyroid, and are you taking the correct dose of medication?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ní hypothyroidism fún ọdún mẹ́fà. Mo ń mu levothyroxine ṣùgbọ́n àárẹ̀ mi ṣì wà. Kò sí ìdánwò TSH mọ́ fún ọdún méjì.
Clinical_Translation_English: Undertreated hypothyroidism with persistent fatigue and two years without TSH monitoring requiring thyroid function testing and levothyroxine dose titration.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any pain specifically in your big toe joint that starts suddenly at night?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìpín ọwọ́ orùka ẹsẹ̀ mi ọ̀tún máa ń dùn lójijì ní alẹ́ tó ń jí mi dìde. O máa ń pupa àti wú. Ó lọ fúnra rẹ̀ ní ọjọ́ mẹ́fà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Podagra with nocturnal first metatarsophalangeal joint pain, redness, and swelling resolving spontaneously over six days, the pathognomonic presentation of acute gout requiring serum urate and dietary modification.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any difficulty with reading or writing since your stroke?
Patient_Yoruba: Lẹ́yìn ìjàǹbá ọpọlọ mi, kíkà àti kíkọ ti ṣòro. Mo lè kà àwọn ọ̀rọ̀ kéékèéké ṣùgbọ́n ìwé pípẹ́ jẹ mí lóru. O ti yẹ̀ díẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Post-stroke alexia and dysgraphia with partial recovery requiring speech and language therapy assessment and cognitive rehabilitation for written language processing.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any unexpected weight gain despite not eating more than usual?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ti pọ̀ sí i ní oṣù mẹ́fà bí ó tilẹ̀ jẹ́ pé mi ò jẹ sí i. Àwọ̀ ara mi tún ti yí padà. Mo ní àárẹ̀ àti tutu gbogbo ìgbà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Unexplained weight gain with cold intolerance and fatigue consistent with hypothyroidism requiring TSH and free T4 measurement.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any difficulty swallowing pills or tablets due to a dry mouth?
Patient_Yoruba: Ẹnu mi gbẹ gan-an fún oṣù mẹ́fà. Mo ń ní ìṣòro gbígbé tábùlẹ́tì. Ó ti ń nípa lórí gbígbọ̀ àti jíjẹun mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Xerostomia causing swallowing difficulty and pill dysphagia, a common side effect of anticholinergic medications, diuretics, and Sjögren's syndrome, requiring medication review and saliva substitute prescription.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any blood in your vomit that looks like coffee grounds?
Patient_Yoruba: Ohun tí mo ègbọn ní àwọ̀ ilẹ̀ kọfì ní ìgbà kan ní ọjọ́ méjì sẹ́yìn. Ó jẹ́ ìgbà àkọ́kọ́ rẹ̀. Mo bẹ̀rù gan-an.
Clinical_Translation_English: Coffee-ground haematemesis indicating upper gastrointestinal haemorrhage from peptic ulcer or varices requiring immediate resuscitation, urgent endoscopy, and proton pump inhibitor infusion.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any pain that radiates into your groin from your back?
Patient_Yoruba: Irora ẹ̀hin mi ọ̀tún máa ń rọ sínú ìdì mi ọ̀tún. Ó máa ń le ní ìgbà-ìgbà. O ti wà fún ọjọ́ méjì.
Clinical_Translation_English: Right flank to groin radiation consistent with ureteric colic from a renal calculus requiring urgent CT KUB and analgesia with alpha-blocker to facilitate stone passage.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any problems with your vision when driving, particularly at night?
Patient_Yoruba: Ní alẹ́, àwọn ìmọ̀lẹ̀ ọkọ̀ tó ń bọ̀ ń bani lójú mi gan-an. Mo máa ń ríran ṣòòfà tún. Mo ti dáwọ̀ wakọ̀ alẹ́ dúró.
Clinical_Translation_English: Glare sensitivity and reduced night vision requiring assessment for early cataract, corneal disease, or refractive error with urgent ophthalmology review before returning to night driving.

Doctor_English: Have you been told that you have a cyst on your kidney or ovary, and has it been followed up?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìdánwò ojú omi ri àpò omi kéékèéké lórí ẹ̀yìn mi ọ̀sì ní ọdún kan sẹ́yìn. Ko sí ìtẹ̀lé ìdánwò. Mi ò mọ̀ bóyá ó ti pọ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Ovarian cyst without follow-up imaging to assess for growth or malignant transformation, requiring repeat ultrasound and gynaecology assessment using RMI score for malignancy risk stratification.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any burning or tingling in your feet that is worse at night?
Patient_Yoruba: Ẹsẹ̀ mi méjèéjì ń jó àti hu bíi àbẹ̀rẹ̀ ń gún wọn ní alẹ́. Ó máa ń buru sí i ní ìgbà tutù. O ti wà fún ọdún kan.
Clinical_Translation_English: Burning nocturnal paraesthesia in a stocking distribution over one year consistent with peripheral neuropathy from diabetes, B12 deficiency, or alcohol requiring nerve conduction studies and metabolic screen.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any difficulty hearing your phone ring or following conversations in a group?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo máa ń padanu ọ̀rọ̀ nínú ìpàdé tí àwọn ènìyàn pọ̀. Mo tún máa ń padanu ìpè fóònù ní ìgbà-ìgbà. O ti ń burú sí i ní ọdún kan.
Clinical_Translation_English: Progressive high-frequency sensorineural hearing loss affecting speech discrimination in noise, consistent with presbyacusis or noise-induced hearing loss requiring pure tone audiometry and hearing aid fitting.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any unusual tastes or smells that others around you cannot detect?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo máa ń gbọ́ òórùn ìjó tó ń bani lóru ní ìgbà-ìgbà tí kò sí ẹni mìíràn tó gbọ́ rẹ̀. O ń ṣẹlẹ̀ fún oṣù mẹ́ta.
Clinical_Translation_English: Olfactory hallucinations consisting of phantom burning smell, a phenomenon called phantosmia associated with temporal lobe epilepsy, migraine, or nasal pathology requiring EEG and neurological assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you been told you need to monitor your INR, and are you having regular blood tests for this?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ń mu warfarin fún ọdún méjì ṣùgbọ́n mi ò ṣe ìdánwò INR rẹ̀ fún oṣù mẹ́fà. Ó gbọ̀ mi pé ó gbọdọ̀ wà ní ìwọ̀n kan.
Clinical_Translation_English: Warfarin therapy without INR monitoring for six months creating significant under or over-anticoagulation risk with either thrombosis or haemorrhage, requiring immediate INR check and structured anticoagulation clinic enrolment.

Doctor_English: Have you been experiencing any vision changes specifically when looking at straight lines?
Patient_Yoruba: Nígbà tí mo bá wò ẹnu ilẹ̀kùn tàbí ìlà tó tọ, wọn máa ń dà bíi tó wọ́n tàbí ń ìgòkè. O ti bẹ̀rẹ̀ oṣù mẹ́ta sẹ́yìn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Metamorphopsia with distortion of straight lines over three months, the characteristic visual symptom of age-related macular degeneration requiring urgent optical coherence tomography and intravitreal anti-VEGF therapy assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any unusual muscular weakness that comes and goes throughout the day?
Patient_Yoruba: Àìlera iṣan ara mi máa ń burú ní òrùlé ọjọ́ àti lẹ́yìn ìdárayá. Mo máa ń dára lẹ́yìn ìsinmi. O ti ń ṣẹlẹ̀ fún oṣù mẹ́fà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Fatigable diurnal muscle weakness worsening with activity and improving with rest over six months, the pathognomonic pattern of myasthenia gravis requiring acetylcholine receptor antibodies, repetitive nerve stimulation, and CT chest for thymoma.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any episodes of sudden severe pain in one eye with headache and nausea?
Patient_Yoruba: Ojú ọ̀tún mi ń dùn gan-an lójijì pẹ̀lú orí fífọ̀ àti ríru ní ìgbà méjì ní oṣù kan. O ń pẹ́ fún wákàtí méjì.
Clinical_Translation_English: Episodic severe unilateral eye pain with headache and nausea consistent with acute angle-closure glaucoma requiring same-day tonometry, anterior chamber assessment, and laser iridotomy to prevent permanent visual loss.

Doctor_English: Have you been told you have an irregular heartbeat and do you know what type it is?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà sọ pé ọkàn mi kò ń lu déédéé ní ọdún méjì sẹ́yìn. Ko ṣàlàyé irú rẹ̀. Mo ń mu aspirin nìkan.
Clinical_Translation_English: Undisclosed cardiac arrhythmia without anticoagulation beyond aspirin, potentially atrial fibrillation with inadequate stroke prevention, requiring ECG, Holter monitor, and CHA2DS2-VASc risk scoring for anticoagulant decision-making.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any problems with your appetite or weight since your chemotherapy ended?
Patient_Yoruba: Lẹ́yìn oògùn kẹmíkà mi parí, ìfẹ́kúfẹ̀ẹ́ mi ṣì kéré. Mo ti tẹ̀nìí ní oṣù mẹ́fà. Àárẹ̀ mi tún ṣì wà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Persistent post-chemotherapy anorexia and weight loss with fatigue requiring surveillance for malignancy recurrence, secondary adrenal insufficiency, and nutritional rehabilitation.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any puffiness under your eyes that is worse after eating salty food?
Patient_Yoruba: Àyíká ìsàlẹ̀ ojú mi máa ń wú lẹ́yìn tí mo bá jẹ oúnjẹ tó ní iyọ̀ pọ̀. Ó máa ń lọ ní ọjọ́ tó tẹ̀le. O ti ń ṣẹlẹ̀ fún oṣù mẹ́fà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Salt-sensitive periorbital oedema consistent with early nephrotic syndrome or sodium-sensitive fluid retention requiring urine protein quantification, renal function, and albumin measurement.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any uncontrolled movements of your arms or legs while sitting still?
Patient_Yoruba: Ẹsẹ̀ mi méjèéjì máa ń gúnjú lórí ara wọn nígbà tí mo bá jókòó tí kò ní ìfẹ́ mi. O ń ṣẹlẹ̀ ní àwọn ìpàdé àti nígbà ìsùn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Involuntary lower limb movements at rest and during sleep consistent with restless legs syndrome requiring iron studies, renal function, and consideration of dopamine agonist therapy.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any problems controlling your emotions, crying or laughing at unexpected times?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo máa ń sọkún tàbí rẹ́rìn-ín lójijì laìsí ìdí tó yẹ. O ń ṣẹlẹ̀ ní iṣẹ́ tó ń jẹ ẹlẹ́yà mi. O ti ń ṣẹlẹ̀ fún oṣù mẹ́fà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Pseudobulbar affect with involuntary emotional expression disorder requiring assessment for multiple sclerosis, stroke, motor neurone disease, or traumatic brain injury, with dextromethorphan-quinidine as pharmacotherapy.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any difficulty breathing specifically when lying on your back?
Patient_Yoruba: Mí mi kúrú nígbà tí mo bá dùbúlẹ̀ pẹ̀tẹ́lẹ̀ ṣùgbọ́n dára nígbà tí mo bá jókòó. Mo ń lo ìrọ̀lẹ́ mẹ́ta láti sùn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Orthopnoea requiring three pillows with supine dyspnoea and sitting relief, the classic features of left ventricular failure with pulmonary oedema requiring urgent echocardiography and diuretic therapy.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any recurrent infections in your chest that keep coming back after antibiotics?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ti mu oògùn àkóràn fún àrùn àyà lẹ́ẹ̀mẹ́fà ní ọdún kan. Gbogbo wọn ràn mí lọ́wọ́ ṣùgbọ́n ó máa ń padà ní ọ̀sẹ̀ díẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Recurrent pulmonary infections with rapid post-antibiotic relapse suggesting bronchiectasis, immunoglobulin deficiency, or structural lung disease requiring HRCT chest and immunology screen.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any change in how much you sweat, either sweating more or less than before?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ti dáwọ̀ gbó bíi ìgbà àárọ̀ mi. Ara mi gbẹ gan-an ní ìgbà ooru. O ti ń ṣẹlẹ̀ fún oṣù mẹ́fà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Anhidrosis over six months raising concern for autonomic neuropathy from diabetes, peripheral nerve disease, or Horner's syndrome, requiring autonomic function testing and thermoregulatory sweat test.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any pressure in your ears or muffled hearing after a flight?
Patient_Yoruba: Lẹ́yìn ìrìn-àjò ọkọ̀ òfuurufú, etí mi méjèéjì ti kún àti gbígbọ́ di àìtó. Ó ti wà fún ọjọ́ mẹ́ta. Gbígbọ̀ kò ràn mí lọ́wọ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Barotrauma from Eustachian tube dysfunction after air travel causing conductive hearing loss requiring autoinflation manoeuvres, decongestants, and ENT review if symptoms persist beyond two weeks.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any pain under your right shoulder blade after eating a heavy meal?
Patient_Yoruba: Ẹ̀hin ìhà ọ̀tún mi ń dùn lẹ́yìn oúnjẹ tó lera. Ó ń tan sínú èjìká mi ọ̀tún. O ti ń ṣẹlẹ̀ fún oṣù méjì.
Clinical_Translation_English: Right subscapular pain after meals with shoulder radiation consistent with biliary colic from cholelithiasis, the referred pain pathway via the right phrenic nerve, requiring abdominal ultrasound and surgical cholecystectomy referral.

Doctor_English: Have you been taking your blood pressure tablets at the same time every day?
Patient_Yoruba: Mi ò mu oògùn mi ní àkókò kannáà ní ọjọ́-ọjọ́. Ní ìgbà-ìgbà mo gbàgbé pátápátá. O ti ń ṣẹlẹ̀ ní gbogbo ọ̀sẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Irregular antihypertensive dosing causing blood pressure variability, which is independently associated with cardiovascular events, requiring education on consistent timing and pill organiser or alarm reminder strategies.

Doctor_English: Have you been experiencing any stiffness in your fingers that prevents you from making a fist in the morning?
Patient_Yoruba: Àwọn ìpín ọwọ́ mi méjèéjì di jáko ní owúrọ̀ fún ìṣẹ́jú àádọ́ta. Mo kò lè di ọwọ́ mi mú. Ó máa ń yẹ̀ lẹ́yìn ìgbésẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Prolonged morning finger stiffness lasting fifty minutes with grip weakness, the hallmark inflammatory pattern of early rheumatoid arthritis, requiring urgent rheumatoid factor, anti-CCP antibody, and rheumatology referral.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any new floaters or a curtain across your vision?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo rí àwọn nǹkan tuntun tó ń fò nínú ojú mi ọ̀tún àti ìhà ìsàlẹ̀ ojú mi di òkùnkùn. O wáyé lójijì ní àárọ̀ ọjọ́ kẹrin.
Clinical_Translation_English: Sudden floaters with an inferior visual field defect constituting the retinal detachment emergency triad requiring same-day ophthalmological assessment and surgical intervention.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any problems with your kidneys since starting your new pain medication?
Patient_Yoruba: Lẹ́yìn tí mo bẹ̀rẹ̀ oògùn NSAID tuntun, ẹsẹ̀ mi bẹ̀rẹ̀ sí wú àti ito mi dínkù. O ti ń ṣẹlẹ̀ fún ọ̀sẹ̀ méjì.
Clinical_Translation_English: NSAID-induced acute kidney injury manifesting as oedema and oliguria requiring immediate drug cessation, renal function testing, and fluid status assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any pain that wraps around from your back to your front like a belt?
Patient_Yoruba: Irora wà ní ẹ̀hin mi ọsì tó ń yika wá sí iwájú bíi ìgbànú. O le gan-an àti máa ń burú lẹ́yìn jíjẹun.
Clinical_Translation_English: Belt-like epigastric and back pain following meals consistent with acute pancreatitis or pancreatic carcinoma requiring urgent serum amylase, lipase, and CT abdomen.

Doctor_English: Have you been told you have sleep apnoea and were you offered any treatment?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà sọ pé mo ní àrùn mímí ní ìsùn ní ọdún kan sẹ́yìn ṣùgbọ́n ko ṣe àbẹ̀wò mọ́. Mi ò gba oògùn tàbí ẹrọ kankan.
Clinical_Translation_English: Obstructive sleep apnoea without treatment initiation one year post-diagnosis, creating ongoing cardiovascular and neurocognitive risk, requiring CPAP titration study and urgent sleep clinic follow-up.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any difficulty controlling your blood pressure despite taking three medications?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ń mu oògùn ìpàná ẹjẹ̀ mẹ́ta ṣùgbọ́n ẹjẹ̀ mi ṣì ga. Dọ́kítà ti ṣàfikún oògùn méjì ní ọdún méjì. Ó kò dára síwájú sí i.
Clinical_Translation_English: Resistant hypertension on three antihypertensive agents requiring investigation for secondary causes including primary aldosteronism, renal artery stenosis, phaeochromocytoma, and medication non-adherence assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any visible pulsation in your neck when you look in the mirror?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo rí nǹkan tó ń lu ní ẹ̀gbẹ́ ọrùn mi ọ̀tún nígbà tí mo bá wò inú dígí. O ń hàn kíáá. Mo ti rí i fún ọ̀sẹ̀ méjì.
Clinical_Translation_English: Visible carotid pulsation suggesting carotid artery aneurysm, carotid body tumour, or prominent carotid pulse from hypertension or aortic regurgitation requiring carotid ultrasound and cardiac assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any pain specifically behind your kneecap when climbing stairs or squatting?
Patient_Yoruba: Irora wà lẹ́yìn tó àgún-ẹsẹ̀ mi nígbà tí mo bá gùn ìtẹ̀lọ́ọ̀dọ̀ tàbí jókòó kúrú. O máa ń yẹ̀ nígbà tí mo bá ní ẹsẹ̀ títọ.
Clinical_Translation_English: Retropatellar pain on knee flexion activities consistent with patellofemoral syndrome requiring vastus medialis strengthening exercises, biomechanical assessment, and patellar taping.

Doctor_English: Have you ever had a blood test that showed your white blood cell count was very high or very low?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìdánwò ẹjẹ̀ kan fihàn pé àwọn ẹ̀dọ̀ ẹjẹ̀ funfun mi pọ̀ jù ní ọdún kan sẹ́yìn ṣùgbọ́n kò sí ẹni tó ṣe ìdánwò mọ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Unexplored leucocytosis without follow-up blood count potentially indicating infection, inflammatory disease, steroid effect, or haematological malignancy requiring immediate repeat full blood count with differential.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any difficulty breastfeeding because of pain in your nipples?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọmú mi méjèéjì ń dùn mi gan-an nígbà tí ọmọ mi bá ń mu. O ń jẹ mí kígbe. Mo ń gbìyànjú pẹ̀lú ìgbà gbogbo.
Clinical_Translation_English: Severe bilateral nipple pain on breastfeeding requiring assessment for poor latch, tongue-tie, Raynaud's phenomenon of the nipple, and candidal infection before concluding on aetiology.

Doctor_English: Have you had any black or tarry stools recently, and have you been taking iron tablets?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìgbẹ́ mi di dúdú àti rírọ̀ fún ọjọ́ méjì. Mo ń mu ìrọ̀bí irin fún ẹjẹ̀ mi. Mi ò mọ̀ bóyá o jẹ́ nítorí oògùn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Black tarry stools requiring differentiation between iron supplementation-related stool darkening and melaena from upper gastrointestinal haemorrhage, based on consistency, odour, and clinical context.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any difficulty opening your jaw wide, or does it lock in one position?
Patient_Yoruba: Ẹnu mi kò ṣí jájá mọ́. O ń dúró ní ìwọ̀n kan. Ní ìgbà-ìgbà ó dì patapata fún ìṣẹ́jú díẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Trismus with jaw locking requiring assessment for temporomandibular joint internal derangement, masseteric hypertrophy, peritonsillar abscess, tetanus, or oral malignancy.

Doctor_English: Have you been experiencing any tingling or weakness in both hands that is worse at night?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọwọ́ mi méjèéjì máa ń hu bíi àbẹ̀rẹ̀ ń gún wọn ní alẹ́ tó ń jí mi dìde. Mo máa ń gbọn wọn láti gba ìtura.
Clinical_Translation_English: Bilateral nocturnal hand paraesthesia relieved by shaking, the characteristic presentation of bilateral carpal tunnel syndrome requiring nerve conduction studies and consideration of surgical decompression.

Doctor_English: Have you been taking any pain medication that contains codeine, and how often?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ń mu oògùn irora tó ní codeine ní ọjọ́-ọjọ́ fún oṣù mẹ́fà. Mi ò lè dáwọ̀ rárá. Nígbà tí mo gbìyànjú, inú mi ń ṣòro.
Clinical_Translation_English: Daily codeine use for six months with physical dependence and inability to discontinue, indicating opioid dependence requiring structured tapering programme and pain specialist review for alternative analgesia.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any episodes of double vision that come and go?
Patient_Yoruba: Ní ìgbà-ìgbà, mo máa ń rí àwòrán méjì fún ohun kan. O ń wáyé àti lọ. O ti ń ṣẹlẹ̀ fún oṣù méjì.
Clinical_Translation_English: Intermittent binocular diplopia requiring assessment for microvascular third nerve palsy, myasthenia gravis, thyroid eye disease, or demyelinating disease.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any drooping of your eyelid that has come on recently?
Patient_Yoruba: Ojú àpèrè mi ọ̀tún ti bẹ̀rẹ̀ sí rọ̀ lẹ́yìn ọjọ́ mẹ́wàá. Mo ní láti gbé orí mi ga láti rí dáradára. Ko dùn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Unilateral ptosis of recent onset requiring urgent assessment for Horner's syndrome from apical lung tumour, third nerve palsy from posterior communicating artery aneurysm, or myasthenia gravis.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any pain specifically around your left shoulder and arm when exercising?
Patient_Yoruba: Irora ń wáyé ní èjìká mi òsì àti apá mi nígbà ìdárayá. Ko ṣẹlẹ̀ nígbà ìsinmi. O ti ń ṣẹlẹ̀ fún oṣù kan.
Clinical_Translation_English: Exertional left shoulder and arm pain without rest symptoms consistent with ischaemic referral from coronary artery disease, requiring urgent exercise ECG and cardiology assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any problems producing saliva, making it difficult to talk or swallow?
Patient_Yoruba: Ẹnu mi gbẹ tó jẹ́ kí ọ̀rọ̀ mi di àìgbọ́ àti gbígbé oúnjẹ ṣòro. O ń burú ní ìgbà oòrùn. O ti wà fún oṣù mẹ́fà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Xerostomia causing dysarthria and dysphagia over six months requiring investigation for Sjögren's syndrome, medication side effects, radiotherapy damage, and salivary gland pathology.

Doctor_English: Have you been having episodes of feeling your heart skip a beat followed by a hard thump?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọkàn mi máa ń dà bíi pé ó fo, lẹ́yìn náà ó máa ń lu pẹ̀lú ìmọ̀lára ìgbóná. O ń ṣẹlẹ̀ lẹ́ẹ̀mẹ́fà ní ọjọ́ kan.
Clinical_Translation_English: Ectopic beats with compensatory pause and post-extrasystolic potentiation thumping sensation requiring ECG and Holter monitor to characterise and assess for underlying structural heart disease.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any pain in the groin that radiates down to the inner thigh?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìdì mi ọ̀tún ń dùn tó ń rọ sínú ìhà inú ẹsẹ̀ mi ọ̀tún. O ti wà fún ọjọ́ méjì. Ó le nígbà tí mo bá rìn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Right groin pain radiating to the medial thigh along the obturator nerve distribution, consistent with obturator hernia, psoas abscess, or hip joint pathology requiring CT pelvis and hip assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any difficulty seeing the edges of things or noticing things on one side?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo máa ń kọlù àwọn ohun ní ẹ̀gbẹ́ òsì nígbà tí mo bá ń rìn. Ìhà òsì ń dà bíi tó ṣòòfà. O ti ń ṣẹlẹ̀ fún oṣù mẹ́fà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Left-sided homonymous hemianopia causing collisions with objects, indicating a right occipital or optic radiation lesion requiring urgent MRI brain to exclude stroke, tumour, or demyelination.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any pain that is worse when you breathe in deeply or press on your chest wall?
Patient_Yoruba: Irora àyà mi ń burú nígbà tí mo bá mí jìnnà tàbí nígbà tí a bá fi ọwọ́ tẹ ìhà àyà mi. Ko ṣẹlẹ̀ nígbà ìsinmi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Pleuritic and musculoskeletal chest pain from deep breathing and palpation consistent with costochondritis or pleuritis requiring anti-inflammatory therapy and investigation for pulmonary embolism.

Doctor_English: Have you been told your blood sugar goes very low at night while you are sleeping?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìdánwò suga mi fihàn pé ó máa ń lọ sísàlẹ̀ gan-an ní alẹ́. Mo ń jí pẹ̀lú ìgbọn àti gbó. O ti ń ṣẹlẹ̀ fún oṣù méjì.
Clinical_Translation_English: Nocturnal hypoglycaemia with autonomic symptoms on waking requiring insulin regimen review, reduction of basal insulin dose, and continuous glucose monitoring to prevent severe hypoglycaemia and hypoglycaemic unawareness.

Doctor_English: Have you been told you have protein in your urine, and have you been investigated for the cause?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà sọ pé ìtọ mi ní àmì ẹjẹ̀ nínú rẹ̀ ní ọdún kan sẹ́yìn. Ko ṣe ìdánwò mọ́. Mo ní àtọ̀gbẹ̀ fún ọdún mẹ́wàá.
Clinical_Translation_English: Persistent proteinuria in a ten-year diabetic without follow-up nephrological assessment, indicating diabetic nephropathy requiring ACE inhibitor initiation, tight glycaemic and blood pressure control, and annual urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio monitoring.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any difficulty sleeping because your legs feel restless at night?
Patient_Yoruba: Ẹsẹ̀ mi máa ń jẹ mí ìyọnu ní alẹ́, bíi ẹni pé wọ́n fẹ́ gúnjú. Gbígbé wọn ń ràn mí lọ́wọ́. O ti ń ṣẹlẹ̀ fún oṣù mẹ́fà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Restless legs syndrome with dysaesthetic nocturnal leg restlessness relieved by movement, requiring iron studies for iron deficiency as a secondary cause and dopamine agonist therapy.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any difficulty with your digestion, with food feeling like it is sitting in your stomach?
Patient_Yoruba: Oúnjẹ máa ń jẹ mí bíi pé ó dúró nínú ikùn mi fún wákàtí pípẹ́ lẹ́yìn jíjẹun. Mo ń ní ríru pẹ̀lú. O ti ń ṣẹlẹ̀ fún oṣù mẹ́fà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Gastroparesis symptoms with delayed gastric emptying sensation, nausea, and early satiety over six months, a common complication of diabetes, requiring gastric emptying scintigraphy and prokinetic therapy.

Doctor_English: Have you been experiencing any hot flushes or sweating episodes at predictable times of day?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìgbóná máa ń wáyé ní ìgbà-ìgbà ní ọ̀sán àti alẹ́ fún oṣù mẹ́fà. Mo gbó lọ́pọ̀lọpọ̀ àti ọkàn mi ń lu yára ní àkókò yẹn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Predictable diurnal hot flushes with diaphoresis and palpitations consistent with perimenopausal vasomotor symptoms or, if male, carcinoid syndrome, requiring hormonal assessment and twenty-four hour urinary 5-HIAA if male.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any unusual sensitivity to medications, with small doses causing big reactions?
Patient_Yoruba: Oògùn tó ń ṣiṣẹ́ fún àwọn ẹlòmíràn ní ìwọ̀n nla máa ń ṣe mí ìyọnu ní iwọ̀n kéékèéké. O ti ṣẹlẹ̀ pẹ̀lú oògùn mẹ́ta tó yàtọ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Unusual drug sensitivity across multiple drug classes suggesting pharmacogenomic variation, hepatic enzyme deficiency, or an underlying autoimmune condition affecting drug metabolism, requiring pharmacogenomic testing.

Doctor_English: Have you been told your vitamin B12 is low, and are you vegetarian or vegan?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìdánwò mi fihàn pé B12 mi kéré ní ọdún kan sẹ́yìn. Mo jẹ oúnjẹ tó ń jẹ ewé àti ẹyin nìkan fún ọdún mẹ́fà. Kò sí ẹni tó fún mi ní oògùn.
Clinical_Translation_English: B12 deficiency in a lacto-ovo vegetarian without supplementation or injection therapy, creating risk of subacute combined degeneration of the spinal cord, requiring intramuscular B12 replacement and dietary counselling.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any episodes of sudden severe vertigo when you first wake up in the morning?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo máa ń jí ní àárọ̀ pẹ̀lú orí yípo tó le gan-an. O ń pẹ́ fún ìṣẹ́jú mẹ́wàá lẹ́yìn náà ó máa ń yẹ̀. O ti ń ṣẹlẹ̀ fún ọ̀sẹ̀ méjì.
Clinical_Translation_English: Morning positional vertigo on first waking consistent with BPPV from posterior semicircular canal canalithiasis, confirming with Dix-Hallpike test and treating with the Epley repositioning manoeuvre.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any pain in your temples or jaw when chewing that is associated with headache?
Patient_Yoruba: Ẹrẹ̀kẹ́ mi àti apá ìhà orí mi ń dùn nígbà jíjẹun. Orí mi tún ń fọ̀ lẹ́yìn oúnjẹ. O ti ń ṣẹlẹ̀ fún oṣù méjì.
Clinical_Translation_English: Jaw claudication with temporal headache on chewing, the pathognomonic symptom of giant cell arteritis requiring immediate high-dose prednisolone to prevent ischaemic stroke and permanent visual loss.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any loss of your peripheral vision, like wearing blinkers or tunnel vision?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìhà ìmọ̀lẹ̀ ojú mi ń dínkù. Mo ń rí dáradára sí iwájú ṣùgbọ́n àwọn nǹkan ní ẹ̀gbẹ́ ti ń sọ̀nù. O ti ń burú sí i fún ọdún méjì.
Clinical_Translation_English: Progressive concentric visual field constriction over two years consistent with advanced glaucoma or retinitis pigmentosa, requiring urgent Humphrey visual field testing, IOP measurement, and optic disc assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any pain in your shoulder that is worse when you reach behind your back?
Patient_Yoruba: Èjìká mi ọ̀tún ń dùn nígbà tí mo bá gbìyànjú láti fi ọwọ́ kan ẹ̀hin mi. Mo kò lè de ìgbádìí bàtà mi. O ti ń ṣẹlẹ̀ fún oṣù mẹ́fà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Restricted internal rotation with posterior shoulder pain consistent with adhesive capsulitis, posterior rotator cuff tear, or glenohumeral joint pathology requiring shoulder MRI and physiotherapy assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you been experiencing any persistent ringing in your ears after attending a loud concert or using headphones?
Patient_Yoruba: Etí mi méjèéjì ti ń rò lẹ́yìn iṣẹ̀lẹ̀ ìró àárọ̀ ní ọ̀sẹ̀ kan sẹ́yìn. Ko tíì lọ. Mo ń lo àwọn headphones ní ìgbà-ìgbà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Persistent post-acoustic tinnitus following noise exposure consistent with noise-induced cochlear damage requiring audiometry and counselling on hearing protection and avoidance of further noise exposure.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any abdominal pain that is relieved by passing wind or opening your bowels?
Patient_Yoruba: Irora ikùn mi máa ń yẹ̀ lẹ́yìn tí afẹ́fẹ́ bá jáde tàbí lẹ́yìn ìgbẹ́. O máa ń wáyé ní gbogbo ọjọ́ fún oṣù mẹ́fà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Abdominal pain relieved by defecation and flatus consistent with irritable bowel syndrome requiring Rome IV diagnostic criteria assessment, dietary modification with low-FODMAP diet trial, and exclusion of inflammatory bowel disease.

Doctor_English: Have you been told you have asthma, and does your peak flow reading change significantly throughout the day?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ní àrùn ẹ̀gbẹ̀. Ìdánwò ẹ̀gbẹ̀ mi yàtọ̀ ní ọjọ́ kan, díẹ̀ sí i ní owúrọ̀ àti dára ní ọ̀sán. O ti ń ṣẹlẹ̀ ní gbogbo ọjọ́.
